You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_64.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 64: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[64]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_64.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 64: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Consolidation-NLI winner (I-wire-001 W1, #1306) — Bidirectional-NLI grouping.
2	
3	Bake-off section winner for consolidation/baskets (`state/section_winner_board.md`):
4	**Bidirectional-NLI (nli-deberta-v3-base)** — R=1.0/P=1.0/f1=1.0, where the literal
5	SHA-1/signature floor merges nothing (R=0.0). The literal `finding_dedup._finding_key`
6	floor only clusters rows whose extracted subject/predicate/value/unit match EXACTLY, so
7	two sources that PARAPHRASE the SAME claim with different surface forms (or that carry no
8	extractable numeric finding) get DISTINCT keys and never corroborate. This module detects
9	those same-claim paraphrases with a bidirectional NLI cross-encoder and UNIONS their
10	literal clusters into one basket, so the corroboration (count + distinct hosts) RISES.
11	
12	DNA (CLAUDE.md §-1.3): this is a CONSOLIDATION / WEIGHT, never a faithfulness relaxation.
13	It only ever MERGES literal clusters into larger baskets (corroboration goes UP, member
14	hosts go UP); it never drops a row, never changes strict_verify / the NLI entailment
15	verifier / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding (those modules are FROZEN and not
16	imported here). A merge can only KEEP more corroborators together — it can never relax a
17	verify verdict.
18	
19	FAITHFULNESS-SAFE MERGE PREDICATE (bidirectional): two cluster representatives merge ONLY
20	when entailment is the argmax of the cross-encoder logits in BOTH directions (A entails B
21	AND B entails A) above a score margin. Requiring BOTH directions makes the predicate
22	symmetric and gives free polarity safety — an antonym pair ("raised" vs "lowered") scores
23	CONTRADICTION (verified in the model smoke test), so it can never falsely corroborate.
24	
25	RUNTIME PARALLELISM (operator-locked, wired bounded-parallel from the start): the pairwise
26	NLI scores are computed bounded-parallel via a `ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=N)` where N
27	= `PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_WORKERS` (default 8). Grouping is a DETERMINISTIC order-independent
28	union-find post-step over the GATHERED score matrix — concurrency can never change the
29	resulting baskets (gather-then-sort by cluster index).
30	
31	Pure grouping: constructs no faithfulness gate, no network LLM. The only heavy dependency
32	(the cross-encoder) is imported and loaded LAZILY inside `score_pairs`, so importing this
33	module is cheap and the legacy path that never calls it pays nothing.
34	"""
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	import logging
38	import math
39	import os
40	import re
41	import threading
42	import time
43	from concurrent.futures import (
44	    FIRST_COMPLETED,
45	    ThreadPoolExecutor,
46	    wait as futures_wait,
47	)
48	from dataclasses import dataclass
49	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
50	
51	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.consolidation_nli")
52	
53	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
54	# Env knobs (LAW VI — zero hard-coding; all tunables are env-overridable)
55	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
56	ENV_FLAG = "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI"                       # default-OFF master gate
57	ENV_MODEL = "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_MODEL"                # cross-encoder id
58	ENV_WORKERS = "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_WORKERS"            # bounded-parallel cap
59	ENV_MARGIN = "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_MARGIN"              # entailment-logit margin
60	ENV_MAX_PAIRS = "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_MAX_PAIRS"        # O(n^2) safety cap
61	# I-deepfix-001 W04-consolidation-nli-wall (#1344): a TOTAL wall-clock deadline for the
62	# whole score_pairs scoring loop. The bounded-parallel pool.map blocks until EVERY chunk
63	# finishes — on a slow/contended/CPU-degraded cross-encoder (the post-OOM CPU re-score
64	# pins the rest of the run), this had NO time bound (the MAX_PAIRS cap bounds COUNT, not
65	# wall-clock), and the prose path runs it ONCE PER SECTION. When the deadline passes we
66	# STOP collecting further edges and return the edges gathered so far. §-1.3: a partial
67	# edge set only UNDER-merges => keeps MORE/equal baskets, never drops a corroborator.
68	# Consolidation is a WEIGHT, not a faithfulness gate. Default generous; <= 0 disables.
69	ENV_WALL_SECONDS = "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_WALL_SECONDS"
70	# I-deepfix-001 fix-3 (#1344): optional device placement for the cross-encoder. On
71	# the crammed 2-GPU split (W6 embedder + W5 reranker + W10 NLI co-resident on cuda:0)
72	# a CUDA OOM during load/predict used to RAISE and KILL the consolidation step (a
73	# §-1.3 WEIGHT, not a faithfulness gate). Unset => NO device kwarg (byte-identical
74	# library auto-placement). A CUDA OOM degrades to CPU (keeps MORE baskets, never dies).
75	ENV_DEVICE = "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_DEVICE"
76	# I-deepfix-001 (#1344): cap the index-pairs scored in ONE cross-encoder `.predict`
77	# forward, INDEPENDENT of the worker count. The old chunk_size = ceil(pairs/min(workers,
78	# pairs)) grew UNBOUNDED with corpus size (~2500 pairs -> a ~5000-tuple forward), which
79	# OOM'd the crammed card on the 890+-source clinical corpora (the CUBLAS_STATUS_ALLOC_FAILED
80	# crash). Bounding the forward keeps peak GPU memory constant regardless of corpus size.
81	ENV_PREDICT_CHUNK = "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_PREDICT_CHUNK"
82	# I-deepfix-001 W04-prebucket (#1369): over-MAX_PAIRS PRE-BUCKETING. The drb_72 live run
83	# fed 311- and 351-text buckets into `score_pairs` (48,205 / 61,425 candidate pairs >
84	# the 20,000 cap), so the whole bucket's NLI consolidation was SKIPPED — near-duplicate
85	# findings stayed UNMERGED and rendered as REPEATED separate findings. A blind cap raise
86	# would re-open the O(n^2) compute/memory blowup the cap guards. Instead, when this flag
87	# is ON, an over-cap bucket is SUB-DIVIDED into lexical-overlap sub-buckets whose
88	# per-sub-bucket pair count stays <= the cap, and the NLI consolidation RUNS within each
89	# sub-bucket. §-1.3 CONSOLIDATE-keep-all: near-dups that share a sub-bucket MERGE
90	# (citations kept on the survivor basket by the callers' keep-all member union); a
91	# cross-sub-bucket near-dup pair is simply never scored => stays UNMERGED, which is a
92	# KEEP — worst case equals the current skip behavior, never worse; NOTHING is dropped.
93	# DEFAULT-OFF => the over-cap skip runs byte-identical (this is a build/validation
94	# scaffold flag per the activate+archive discipline; the run slates flip it ON).
95	ENV_SUBBUCKET = "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_SUBBUCKET"
96	# Content-word Jaccard threshold for routing a text into an existing sub-bucket (the
97	# similarity key that keeps near-duplicates together). LAW VI env-tunable.
98	ENV_SUBBUCKET_JACCARD = "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_SUBBUCKET_JACCARD"
99	
100	_DEFAULT_MODEL = "cross-encoder/nli-deberta-v3-base"
101	_DEFAULT_WORKERS = "8"
102	_DEFAULT_MARGIN = "0.0"      # entailment must be the argmax (logit > the other two)
103	_DEFAULT_MAX_PAIRS = "20000"
104	# I-deepfix-001 C2 (#1344): raised 90 -> 180. The prose path runs score_pairs ONCE PER
105	# SECTION; on a large corpus (890+ sources) with a CPU-degraded cross-encoder the 90s wall
106	# STARVED the consolidation — it truncated mid-scoring and UNDER-merged, dropping real
107	# corroboration weight (a §-1.3 loss of keep-all corroborators from the basket). A more
108	# generous default lets a large section's paraphrase clusters fully union before the wall.
109	# Still a WEIGHT, not a faithfulness gate; still fully env-overridable (LAW VI); <=0 disables.
110	_DEFAULT_WALL_SECONDS = "180"   # I-deepfix-001 C2: per-call score_pairs total wall (s)
111	_DEFAULT_PREDICT_CHUNK = "256"  # I-deepfix-001 #1344: max index-pairs per `.predict` forward
112	_DEFAULT_SUBBUCKET_JACCARD = "0.30"  # I-deepfix-001 W04-prebucket (#1369): routing threshold
113	_CPU_DEVICE = "cpu"         # the OOM-degrade target
114	
115	# nli-deberta-v3-base label order (verified from model.config.id2label in the smoke
116	# test): index 0 = contradiction, 1 = entailment, 2 = neutral. These are the
117	# FALLBACK indices when a model exposes no id2label; the real indices are RESOLVED
118	# from the loaded model's config by ``_resolve_label_indices`` (I-deepfix-001 L4).
119	_DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_IDX = 1
120	_DEFAULT_CONTRADICTION_IDX = 0
121	_ENTAILMENT_IDX = _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_IDX
122	_CONTRADICTION_IDX = _DEFAULT_CONTRADICTION_IDX
123	
124	# I-deepfix-001 L4 (#1344): MULTILINGUAL NLI.
125	#
126	# The consolidation entailment leg is a WEIGHT (it only merges paraphrase baskets;
127	# it never drops a source or touches the faithfulness gate), so the model is fully
128	# env-overridable via ``PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_MODEL`` (LAW VI). The English default
129	# above under-entails a cross-lingual paraphrase — a native-language claim and its
130	# supporting span in another language would NOT be recognised as corroborating, so
131	# their baskets stay split and the corroboration weight is lost.
132	#
133	# To retrieve/consolidate in the task language, point ``PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_MODEL``
134	# at a MULTILINGUAL cross-encoder — recommended:
135	#     MoritzLaurer/mDeBERTa-v3-base-xnli-multilingual-nli-2mil7
136	# whose 3-way label order differs from the English default. The old code hardcoded
137	# index 0=contradiction / 1=entailment, so swapping the model would have SILENTLY
138	# mis-read the logits (a correctness bug). ``_resolve_label_indices`` now reads the
139	# entailment / contradiction indices from the LOADED model's ``config.id2label`` so
140	# ANY 3-way NLI model plugs in correctly. Engine-neutral for basket weighting; it
141	# only makes the multilingual model usable, never relaxes a gate.
142	
143	
144	def _resolve_label_indices(model: Any) -> None:
145	    """Set ``_ENTAILMENT_IDX`` / ``_CONTRADICTION_IDX`` from ``model``'s label map.
146	
147	    Reads a 3-way ``id2label`` (via ``model.config`` or ``model.model.config``) and
148	    finds the index whose label contains "entail" and the one containing
149	    "contradict". Only overrides the module defaults when BOTH are found, distinct,
150	    and the map is exactly 3-way; otherwise the defaults stand (fail-safe: an
151	    unreadable config keeps the verified nli-deberta order). Idempotent — safe to
152	    call on every load."""
153	    global _ENTAILMENT_IDX, _CONTRADICTION_IDX
154	    try:
155	        config = getattr(model, "config", None) or getattr(
156	            getattr(model, "model", None), "config", None
157	        )
158	        id2label = getattr(config, "id2label", None)
159	        if not id2label:
160	            return
161	        norm = {int(k): str(v).strip().lower() for k, v in id2label.items()}
162	        if len(norm) != 3:
163	            return
164	        ent = next((i for i, lab in norm.items() if "entail" in lab), None)
165	        con = next((i for i, lab in norm.items() if "contradict" in lab), None)
166	        if ent is None or con is None or ent == con:
167	            return
168	        _ENTAILMENT_IDX = ent
169	        _CONTRADICTION_IDX = con
170	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a bad config never breaks scoring; keep defaults
171	        return
172	
173	
174	def consolidation_nli_enabled() -> bool:
175	    """`PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI` master gate. DEFAULT-OFF => the caller's legacy literal
176	    floor runs byte-identical (this module is never imported on that path). ON => the
177	    bidirectional-NLI consolidation runs as a post-step over the literal clusters."""
178	    return os.getenv(ENV_FLAG, "0").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
179	
180	
181	def _read_int(env: str, default: str, *, lo: int, hi: int) -> int:
182	    raw = os.environ.get(env, "").strip() or default
183	    try:
184	        value = int(raw)
185	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
186	        logger.warning("[consolidation_nli] %s=%r not an int; using %s", env, raw, default)
187	        return int(default)
188	    return max(lo, min(hi, value))
189	
190	
191	def _read_float(env: str, default: str) -> float:
192	    raw = os.environ.get(env, "").strip() or default
193	    try:
194	        return float(raw)
195	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
196	        logger.warning("[consolidation_nli] %s=%r not a float; using %s", env, raw, default)
197	        return float(default)
198	
199	
200	def _workers() -> int:
201	    return _read_int(ENV_WORKERS, _DEFAULT_WORKERS, lo=1, hi=64)
202	
203	
204	def _margin() -> float:
205	    return _read_float(ENV_MARGIN, _DEFAULT_MARGIN)
206	
207	
208	def _max_pairs() -> int:
209	    return _read_int(ENV_MAX_PAIRS, _DEFAULT_MAX_PAIRS, lo=1, hi=10_000_000)
210	
211	
212	def _predict_chunk() -> int:
213	    """I-deepfix-001 (#1344): the max index-pairs scored in ONE cross-encoder `.predict`
214	    forward. Caps peak GPU memory INDEPENDENT of corpus size (the old chunk grew unbounded
215	    and OOM'd the clinical corpora). The grouping is an order-independent union-find over
216	    the gathered edges, so smaller forwards are byte-identical to the verdict (only
217	    WHERE-in-a-batch a pair sits, never WHICH pairs are compared or the entailment margin).
218	    ``<= 0`` disables the cap (unbounded, the byte-identical legacy behaviour). Default 256."""
219	    return _read_int(ENV_PREDICT_CHUNK, _DEFAULT_PREDICT_CHUNK, lo=0, hi=1_000_000)
220	
221	
222	def _wall_seconds() -> float:
223	    """I-deepfix-001 W04 (#1344): the total score_pairs wall in seconds. ``<= 0`` (or a
224	    non-finite value) disables the wall => the loop blocks unbounded exactly as before
225	    (the escape hatch). Default 180s (C2 raised it from 90 to stop starving large
226	    sections mid-scoring)."""
227	    raw = os.environ.get(ENV_WALL_SECONDS, "").strip() or _DEFAULT_WALL_SECONDS
228	    try:
229	        value = float(raw)
230	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
231	        logger.warning(
232	            "[consolidation_nli] %s=%r not a float; using %s",
233	            ENV_WALL_SECONDS, raw, _DEFAULT_WALL_SECONDS,
234	        )
235	        return float(_DEFAULT_WALL_SECONDS)
236	    import math as _math
237	    if not _math.isfinite(value) or value <= 0:
238	        return 0.0
239	    return value
240	
241	
242	def _device() -> Optional[str]:
243	    """Configured cross-encoder device (``PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_DEVICE``), or None when
244	    unset/blank => NO device kwarg passed (byte-identical library auto-placement)."""
245	    raw = os.environ.get(ENV_DEVICE, "").strip()
246	    return raw or None
247	
248	
249	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
250	# Over-cap PRE-BUCKETING — I-deepfix-001 W04-prebucket (#1369)
251	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
252	_SUBBUCKET_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z0-9]+")
253	# Minimal English glue words excluded from the routing signature. Words shorter than
254	# 3 chars ("a"/"is"/"of"/"to"/"in"...) are already dropped by the length floor below.
255	# Over-inclusion here only splits sub-buckets finer (an under-merge, §-1.3-safe);
256	# under-inclusion only wastes a few bounded NLI pairs — neither direction is unsafe.
257	_SUBBUCKET_STOPWORDS = frozenset(
258	    "the and for with that this from are was were has have had its their them they "
259	    "not but into over under between among also been being can may will would could "
260	    "should than then when where which while who whom whose what how why all any "
261	    "each more most other some such only own same very just both few nor too".split()
262	)
263	
264	
265	def _subbucket_enabled() -> bool:
266	    """``PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_SUBBUCKET`` gate for the over-cap pre-bucketing path.
267	    DEFAULT-OFF => the over-cap SKIP runs byte-identical (whole bucket passes through
268	    unmerged, exactly the current behavior)."""
269	    return os.getenv(ENV_SUBBUCKET, "0").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
270	
271	
272	def _subbucket_jaccard() -> float:
273	    """Content-word Jaccard threshold for routing a text into an existing sub-bucket
274	    (default 0.30). Out-of-range / malformed => default (logged, never raised — a typo
275	    must not crash a paid run)."""
276	    value = _read_float(ENV_SUBBUCKET_JACCARD, _DEFAULT_SUBBUCKET_JACCARD)
277	    if not (0.0 < value <= 1.0):
278	        logger.warning(
279	            "[consolidation_nli] %s=%s out of (0,1]; using default %s",
280	            ENV_SUBBUCKET_JACCARD, value, _DEFAULT_SUBBUCKET_JACCARD,
281	        )
282	        return float(_DEFAULT_SUBBUCKET_JACCARD)
283	    return value
284	
285	
286	def _subbucket_tokens(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
287	    """Deterministic content-word routing signature: lowercased alnum words of >= 3
288	    chars minus the glue-word set. Self-contained on purpose — consolidation_nli must
289	    stay dependency-light because BOTH dedup modules lazily import THIS module (a
290	    fact_dedup import here would risk an import cycle)."""
291	    words = _SUBBUCKET_WORD_RE.findall((text or "").lower())
292	    return frozenset(w for w in words if len(w) >= 3 and w not in _SUBBUCKET_STOPWORDS)
293	
294	
295	def _max_subbucket_size(max_pairs: int) -> int:
296	    """Largest ``k`` with ``k*(k-1)/2 <= max_pairs`` (floored at 2 so one pair can
297	    always be scored). This is the per-sub-bucket SIZE cap that keeps every
298	    sub-bucket's pairwise count under the O(n^2) guard."""
299	    k = int((1.0 + math.sqrt(1.0 + 8.0 * float(max_pairs))) / 2.0)
300	    while k > 2 and (k * (k - 1)) // 2 > max_pairs:
301	        k -= 1  # float-precision guard: never let rounding exceed the cap
302	    return max(2, k)
303	
304	
305	def _pre_bucket_indices(texts: list[str], max_pairs: int) -> list[list[int]]:
306	    """Partition ``range(len(texts))`` into similarity-keyed SUB-BUCKETS, each small
307	    enough that its pairwise count stays <= ``max_pairs``.
308	
309	    Greedy single-pass over the input order (deterministic, order-stable): each text
310	    joins the best-Jaccard existing sub-bucket (vs. the sub-bucket's FIRST member's
311	    token signature) that is (a) below the size cap and (b) at/above the routing
312	    threshold; ties keep the earliest bucket; otherwise it OPENS a new sub-bucket.
313	    Near-duplicate findings share most content words, so they route together; two
314	    texts with no token overlap (or empty signatures) never share a sub-bucket.
315	
316	    COMPLETE + DISJOINT: every index lands in exactly one sub-bucket — nothing is
317	    dropped. A near-dup pair split across sub-buckets is simply never NLI-scored
318	    (stays unmerged = KEEP, the same outcome the over-cap skip gave EVERY pair).
319	    Complexity O(n * buckets) set ops — trivial at the few-hundred-text scale that
320	    overflows the pair cap (311/351 in the drb_72 evidence)."""
321	    size_cap = _max_subbucket_size(max_pairs)
322	    threshold = _subbucket_jaccard()
323	    signatures = [_subbucket_tokens(t) for t in texts]
324	    buckets: list[list[int]] = []
325	    representatives: list[frozenset[str]] = []
326	    for i in range(len(texts)):
327	        sig = signatures[i]
328	        best = -1
329	        best_score = 0.0
330	        if sig:
331	            for b, rep in enumerate(representatives):
332	                if len(buckets[b]) >= size_cap or not rep:
333	                    continue
334	                overlap = len(sig & rep)
335	                if not overlap:
336	                    continue
337	                score = overlap / float(len(sig | rep))
338	                if score >= threshold and score > best_score:
339	                    best, best_score = b, score
340	        if best >= 0:
341	            buckets[best].append(i)
342	        else:
343	            buckets.append([i])
344	            representatives.append(sig)
345	    return buckets
346	
347	
348	def _is_cuda_oom(exc: BaseException) -> bool:
349	    """True iff ``exc`` is a CUDA out-of-memory / allocation failure (the only failure the
350	    W10 winner DEGRADES to CPU; every other error fails loud per §-1.4). Detects the typed
351	    ``torch.cuda.OutOfMemoryError``, the generic ``RuntimeError('CUDA out of memory ...')``,
352	    AND the cuBLAS allocation failures ``CUBLAS_STATUS_ALLOC_FAILED`` /
353	    ``CUBLAS_STATUS_NOT_INITIALIZED`` — by class name + message, so no hard torch import.
354	
355	    I-deepfix-001 (#1344): the cuBLAS branch is the crash fix. When the card is full the
356	    SAME out-of-memory condition surfaces from a cuBLAS handle allocation as
357	    ``CUDA error: CUBLAS_STATUS_ALLOC_FAILED`` (or ``..._NOT_INITIALIZED`` when a prior
358	    alloc poisoned the handle) with NO 'out of memory' substring. The old matcher missed
359	    it, so the CPU degrade never fired and the run DIED at the consolidation step on the
360	    large clinical corpora. cuBLAS-alloc is OOM-equivalent — route it to the SAME
361	    already-tested identical-result CPU degrade."""
362	    name = type(exc).__name__
363	    if name in ("OutOfMemoryError", "CudaOutOfMemoryError"):
364	        return True
365	    msg = str(exc).lower()
366	    if "out of memory" in msg and ("cuda" in msg or "gpu" in msg):
367	        return True
368	    return "cublas_status_alloc_failed" in msg or "cublas_status_not_initialized" in msg
369	
370	
371	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
372	# Lazy cross-encoder load (one model per process, thread-safe)
373	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
374	_MODEL_LOCK = threading.Lock()
375	_MODEL: Any = None
376	# I-deepfix-001 fix-3 (#1344): the device the resident `_MODEL` was loaded on (or None
377	# for library auto-placement). When a predict-time CUDA OOM forces a CPU degrade, the
378	# model is rebuilt on CPU and this is set to "cpu" so the rebuild happens once.
379	_MODEL_DEVICE: Optional[str] = None
380	
381	
382	def _construct_cross_encoder(model_id: str, device: Optional[str]) -> Any:
383	    """Build a `CrossEncoder` with an OPTIONAL device kwarg (lazy import). A None
384	    device passes NO kwarg (byte-identical library auto-placement)."""
385	    from sentence_transformers import CrossEncoder  # noqa: PLC0415 — lazy by design
386	
387	    if device is None:
388	        return CrossEncoder(model_id)
389	    return CrossEncoder(model_id, device=device)
390	
391	
392	def _load_model(device: Optional[str] = None) -> Any:
393	    """Lazily load the cross-encoder ONCE per process (double-checked lock). Loading
394	    happens only inside the flag-ON branch (the caller already gated on the flag), so an
395	    environment without `sentence_transformers` only fails when the winner is actually
396	    activated — never on import.
397	
398	    I-deepfix-001 fix-3 (#1344): honors ``PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_DEVICE`` (or the explicit
399	    ``device`` arg, used by the predict-OOM CPU degrade). On a CUDA OOM during the GPU
400	    load the model is RETRIED on CPU (degrade) so the consolidation winner still FIRES —
401	    it never raises an OOM and never loses a basket. A non-OOM load error still fails
402	    loud (§-1.4). The model is cached with its loaded device in ``_MODEL_DEVICE``."""
403	    global _MODEL, _MODEL_DEVICE
404	    if _MODEL is not None:
405	        return _MODEL
406	    with _MODEL_LOCK:
407	        if _MODEL is not None:
408	            return _MODEL
409	        model_id = os.environ.get(ENV_MODEL, "").strip() or _DEFAULT_MODEL
410	        want_device = _device() if device is None else device
411	        logger.info(
412	            "[consolidation_nli] loading cross-encoder %s (device=%s)",
413	            model_id, want_device or "auto",
414	        )
415	        try:
416	            _MODEL = _construct_cross_encoder(model_id, want_device)
417	            _MODEL_DEVICE = want_device
418	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — classify: only a CUDA OOM degrades
419	            if not _is_cuda_oom(exc) or want_device == _CPU_DEVICE:
420	                raise  # non-OOM (or already-CPU) load failure => fail loud (§-1.4)
421	            logger.warning(
422	                "[consolidation_nli] CUDA OOM loading the cross-encoder on device=%s; "
423	                "DEGRADING to CPU so consolidation still fires (no basket lost): %s",
424	                want_device, exc,
425	            )
426	            _MODEL = _construct_cross_encoder(model_id, _CPU_DEVICE)
427	            _MODEL_DEVICE = _CPU_DEVICE
428	        # I-deepfix-001 L4 (#1344): resolve entailment/contradiction indices from the
429	        # loaded model so a MULTILINGUAL cross-encoder with a different label order is
430	        # read correctly (never mis-mapped to the English default).
431	        _resolve_label_indices(_MODEL)
432	        return _MODEL
433	
434	
435	def _reload_model_on_cpu() -> Any:
436	    """Drop the resident (GPU) cross-encoder and rebuild it on CPU — the predict-time
437	    CUDA OOM degrade. Returns the CPU model. Thread-safe (under ``_MODEL_LOCK``)."""
438	    global _MODEL, _MODEL_DEVICE
439	    with _MODEL_LOCK:
440	        if _MODEL is not None and _MODEL_DEVICE == _CPU_DEVICE:
441	            return _MODEL  # another thread already degraded
442	        model_id = os.environ.get(ENV_MODEL, "").strip() or _DEFAULT_MODEL
443	        logger.warning(
444	            "[consolidation_nli] CUDA OOM during predict; DEGRADING the cross-encoder "
445	            "to CPU and re-scoring (no basket lost)."
446	        )
447	        _MODEL = _construct_cross_encoder(model_id, _CPU_DEVICE)
448	        _MODEL_DEVICE = _CPU_DEVICE
449	        _resolve_label_indices(_MODEL)  # I-deepfix-001 L4 (#1344)
450	        return _MODEL
451	
452	
453	def _entails(logits: Any, margin: float) -> bool:
454	    """True iff entailment is the strict argmax of the 3-way logits by at least `margin`.
455	
456	    Pure index comparison on the model's [contradiction, entailment, neutral] logits — no
457	    softmax needed (argmax is monotone under softmax). Requiring entailment > BOTH others
458	    (by `margin`) means an antonym pair (argmax=contradiction) can never pass."""
459	    ent = float(logits[_ENTAILMENT_IDX])
460	    con = float(logits[_CONTRADICTION_IDX])
461	    neu = float(logits[3 - _ENTAILMENT_IDX - _CONTRADICTION_IDX])  # the remaining (neutral) idx
462	    return ent > con + margin and ent > neu + margin
463	
464	
465	def entails_directional(
466	    premise: str,
467	    hypothesis: str,
468	    *,
469	    margin: Optional[float] = None,
470	    predict_fn: Optional[Callable[[list[tuple[str, str]]], Any]] = None,
471	) -> Optional[bool]:
472	    """DIRECTIONAL single-pair entailment: does ``premise`` entail ``hypothesis``?
473	
474	    I-deepfix-001 P2 citation-purity (#1344): the ALCE / DeepTRACE citation direction —
475	    ``premise`` = the corroborator's claim-local cited span, ``hypothesis`` = the rendered
476	    claim (or one of its claim-local clauses). This is the ONE-directional counterpart to the
477	    bidirectional ``score_pairs`` used for basket consolidation: a citation is a
478	    span->claim SUPPORT relation (the span must entail the claim), which is asymmetric, so we
479	    read ONLY the forward logits — NOT both directions.
480	
481	    Returns three states so the caller can distinguish a real NON-entailment from an
482	    infra fault:
483	      * ``True``  — ``premise`` entails ``hypothesis`` (entailment is the strict argmax by
484	        ``margin``, the SAME threshold ``_entails`` applies in consolidation);
485	      * ``False`` — a CONFIDENT non-entailment (the detach signal — the cited span does not
486	        carry this claim);
487	      * ``None``  — the verdict is UNAVAILABLE (empty text, or the cross-encoder could not be
488	        loaded / scored for a NON-OOM reason). None is the DEGRADE sentinel: the caller keeps
489	        the citation on its already-passed lexical/numeric grounding rather than detaching on
490	        a model outage (never fights §-1.3 breadth on infra failure).
491	
492	    Reuses ``_load_model`` + ``_entails`` on ONE forward pair, with the SAME CUDA-OOM -> CPU
493	    degrade as ``score_pairs`` (an OOM re-scores on CPU, never dies). ``predict_fn`` is the
494	    deterministic test-injection seam (no GPU / model download); production passes None => the
495	    lazy cross-encoder. No new model is loaded — it is the SAME resident cross-encoder the
496	    consolidation leg already loads, so P2 adds ZERO OpenRouter / GPU spend.
497	    """
498	    if not premise or not premise.strip() or not hypothesis or not hypothesis.strip():
499	        return None
500	    margin = _margin() if margin is None else margin
501	    _injected = predict_fn is not None
502	    if predict_fn is None:
503	        try:
504	            model = _load_model()
505	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — infra fault => UNKNOWN (caller keeps)
506	            logger.warning(
507	                "[consolidation_nli] entails_directional: cross-encoder unavailable (%s); "
508	                "returning None (caller keeps the citation on lexical/numeric grounding).",
509	                exc,
510	            )
511	            return None
512	        predict_fn = model.predict  # type: ignore[assignment]
513	    batch: list[tuple[str, str]] = [(premise, hypothesis)]
514	    try:
515	        logits = predict_fn(batch)
516	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — only a CUDA OOM degrades; else UNKNOWN
517	        if not _is_cuda_oom(exc) or _injected:
518	            logger.warning(
519	                "[consolidation_nli] entails_directional: predict failed (%s); returning "
520	                "None (caller keeps the citation).", exc,
521	            )
522	            return None
523	        try:
524	            cpu_model = _reload_model_on_cpu()
525	            logits = cpu_model.predict(batch)
526	        except Exception as exc2:  # noqa: BLE001 — CPU degrade also failed => UNKNOWN
527	            logger.warning(
528	                "[consolidation_nli] entails_directional: CPU re-score failed (%s); "
529	                "returning None.", exc2,
530	            )
531	            return None
532	    return _entails(logits[0], margin)
533	
534	
535	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
536	# Union-find (deterministic, order-independent post-step)
537	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
538	@dataclass
539	class _UnionFind:
540	    """Tiny union-find over [0, n). Merges to the LOWEST index so the assignment is
541	    fully deterministic regardless of the order edges arrive (order-independence)."""
542	
543	    parent: list[int]
544	
545	    @classmethod
546	    def of(cls, n: int) -> "_UnionFind":
547	        return cls(parent=list(range(n)))
548	
549	    def find(self, x: int) -> int:
550	        root = x
551	        while self.parent[root] != root:
552	            root = self.parent[root]
553	        while self.parent[x] != root:  # path-compression
554	            self.parent[x], x = root, self.parent[x]
555	        return root
556	
557	    def union(self, a: int, b: int) -> None:
558	        ra, rb = self.find(a), self.find(b)
559	        if ra == rb:
560	            return
561	        lo, hi = (ra, rb) if ra < rb else (rb, ra)
562	        self.parent[hi] = lo  # always attach to the lower root => deterministic
563	
564	
565	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
566	# Pairwise scoring (BOUNDED-parallel) + grouping (deterministic post-step)
567	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
568	def score_pairs(
569	    texts: list[str],
570	    *,
571	    margin: Optional[float] = None,
572	    workers: Optional[int] = None,
573	    max_pairs: Optional[int] = None,
574	    predict_fn: Optional[Callable[[list[tuple[str, str]]], Any]] = None,
575	) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
576	    """Return the list of `(i, j)` cluster index pairs (i < j) that BIDIRECTIONALLY entail.
577	
578	    The cross-encoder predict is invoked bounded-parallel across pair-chunks via a
579	    `ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=workers)`. Each chunk produces `(i, j, both_entail)`
580	    triples; results are GATHERED and then sorted by `(i, j)` so the returned edge list is
581	    identical for any worker count (order-independence).
582	
583	    `predict_fn` is an injection seam for the fire-test (a deterministic stub that needs no
584	    GPU/model download); production passes None => the real lazy cross-encoder is used.
585	    """
586	    margin = _margin() if margin is None else margin
587	    workers = _workers() if workers is None else workers
588	    max_pairs = _max_pairs() if max_pairs is None else max_pairs
589	
590	    n = len(texts)
591	    if n < 2:
592	        return []
593	
594	    # Total upper-triangle pair count, computed WITHOUT materializing the O(n^2) list —
595	    # an over-cap bucket must never allocate the full list just to be told it is over
596	    # the cap (I-deepfix-001 W04-prebucket #1369; under-cap construction is unchanged).
597	    total_pairs = n * (n - 1) // 2
598	    pairs: list[tuple[int, int]]
599	    if total_pairs > max_pairs:
600	        if _subbucket_enabled():
601	            # I-deepfix-001 W04-prebucket (#1369): the RIGHT over-cap behavior — SUB-DIVIDE
602	            # the bucket into lexical-overlap sub-buckets whose per-sub-bucket pair count
603	            # stays <= the cap, then score ONLY the within-sub-bucket pairs through the
604	            # UNCHANGED bounded-parallel/predict-chunk/wall machinery below. Near-dups
605	            # (which share most content words) route into the SAME sub-bucket and still
606	            # MERGE; a cross-sub-bucket pair is never scored => stays UNMERGED (a KEEP —
607	            # worst case equals the old whole-bucket skip, never worse; §-1.3). Total
608	            # scored pairs grow ~linearly with n (<= n/2 * size_cap), never O(n^2), so
609	            # the compute/memory blowup the cap guards stays closed.
610	            sub_buckets = _pre_bucket_indices(texts, max_pairs)
611	            pairs = [
612	                (bucket[a], bucket[b])
613	                for bucket in sub_buckets
614	                for a in range(len(bucket))
615	                for b in range(a + 1, len(bucket))
616	            ]
617	            largest = max((len(b) for b in sub_buckets), default=0)
618	            logger.warning(
619	                "[consolidation_nli] W04: %d candidate pairs exceeds %s=%d — PRE-BUCKETING "
620	                "%d texts into %d lexical sub-buckets (largest=%d, per-sub-bucket pairs <= "
621	                "cap); NLI consolidation RUNS on %d within-sub-bucket pairs. Cross-sub-bucket "
622	                "near-dups stay UNMERGED (KEEP — never worse than the skip; no basket "
623	                "dropped, §-1.3).",
624	                total_pairs, ENV_MAX_PAIRS, max_pairs, n, len(sub_buckets), largest,
625	                len(pairs),
626	            )
627	            if not pairs:
628	                return []
629	        else:
630	            # I-deepfix-001 W04-consolidation-nli-wall (#1344): an over-MAX_PAIRS scale guard
631	            # must DEGRADE, not ABORT the whole run. The prior `raise ValueError` propagated
632	            # uncaught through `_apply_consolidation_nli` (finding_dedup.py) and aborted the
633	            # run on a large cluster count. Consolidation is a WEIGHT, not a faithfulness
634	            # gate: when the pair count exceeds the cap, SKIP scoring and return NO edges =>
635	            # the literal clusters pass through UNMERGED (keeps MORE/equal baskets, §-1.3),
636	            # exactly mirroring the prose path's correct over-cap skip in fact_dedup.py. The
637	            # skip is a LOUD telemetry note, never silent. The whole-run abort is converted to
638	            # a per-step under-merge.
639	            logger.warning(
640	                "[consolidation_nli] W04: %d candidate pairs exceeds %s=%d — SKIPPING NLI "
641	                "consolidation for this bucket (literal clusters pass through UNMERGED; no "
642	                "basket dropped). Raise %s or set %s=1 to pre-bucket and merge them.",
643	                total_pairs, ENV_MAX_PAIRS, max_pairs, ENV_MAX_PAIRS, ENV_SUBBUCKET,
644	            )
645	            return []
646	    else:
647	        # All upper-triangle index pairs. O(n^2) is bounded by `max_pairs`.
648	        pairs = [(i, j) for i in range(n) for j in range(i + 1, n)]
649	
650	    # I-deepfix-001 fix-3 (#1344): track whether the predict came from the real lazy
651	    # cross-encoder (production) vs an injected stub (the fire-test seam). On a CUDA OOM
652	    # during predict, the production path RELOADS the model on CPU and re-scores (degrade,
653	    # no basket lost); an injected stub is simply retried (its own recovery semantics).
654	    _injected_predict = predict_fn is not None
655	    if predict_fn is None:
656	        model = _load_model()
657	        predict_fn = model.predict  # type: ignore[assignment]
658	    # A 1-element holder so a CPU degrade in one chunk swaps the active predict for ALL
659	    # subsequent chunks (the GPU model is gone; reloading per chunk would thrash).
660	    _predict_holder: list[Any] = [predict_fn]
661	    _degraded = threading.Event()
662	
663	    # Chunk the pairs across the bounded worker pool. A chunk is scored with ONE batched
664	    # predict (both directions in the same batch), so a chunk == one model call.
665	    n_chunks = max(1, min(workers, len(pairs)))
666	    chunk_size = (len(pairs) + n_chunks - 1) // n_chunks
667	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1344): cap the per-forward batch so it cannot grow unbounded with
668	    # corpus size (the clinical CUBLAS_STATUS_ALLOC_FAILED). At most `workers` chunks run
669	    # concurrently, so peak GPU memory is bounded by workers * predict_chunk regardless of
670	    # corpus size. Union-find over the edges is order-independent => byte-identical output.
671	    _pchunk = _predict_chunk()
672	    if _pchunk > 0:
673	        chunk_size = min(chunk_size, _pchunk)
674	    chunks = [pairs[k:k + chunk_size] for k in range(0, len(pairs), chunk_size)]
675	
676	    def _predict_with_oom_degrade(batch: list[tuple[str, str]]) -> Any:
677	        """Run the active predict; on a CUDA OOM, DEGRADE to CPU (production) or retry
678	        the injected stub, then re-run ONCE. A non-OOM error fails loud (§-1.4)."""
679	        try:
680	            return _predict_holder[0](batch)
681	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — classify: only a CUDA OOM degrades
682	            if not _is_cuda_oom(exc):
683	                raise
684	            if _injected_predict:
685	                # Test/degrade seam: retry the same injected predict once.
686	                _degraded.set()
687	                return _predict_holder[0](batch)
688	            # Production: rebuild the cross-encoder on CPU and swap it in for this and
689	            # every later chunk, then re-score this batch.
690	            cpu_model = _reload_model_on_cpu()
691	            _predict_holder[0] = cpu_model.predict
692	            _degraded.set()
693	            return _predict_holder[0](batch)
694	
695	    def _score_chunk(chunk: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
696	        # Build BOTH directions for every pair in one batch: [A->B, B->A, ...].
697	        batch: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
698	        for i, j in chunk:
699	            batch.append((texts[i], texts[j]))
700	            batch.append((texts[j], texts[i]))
701	        logits = _predict_with_oom_degrade(batch)  # shape (2*len(chunk), 3)
702	        edges: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
703	        for idx, (i, j) in enumerate(chunk):
704	            fwd = logits[2 * idx]
705	            rev = logits[2 * idx + 1]
706	            if _entails(fwd, margin) and _entails(rev, margin):
707	                edges.append((i, j))
708	        return edges
709	
710	    # I-deepfix-001 W04-consolidation-nli-wall (#1344): a TOTAL wall-clock deadline over the
711	    # whole scoring loop. The bounded-parallel gather previously BLOCKED until EVERY chunk
712	    # finished (pool.map drains all); on a slow/CPU-degraded cross-encoder that had no time
713	    # bound. When the deadline passes we STOP collecting further chunk edges and return the
714	    # edges gathered so far. A partial edge set only UNDER-merges => keeps MORE/equal baskets
715	    # (§-1.3), never drops a corroborator. `wall <= 0` disables the deadline (unbounded, the
716	    # escape hatch / byte-identical default-OFF caller). Deterministic: the returned edges are
717	    # still sorted, so for a non-truncated run the output is identical regardless of timing.
718	    _wall = _wall_seconds()
719	    _deadline = (time.monotonic() + _wall) if _wall > 0 else None
720	
721	    def _deadline_passed() -> bool:
722	        return _deadline is not None and time.monotonic() > _deadline
723	
724	    all_edges: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
725	    _truncated = False
726	    if workers <= 1 or len(chunks) <= 1:
727	        for chunk in chunks:
728	            if _deadline_passed():
729	                _truncated = True
730	                break
731	            all_edges.extend(_score_chunk(chunk))
732	    else:
733	        # I-deepfix-001 W04 (#1344): manage the pool MANUALLY (NOT `with`) so the
734	        # non-blocking shutdown cannot be defeated by `with`'s __exit__ shutdown(wait=True),
735	        # which would BLOCK until a wedged cross-encoder chunk finishes — making the wall
736	        # cosmetic (the function would not RETURN until the slow chunks drained).
737	        pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=workers)
738	        try:
739	            futures = [pool.submit(_score_chunk, chunk) for chunk in chunks]
740	            pending = set(futures)
741	            while pending:
742	                _remaining = None if _deadline is None else max(0.0, _deadline - time.monotonic())
743	                if _remaining is not None and _remaining <= 0:
744	                    _truncated = True
745	                    break
746	                done, pending = futures_wait(
747	                    pending, timeout=_remaining, return_when=FIRST_COMPLETED,
748	                )
749	                if not done:
750	                    # `wait` returned on the timeout with nothing newly completed => the wall
751	                    # elapsed mid-flight. Collect whatever already finished and stop.
752	                    _truncated = True
753	                    break
754	                for fut in done:
755	                    all_edges.extend(fut.result())
756	            if _truncated:
757	                # Collect any futures that DID finish before the wall (do not block on the
758	                # rest — they keep running to completion in their threads but we stop waiting,
759	                # SS-1.3: skipping their edges only UNDER-merges).
760	                for fut in list(pending):
761	                    if fut.done():
762	                        try:
763	                            all_edges.extend(fut.result())
764	                        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a late failure must not abort the run
765	                            pass
766	        finally:
767	            # NON-BLOCKING teardown so a wedged chunk cannot delay the partial return.
768	            pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
769	    if _truncated:
770	        logger.warning(
771	            "[consolidation_nli] W04: scoring wall (%ss) elapsed with %d/%d chunks scored "
772	            "— returning the partial edge set (UNDER-merges only; no basket dropped, §-1.3). "
773	            "Raise %s to score more pairs.",
774	            _wall, len(all_edges), len(chunks), ENV_WALL_SECONDS,
775	        )
776	
777	    # DETERMINISTIC post-step: sort the gathered edges so the union-find input is
778	    # concurrency-invariant (the union is order-stable anyway because it attaches to the
779	    # lowest root, but sorting makes the contract explicit and the output reproducible).
780	    all_edges.sort()
781	    return all_edges
782	
783	
784	def group_clusters(
785	    cluster_texts: list[str],
786	    *,
787	    margin: Optional[float] = None,
788	    workers: Optional[int] = None,
789	    max_pairs: Optional[int] = None,
790	    predict_fn: Optional[Callable[[list[tuple[str, str]]], Any]] = None,
791	) -> dict[int, int]:
792	    """Bidirectional-NLI consolidation over literal clusters.
793	
794	    Input: `cluster_texts[k]` = the representative text of literal cluster `k`.
795	    Output: a mapping `cluster_index -> merged_group_root` where same-claim paraphrase
796	    clusters share a root (the LOWEST member index). Clusters with no entailing partner map
797	    to themselves. The mapping is identical for any `workers` value (order-independent).
798	    """
799	    n = len(cluster_texts)
800	    uf = _UnionFind.of(n)
801	    edges = score_pairs(
802	        cluster_texts, margin=margin, workers=workers,
803	        max_pairs=max_pairs, predict_fn=predict_fn,
804	    )
805	    for i, j in edges:
806	        uf.union(i, j)
807	    return {k: uf.find(k) for k in range(n)}
808

1	"""I-deepfix-001 (#1344) W9 — content-dedup CONSOLIDATE-KEEP-ALL (body-syndication baskets).
2	
3	The W9 winner is dedup=ContentDeduplicator. Its native ``deduplicate()`` returns
4	``unique_items`` — a DROP that sheds corroborators, which VIOLATES §-1.3 (the pipeline
5	is WEIGHT-and-CONSOLIDATE, never FILTER-and-DROP). So W9 is wired here in the ONLY
6	§-1.3-legal form: ContentDeduplicator's body-MinHash CLUSTERS are used to GROUP
7	near-identical-body sources into a corroboration BASKET, and EVERY row is kept.
8	Nothing is dropped; nothing is merged.
9	
10	WHAT IT ADDS over the existing finding_dedup same-work consolidation (#7, which keys
11	on DOI / folded title): finding_dedup catches the SAME work cited under the same
12	identity. It MISSES different-title near-identical-BODY syndication — the same report
13	republished at two URLs with different titles and no shared DOI. That is exactly what
14	ContentDeduplicator's body-MinHash catches. Here it is surfaced as a keep-all
15	corroboration WEIGHT, never a drop.
16	
17	FAITHFULNESS POSTURE (the load-bearing safety argument — VERIFY it):
18	  * KEEP-ALL: every input row is returned, unchanged except for additive annotation
19	    keys. No row is removed; the output list has the SAME length as the input.
20	  * MERGE-NOTHING: unlike finding_dedup this NEVER collapses two findings into one
21	    representative. It only ATTACHES "these N rows share a near-identical body" to
22	    each member. So it cannot over-merge two distinct clinical findings — the
23	    clinical-lethal over-merge risk finding_dedup's conservative key guards against is
24	    not in play here, because no claim is ever dropped, rewritten, or merged.
25	  * NEAR-IDENTICAL ONLY: grouping fires only at the EXACT/NEAR_DUPLICATE tier
26	    (MinHash similarity >= ``PG_W9_BODY_SIM``, default 0.85). The loose SIMILAR tier
27	    (0.70-0.85) is DELIBERATELY excluded (we pin ``similar_threshold`` up to the
28	    near-dup floor) so merely-topical sources are NOT grouped — only true syndication.
29	  * GROUNDING-UNTOUCHED: the annotation is metadata. It never changes which rows
30	    ground prose, never feeds or relaxes strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / span-grounding.
31	    A corroboration count is a disclosure WEIGHT, not a gate.
32	
33	Pure leaf: no network, no model, no LLM. Reuses ``src.utils.content_deduplicator``
34	(MinHash/SimHash, pure python, bounded O(n^2) over the corpus). Env kill-switch
35	(LAW VI). DEFAULT ON. ROW-level byte-identical when no two rows share a near-identical
36	body (no multi-member cluster => no row gains an annotation key); the stage still emits
37	a zeroed telemetry dict + one canary log line as the observability signal (it does NOT
38	claim an empty/absent result object).
39	"""
40	from __future__ import annotations
41	
42	import logging
43	import os
44	from typing import Any
45	
46	from src.utils.content_deduplicator import (
47	    ContentDeduplicator,
48	    DeduplicationConfig,
49	)
50	
51	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.content_dedup_consolidate")
52	
53	_ENV_FLAG = "PG_CONTENT_DEDUP_CONSOLIDATE"
54	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", ""})
55	
56	# MinHash similarity floor for "near-identical body" (LAW VI). The SIMILAR tier is
57	# pinned UP to this floor so only EXACT + NEAR_DUPLICATE group — never merely-topical.
58	_ENV_SIM = "PG_W9_BODY_SIM"
59	_DEFAULT_SIM = 0.85
60	
61	# A body shorter than this is too small to call "syndication" reliably (MinHash on a
62	# tiny snippet is noisy). Such rows stay SINGLETONS (kept, never annotated, never
63	# dropped). LAW VI.
64	_ENV_MIN_BODY_CHARS = "PG_W9_MIN_BODY_CHARS"
65	_DEFAULT_MIN_BODY_CHARS = 200
66	
67	
68	def consolidate_enabled() -> bool:
69	    """Kill-switch. DEFAULT ON. Set ``PG_CONTENT_DEDUP_CONSOLIDATE`` to a falsey value
70	    to disable — rows are then returned unannotated (byte-identical)."""
71	    return os.getenv(_ENV_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
72	
73	
74	def _sim() -> float:
75	    raw = os.getenv(_ENV_SIM, "").strip()
76	    if not raw:
77	        return _DEFAULT_SIM
78	    try:
79	        return min(max(float(raw), 0.0), 1.0)
80	    except ValueError:
81	        return _DEFAULT_SIM
82	
83	
84	def _min_body_chars() -> int:
85	    try:
86	        return max(0, int(os.getenv(_ENV_MIN_BODY_CHARS, str(_DEFAULT_MIN_BODY_CHARS))))
87	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
88	        return _DEFAULT_MIN_BODY_CHARS
89	
90	
91	def _dedup_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
92	    """The body text W9 compares: direct_quote, then statement, then title. NOT the
93	    empty 'content' key (which is absent on Gate-B rows — comparing it would fold every
94	    source into ONE cluster, the false-green the workflow draft hit)."""
95	    return str(
96	        row.get("direct_quote")
97	        or row.get("statement")
98	        or row.get("title")
99	        or row.get("source_title")
100	        or ""
101	    ).strip()
102	
103	
104	class _UnionFind:
105	    def __init__(self, n: int) -> None:
106	        self._parent = list(range(n))
107	
108	    def find(self, x: int) -> int:
109	        root = x
110	        while self._parent[root] != root:
111	            root = self._parent[root]
112	        while self._parent[x] != root:  # path compression
113	            self._parent[x], x = root, self._parent[x]
114	        return root
115	
116	    def union(self, a: int, b: int) -> None:
117	        ra, rb = self.find(a), self.find(b)
118	        if ra != rb:
119	            self._parent[max(ra, rb)] = min(ra, rb)
120	
121	
122	def consolidate_body_syndication(
123	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
124	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
125	    """Annotate near-identical-body sources into KEEP-ALL corroboration baskets.
126	
127	    Returns ``(rows, telemetry)``. ``rows`` is the SAME list object, with each member
128	    of a multi-row body-syndication basket gaining three additive keys:
129	    ``body_syndication_cluster_id`` (stable, derived from the basket's smallest
130	    evidence_id), ``body_syndication_count`` (basket size >= 2), and
131	    ``body_syndication_ev_ids`` (the sorted basket of evidence_ids). Singletons and
132	    short-body rows are untouched. The list NEVER shrinks and no two rows are merged.
133	
134	    ``telemetry`` reports ``rows_in`` / ``eligible`` / ``baskets`` / ``rows_grouped`` /
135	    ``rows_dropped`` (always 0) so the stage is OBSERVABLE in the run artifacts.
136	    """
137	    n_total = len(rows)
138	    telemetry: dict[str, Any] = {
139	        "rows_in": n_total,
140	        "eligible": 0,
141	        "baskets": 0,
142	        "rows_grouped": 0,
143	        "rows_dropped": 0,
144	        "enabled": consolidate_enabled(),
145	    }
146	    if not consolidate_enabled() or n_total < 2:
147	        return rows, telemetry
148	
149	    min_chars = _min_body_chars()
150	    # Eligible rows: a non-trivial body. Build the items the deduplicator scores, and a
151	    # back-map item_index -> original row index. Short/empty-body rows stay singletons.
152	    items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
153	    positions: list[int] = []
154	    for idx, row in enumerate(rows):
155	        text = _dedup_text(row)
156	        if len(text) >= min_chars:
157	            items.append({"_w9_text": text})
158	            positions.append(idx)
159	    telemetry["eligible"] = len(items)
160	    if len(items) < 2:
161	        logger.info(
162	            "[content_dedup_consolidate] W9: %d rows, %d eligible (>=%d chars) -> "
163	            "0 baskets, 0 dropped (KEEP-ALL)",
164	            n_total, len(items), min_chars,
165	        )
166	        return rows, telemetry
167	
168	    # Pin similar_threshold UP to the near-dup floor so ONLY exact + near-identical
169	    # bodies are flagged duplicates (the loose SIMILAR tier never fires) — no topical
170	    # over-grouping, and no row is prematurely claimed by a loose match.
171	    sim = _sim()
172	    cfg = DeduplicationConfig(
173	        exact_match_threshold=1.0,
174	        near_duplicate_threshold=sim,
175	        similar_threshold=sim,
176	    )
177	    try:
178	        result = ContentDeduplicator(cfg).deduplicate(items, content_key="_w9_text")
179	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a dedup bug must NEVER drop/merge a row
180	        logger.warning(
181	            "[content_dedup_consolidate] W9 dedup errored (%s) — returning rows "
182	            "UNANNOTATED (no drop, no merge on a bug).", str(exc)[:160],
183	        )
184	        return rows, telemetry
185	
186	    # Union the near-identical edges over the ELIGIBLE-item index space, then translate
187	    # the multi-member components back to ORIGINAL row indices. Every edge here is
188	    # EXACT/NEAR (>= sim) by construction of cfg, so no SIMILAR-tier edge sneaks in.
189	    uf = _UnionFind(len(items))
190	    for dup in result.duplicates:
191	        uf.union(dup.original_index, dup.duplicate_index)
192	    groups: dict[int, list[int]] = {}
193	    for item_idx in range(len(items)):
194	        groups.setdefault(uf.find(item_idx), []).append(item_idx)
195	
196	    baskets = 0
197	    rows_grouped = 0
198	    for members in groups.values():
199	        if len(members) < 2:
200	            continue
201	        orig_idx = [positions[m] for m in members]
202	        ev_ids = sorted(str(rows[o].get("evidence_id", "")) for o in orig_idx)
203	        cluster_id = "bsyn_" + (next((e for e in ev_ids if e), str(orig_idx[0])))
204	        for o in orig_idx:
205	            rows[o]["body_syndication_cluster_id"] = cluster_id
206	            rows[o]["body_syndication_count"] = len(orig_idx)
207	            rows[o]["body_syndication_ev_ids"] = ev_ids
208	        baskets += 1
209	        rows_grouped += len(orig_idx)
210	
211	    telemetry["baskets"] = baskets
212	    telemetry["rows_grouped"] = rows_grouped
213	    # Firing canary: present on every Gate-B run that reaches this stage; its counts
214	    # make W9 OBSERVABLE (vs. the prior build-deferred ABSENCE). KEEP-ALL invariant
215	    # asserted in the message: 0 dropped, list length unchanged.
216	    logger.info(
217	        "[content_dedup_consolidate] W9: %d rows (%d eligible) -> %d body-syndication "
218	        "basket(s), %d rows grouped, 0 dropped (KEEP-ALL, sim>=%.2f)",
219	        n_total, len(items), baskets, rows_grouped, sim,
220	    )
221	    return rows, telemetry
222
