1	"""I-cred-012 (#1162) — credibility-analysis pass ORCHESTRATOR (the activation chain).
2	
3	Pure orchestrator that runs the committed P4→P3→P2→P5→P6 redesign chain over the generator's EFFECTIVE
4	evidence pool and returns the disclosure inputs (``credibility_by_evidence``, ``origin_by_evidence``,
5	``claims``, ``edges``, ``weight_mass``). The sweep runner calls it under the master slate
6	``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN``; OFF ⇒ not invoked ⇒ byte-identical. ADVISORY only: ``strict_verify`` +
7	the 4-role D8 release policy stay the ONLY binding gates — nothing here keeps/drops a sentence or flips
8	release.
9	
10	FAIL-LOUD vs LABEL (I-arch-005 B12, operator-locked 2026-06-14 "VERIFY = LABEL, NEVER HOLD"):
11	a row missing ``evidence_id``, a P4 independence-annotation gap, or a wired-module crash still ABORT the
12	pass (``CredibilityPassError``) — those are real, unrecoverable integrity holes. But the two INFRA
13	conditions that used to abort the WHOLE report no longer hold it (I-arch-005 B12-P1, operator-locked
14	2026-06-14 "nothing shall hold the report"):
15	  * a PER-SOURCE P2 ``judge_error`` — ``score_source_credibility`` isolates it to ONE row and falls back
16	    to that source's DETERMINISTIC priors (a real weight, never fabricated);
17	  * a MISSING production credibility judge (``judge=None``) — an infra/config condition, NOT a
18	    faithfulness finding: the chain runs priors-only and every source carries its real deterministic
19	    authority weight.
20	In both cases the affected sources are LABELED ``credibility_unscored`` (a disclosed gap, surfaced LOUD
21	per LAW II — never a silent downgrade) and the rest keep scoring; the report SHIPS with the gap rather
22	than HELD. The genuine cited-evidence coverage gap (a CITED evidence_id with NO credibility row at all)
23	stays fail-loud — that is a real provenance hole, caught downstream by ``apply_disclosure_to_svs``'s
24	coverage assertion. The credibility WEIGHT math is untouched; only the abort-the-basket reaction on
25	those two infra conditions became a per-source label (the pass is ADVISORY — the binding gates are
26	``strict_verify`` + the 4-role D8 release policy).
27	
28	Order (locked): P4 copied-annotated rows (fail-loud on missing eid) → P3 supersession → P2 score
29	(LABEL credibility_unscored on judge_error / judge=None, never abort) → POST-P3 credibility =
30	P2 × P3 multiplier (certainty carried) → P5 claim graph
31	→ P6 weight-mass over the POST-P3 judgments. P10 dissent + the M-52 effective-pool hoist + the P8
32	render-site wrapper are the per-hook sub-issues; this module is the chain core they consume.
33	"""
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import dataclasses
37	import os
38	import time
39	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
40	from typing import Any, Callable
41	
42	from src.polaris_graph.authority.credibility_skill import score_source_credibility
43	from src.polaris_graph.authority.supersession import supersession_adjustment
44	from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.claim_graph import build_claim_graph
45	from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.independence_collapse import collapse_independent_origins
46	from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.weight_mass import aggregate_weight_mass
47	
48	_MASTER_FLAG = "PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN"
49	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
50	
51	# I-arch-008 HOP-A KEYSTONE (#1265): the basket consolidator re-clusters claims from scratch via
52	# ``claim_graph.build_claim_graph`` whose FAIL-CLOSED ``build_merge_key`` singletons every clinical
53	# numeric whose dose/comparator/effect_measure/endpoint is blank (the documented A13 residual). That
54	# fragments 787 sources -> ~1781 mostly-singleton clusters (only 1 multi-member), so
55	# ``verified_support_origin_count`` stays <=1 everywhere and the rendered header reads "Multi-source
56	# corroborated (>=2 verified origins): 0". MEANWHILE ``finding_dedup.dedup_by_finding`` ALREADY groups
57	# the SAME rows by numeric finding (787 -> ~99 clusters WITH member_indices + member_hosts), and that
58	# grouping is on the LIVE run path (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:8079) — but it is thrown away for the basket
59	# path. When this flag is ON the basket consolidator CONSUMES finding_dedup's already-computed cluster
60	# grouping: it MERGES claim_graph's existing claim partition (never rebuilds members from rows, never
61	# splits, never drops) so same-finding members land in ONE basket, then runs the EXISTING isolated
62	# per-member verify UNCHANGED. Multi-source baskets EMERGE because the members are already grouped — NOT
63	# because any member newly passes the gate (the set that passes ``_verify_member_in_isolation`` is
64	# byte-identical: same claim.text, same span, same verify_fn). DEFAULT OFF => the legacy claim_graph
65	# grouping runs byte-identical (§-1.3 WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, never FILTER-AND-CAP).
66	_ENV_BASKET_CONSUME_FINDING_DEDUP = "PG_BASKET_CONSUME_FINDING_DEDUP"
67	
68	
69	def basket_consume_finding_dedup_enabled() -> bool:
70	    """True iff the HOP-A keystone (consume finding_dedup grouping for baskets) is explicitly enabled.
71	
72	    DEFAULT OFF (unset / 0 / off / false / no) => the legacy claim_graph cluster grouping is used
73	    verbatim => the assembled baskets, the rendered header, and the disclosure JSON are byte-identical
74	    to the pre-change tree. ON => ``_regroup_graph_by_finding_dedup`` merges the claim partition using
75	    finding_dedup membership before ``_assemble_baskets`` (grouping + origin-counting ONLY — the
76	    per-member verify and strict_verify are NEVER touched)."""
77	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_BASKET_CONSUME_FINDING_DEDUP, "").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
78	
79	# I-arch-007 ITEM 1b (#1264): bounded parallelism for the per-member isolated-verify loop in
80	# ``_assemble_baskets``. The advisory pass verifies EVERY basket member against its OWN single span via
81	# the production entailment judge — a SERIAL O(N) loop over hundreds of members at up to 150 s/call is the
82	# wedge that hung Q72/Q76/Q90 (the credibility-pass death). Parallelizing the INDEPENDENT per-member
83	# verifies with a BOUNDED pool + deterministic post-step reassembly changes WALL-CLOCK only — never which
84	# verdict any member gets (``_verify_member_in_isolation`` is pure per-member: each member's claim vs its
85	# OWN single span) and never a binding gate (the pass is ADVISORY; ``basket_verdict`` is a pure LABEL).
86	# LAW VI: env-overridable, no magic number. DEFAULT 1 = the byte-identical SERIAL path — the parallelism
87	# stays INERT until explicitly enabled, which honors the HARD ordering constraint that 1b's parallel
88	# verifies (they share the singleton entailment-judge client) must NOT run concurrently before ITEM 2a
89	# makes that client thread-safe/thread-local (CONSOLIDATED_FIX_PLAN ITEM 1b "HARD ORDERING CONSTRAINT").
90	_ENV_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT = "PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT"
91	_DEFAULT_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT = 1
92	
93	# I-deepfix-001 BANK-BEFORE-WALL (#1264 follow-up): when the caller threads a soft deadline, the
94	# fraction of the REMAINING budget granted to phase A (P2 source scoring) — the rest is reserved for
95	# the per-member basket verifies (phase B, the leg that feeds the SUPPORTS baskets / the breadth
96	# enrichment). Without this split, phase A could consume the entire budget and phase B would bank
97	# ZERO verified members => the corroboration layer still empties (the exact drb_72 box1 defect
98	# reappearing one phase later). LAW VI: env-overridable; a bad value falls back to the default.
99	_ENV_PHASE_A_BUDGET_FRAC = "PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_PHASE_A_FRAC"
100	_DEFAULT_PHASE_A_BUDGET_FRAC = 0.5
101	# Floor on any phase budget (seconds) so a near-expired deadline still lets a few members bank
102	# rather than sentinel-filling everything on arrival.
103	_MIN_PHASE_BUDGET_S = 60.0
104	
105	
106	def _phase_a_budget_frac() -> float:
107	    """The phase-A share of the remaining soft-deadline budget, in (0, 1). Bad/out-of-range env
108	    values fall back to the default (fail-safe: the split must always leave phase B a share)."""
109	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_PHASE_A_BUDGET_FRAC, "").strip()
110	    if not raw:
111	        return _DEFAULT_PHASE_A_BUDGET_FRAC
112	    try:
113	        val = float(raw)
114	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
115	        return _DEFAULT_PHASE_A_BUDGET_FRAC
116	    if not (0.0 < val < 1.0):
117	        return _DEFAULT_PHASE_A_BUDGET_FRAC
118	    return val
119	
120	
121	def _pass_max_inflight() -> int:
122	    """The bounded per-member verify concurrency (LAW VI). 1 (default) ⇒ the serial path is taken
123	    verbatim — byte-identical to the pre-1b loop. A bad/empty value falls back to the serial default
124	    (fail-safe, never an unbounded pool)."""
125	    try:
126	        value = int(
127	            os.environ.get(_ENV_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT, _DEFAULT_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT)
128	            or _DEFAULT_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT
129	        )
130	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
131	        return _DEFAULT_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT
132	    return value if value >= 1 else _DEFAULT_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT
133	
134	
135	# ── I-deepfix-001 B9(a) (#1353): deterministic tier-authority prior join ─────────────────────────
136	# FORENSIC ROOT (verified against the banked drb_72 corpus_snapshot): the generator-visible evidence
137	# rows arrive with NO ``authority_score`` (and no ``source_class`` / ``signal_scores`` to recompute the
138	# full authority model from), so ``weight_mass.aggregate_weight_mass`` reads ``authority_score`` = None
139	# -> ``cluster_mass`` = 0.0 across EVERY claim and ``weight_mass`` renders 0.0 — the opposite of the
140	# CONSOLIDATE-and-WEIGHT goal. The rows DO carry a ``tier`` (T1..T7 / UNKNOWN) on every row. The fix:
141	# when a row lacks ``authority_score``, JOIN a deterministic tier-derived prior onto the row copy so a
142	# real, honest weight flows to BOTH the canonical-selection in independence_collapse (lowest-authority
143	# canonical for an undated cluster) AND to weight_mass.cluster_mass. This is a WEIGHT, never a DROP /
144	# CAP / FILTER (§-1.3); it never touches strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance. It is joined at the
145	# TOP of _run_chain (before collapse) so collapse and weight_mass agree on the canonical.
146	#
147	# The per-tier prior MIRRORS ``nodes.weighted_corpus_gate._DEFAULT_TIER_CREDIBILITY_PRIOR`` (one
148	# semantic source of truth; kept local to avoid coupling a synthesis module to a nodes module). LAW VI:
149	# the whole map is env-overridable via PG_CREDIBILITY_TIER_AUTHORITY_PRIOR (JSON), and the join itself
150	# is gated default-ON by PG_CREDIBILITY_TIER_AUTHORITY_JOIN.
151	_ENV_TIER_AUTHORITY_JOIN = "PG_CREDIBILITY_TIER_AUTHORITY_JOIN"
152	_ENV_TIER_AUTHORITY_PRIOR = "PG_CREDIBILITY_TIER_AUTHORITY_PRIOR"
153	# I-deepfix-001 (#1344) W1: the UNKNOWN/no-match prior is RAISED off 0.20 to a neutral 0.45
154	# ("unclassified", disclosed) — a credible NON-journal institution that the tier classifier does
155	# not recognize (WEF/OECD/most IGOs are absent from every tier set) was being pinned to the SAME
156	# 0.20 band as an anonymous blog, a de-facto SOFT FILTER (weight_mass = authority_score, so it
157	# sank and lost slots). 0.45 is a CALIBRATED credibility weight on the continuous scale, not a
158	# floor forcing a coverage number — it is the honest "unclassified" position; predatory/junk still
159	# lands low via the authority model's junk_detection. LAW VI: fully overridable via the env JSON.
160	_DEFAULT_TIER_AUTHORITY_PRIOR: dict[str, float] = {
161	    "T1": 0.95, "T2": 0.85, "T3": 0.75, "T4": 0.60,
162	    "T5": 0.40, "T6": 0.30, "T7": 0.15, "UNKNOWN": 0.45,
163	}
164	# B9(a) fail-loud canary: with the master redesign ON, an all-zero authority_score across rows is a
165	# WIRING BREAK (the join no-oped), not a legitimate zero. Default ON => the LOUD warning always fires;
166	# the hard raise is opt-in (PG_REQUIRE_NONZERO_AUTHORITY) to honor the FAIL-OPEN rule (a scorer-side
167	# wiring detection must never itself drop a source).
168	_ENV_REQUIRE_NONZERO_AUTHORITY = "PG_REQUIRE_NONZERO_AUTHORITY"
169	
170	# ── I-deepfix-003 (#1374): recognized-institution TIER FLOOR (RAISE-ONLY, anchor-eligibility) ─────
171	# The tier classifier buries credible NON-journal institutions at T6/UNKNOWN, and the downstream
172	# anchor-to-strong-source signal reads the TIER label — so a WEF/OECD/BLS/think-tank/university
173	# source is not seen as a valid anchor even though its authority WEIGHT is high. When a row's URL
174	# resolves to an ``institutional_authority`` band (registry OR the ``*.gov`` / ``*.edu`` rule) this
175	# stamps a RAISE-ONLY tier floor so the source becomes anchor-eligible in the synthesis layer
176	# (AtomicClaim.source_tier / BasketMember.source_tier both derive from the row ``tier`` this join
177	# writes). It NEVER lowers a stronger existing tier (a genuine T1 journal keeps T1) and NEVER touches
178	# the faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance) — a claim's grounding is
179	# unchanged; only where the source SORTS as an anchor changes. Behind its OWN kill-switch
180	# (default ON); OFF => tier unchanged while the WEIGHT leg still runs (that rides
181	# PG_INSTITUTIONAL_AUTHORITY_WEIGHT). §-1.3 WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, never FILTER-AND-CAP.
182	_ENV_INSTITUTIONAL_TIER = "PG_INSTITUTIONAL_AUTHORITY_TIER"
183	# band_key -> anchor-eligible tier FLOOR. igo / statistical_agency / government (primary
184	# institutional producers) AND think_tank / news_masthead / university (rigorous secondary
185	# analysis) ALL floor to T3 — operator decision 2026-07-09 (Fable gate item A) so credible
186	# institutions clear the T1-T3 anchor bar. (``government`` is the band the ``*.gov`` /
187	# ``ed.gov`` suffix rule assigns.)
188	_INSTITUTIONAL_TIER_FLOOR: dict[str, str] = {
189	    "igo": "T3",
190	    "statistical_agency": "T3",
191	    "government": "T3",
192	    # Operator decision 2026-07-09 (Fable gate item A): raise think_tank / news_masthead /
193	    # university from T4 to T3 so credible institutions (HBR, MIT Sloan, Tony Blair Institute,
194	    # AFL-CIO, universities) CLEAR the T1-T3 "strong anchor" bar (Signal-2). The explicit
195	    # authority_note ("institutional authority: <band>") preserves transparency — T3 here means
196	    # "government OR recognized institutional authority", it never implies a think-tank IS a
197	    # government agency. Still RAISE-ONLY (a genuine T1/T2 journal is never lowered to T3).
198	    "think_tank": "T3",
199	    "news_masthead": "T3",
200	    "university": "T3",
201	}
202	# Tier STRENGTH rank: lower = stronger (T1 strongest, UNKNOWN weakest). Used ONLY for the RAISE-ONLY
203	# guard — a floor is applied to a field iff it is STRICTLY STRONGER than that field's current value,
204	# so no field is ever lowered.
205	_TIER_STRENGTH_RANK: dict[str, int] = {
206	    "T1": 1, "T2": 2, "T3": 3, "T4": 4, "T5": 5, "T6": 6, "T7": 7, "UNKNOWN": 8,
207	}
208	
209	
210	def institutional_tier_floor_enabled() -> bool:
211	    """True iff the I-deepfix-003 recognized-institution TIER-floor leg is active (LAW VI kill-switch
212	    ``PG_INSTITUTIONAL_AUTHORITY_TIER``, default ON). OFF => tiers are left untouched by this join
213	    (and no ``authority_note`` is stamped); the WEIGHT leg is independent and keeps running."""
214	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_INSTITUTIONAL_TIER, "on").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
215	
216	
217	def _tier_rank_of(value: object) -> int:
218	    """The STRENGTH rank of a raw tier value (blank/unrecognized => UNKNOWN = weakest). RAISE-ONLY
219	    guard helper: read per-field so a stronger existing tier in either ``tier`` / ``source_tier`` is
220	    never lowered."""
221	    key = str(value or "").strip().upper()
222	    return _TIER_STRENGTH_RANK.get(key if key in _TIER_STRENGTH_RANK else "UNKNOWN", 8)
223	
224	
225	def tier_authority_join_enabled() -> bool:
226	    """True iff the default-ON B9(a) tier-authority prior join is active (LAW VI kill-switch
227	    ``PG_CREDIBILITY_TIER_AUTHORITY_JOIN=0`` => rows keep whatever authority_score they arrived with)."""
228	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_TIER_AUTHORITY_JOIN, "on").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
229	
230	
231	def _tier_authority_prior_map() -> dict[str, float]:
232	    """The per-tier authority prior (LAW VI). Env JSON override merges over the default; a malformed
233	    override falls back to the default (fail-safe: never an empty/zeroed map). PURE-ish (reads env)."""
234	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_TIER_AUTHORITY_PRIOR, "").strip()
235	    if not raw:
236	        return dict(_DEFAULT_TIER_AUTHORITY_PRIOR)
237	    try:
238	        import json as _json  # noqa: PLC0415
239	        override = _json.loads(raw)
240	        if not isinstance(override, dict):
241	            return dict(_DEFAULT_TIER_AUTHORITY_PRIOR)
242	        merged = dict(_DEFAULT_TIER_AUTHORITY_PRIOR)
243	        for k, v in override.items():
244	            merged[str(k).strip().upper()] = _clamp01(v) or 0.0
245	        return merged
246	    except Exception:
247	        return dict(_DEFAULT_TIER_AUTHORITY_PRIOR)
248	
249	
250	def _tier_of_row(row: dict) -> str:
251	    """The row's tier label, normalized to the prior-map key shape (``T4`` / ``UNKNOWN``). A blank /
252	    unrecognized tier maps to ``UNKNOWN`` (the conservative low prior, never a wrong-high default)."""
253	    tier = str(row.get("source_tier") or row.get("tier") or "").strip().upper()
254	    return tier if tier in _DEFAULT_TIER_AUTHORITY_PRIOR else "UNKNOWN"
255	
256	
257	def _row_url(row: dict) -> str:
258	    """The row's source URL for the W1 institutional lookup (first non-empty of the usual keys)."""
259	    for key in ("source_url", "url", "canonical_url", "final_url"):
260	        val = row.get(key)
261	        if isinstance(val, str) and val.strip():
262	            return val.strip()
263	    return ""
264	
265	
266	def _join_tier_authority_prior(rows: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
267	    """B9(a): return COPIES of ``rows`` with a deterministic tier-derived ``authority_score`` joined
268	    onto any row that lacks one (None / missing / non-numeric). A row that ALREADY carries a numeric
269	    authority_score is preserved verbatim (the real computed weight wins; the tier prior is only a
270	    fallback). Never mutates the caller's rows; never drops a row. Returns the rows unchanged when the
271	    join is disabled. PURE-ish (reads env via the prior map).
272	
273	    I-deepfix-001 (#1344) W1 — POSITIVE institutional-authority WEIGHT: after the base
274	    authority_score is resolved (real computed weight OR tier prior), a recognized credible
275	    institution (WEF/OECD/IGO, national statistical agency, major think-tank, reputable news
276	    masthead — the LAW-VI ``institutional_authority`` registry) has its authority_score RAISED to
277	    its calibrated institutional band. This is a RAISE-ONLY floor: a real computed weight ABOVE the
278	    band is never lowered, and a freak-low weight can never demote a real institution. Disclosed via
279	    ``authority_score_source``.
280	
281	    I-deepfix-003 (#1374) — recognized-institution TIER FLOOR + explicit label: when the row's URL
282	    resolves to an institutional band (registry OR the ``*.gov`` / ``*.edu`` rule) this ALSO stamps a
283	    RAISE-ONLY anchor-eligible tier floor (igo/statistical_agency/government -> T3;
284	    think_tank/news_masthead/university -> T3) so a credible institution the tier classifier buried at
285	    T6/UNKNOWN is seen as a valid anchor downstream (AtomicClaim.source_tier / BasketMember.source_tier
286	    both derive from the row ``tier`` written here). Each tier field is guarded against ITS OWN current
287	    rank, so a stronger existing tier (a genuine T1 journal) is NEVER lowered. It also stamps an
288	    explicit ``authority_note = "institutional authority: <band>"`` so a reader sees WHY the source is
289	    credible. The tier leg is behind its OWN kill-switch ``PG_INSTITUTIONAL_AUTHORITY_TIER`` (default
290	    ON); OFF => tier + note untouched while the WEIGHT leg (``PG_INSTITUTIONAL_AUTHORITY_WEIGHT``) still
291	    runs. Nothing is dropped and the faithfulness engine is untouched — a pure WEIGHT/tier correction
292	    of the de-facto soft-filter (``weight_mass = authority_score``, so a mis-low institution sank and
293	    lost slots). §-1.3 WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, never FILTER-AND-CAP."""
294	    if not tier_authority_join_enabled():
295	        return rows
296	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.institutional_authority import (  # noqa: PLC0415
297	        institutional_authority_for_url,
298	        institutional_band_for_url,
299	    )
300	
301	    prior = _tier_authority_prior_map()
302	    tier_floor_on = institutional_tier_floor_enabled()
303	    out: list[dict] = []
304	    for row in rows:
305	        existing = row.get("authority_score")
306	        has_existing = (
307	            isinstance(existing, (int, float)) and not isinstance(existing, bool)
308	        )
309	        base = (
310	            float(existing)
311	            if has_existing
312	            else float(prior.get(_tier_of_row(row), prior["UNKNOWN"]))
313	        )
314	        url = _row_url(row)
315	        # ``new_row`` stays None until something actually changes -> the caller's row is appended
316	        # verbatim (byte-identical no-op) when neither leg fires.
317	        new_row: dict | None = None
318	
319	        # ── W1 WEIGHT leg (PG_INSTITUTIONAL_AUTHORITY_WEIGHT, RAISE-ONLY) ──
320	        inst_weight = institutional_authority_for_url(url)
321	        if inst_weight is not None and inst_weight > base:
322	            new_row = dict(row)  # COPY — never mutate the caller's row
323	            new_row["authority_score"] = float(inst_weight)
324	            new_row["authority_score_source"] = "institutional_registry"  # disclosed
325	            new_row["institutional_authority_band"] = float(inst_weight)
326	        elif not has_existing:
327	            new_row = dict(row)  # COPY — never mutate the caller's row
328	            new_row["authority_score"] = base
329	            new_row["authority_score_source"] = "tier_prior"  # disclosed deterministic fallback
330	        # else: has_existing and no institutional raise -> weight kept verbatim (new_row stays None).
331	
332	        # ── I-deepfix-003 TIER-floor leg + authority_note (PG_INSTITUTIONAL_AUTHORITY_TIER, RAISE-ONLY) ──
333	        if tier_floor_on:
334	            band_key = institutional_band_for_url(url)
335	            if band_key is not None:
336	                if new_row is None:
337	                    new_row = dict(row)  # promote to a COPY so we never mutate the caller's row
338	                # Explicit institutional-authority label — reader sees WHY the source is credible
339	                # (operator decision: reuse the T3/T4 tier BUT surface an explicit label, not a bare
340	                # badge). Rides on the row's authority record (no external consumer reads it yet —
341	                # surface it wherever that record is disclosed).
342	                new_row["authority_note"] = f"institutional authority: {band_key}"
343	                floor_tier = _INSTITUTIONAL_TIER_FLOOR.get(band_key)
344	                if floor_tier is not None:
345	                    floor_rank = _TIER_STRENGTH_RANK[floor_tier]
346	                    raised = False
347	                    # Guard EACH tier field independently against ITS OWN current rank so a stronger
348	                    # existing value is NEVER lowered. ``tier`` is the field every downstream
349	                    # claim/basket consumer reads (claim_graph, BasketMember); ``source_tier`` is
350	                    # raised too ONLY when the row already carries it.
351	                    for field in ("tier", "source_tier"):
352	                        if field == "source_tier" and field not in row:
353	                            continue
354	                        if floor_rank < _tier_rank_of(row.get(field)):
355	                            new_row[field] = floor_tier
356	                            raised = True
357	                    if raised:
358	                        new_row["tier_before_institutional_floor"] = _tier_of_row(row)  # disclosed
359	                        new_row["institutional_authority_tier_floor"] = floor_tier  # disclosed
360	
361	        out.append(new_row if new_row is not None else row)
362	    return out
363	
364	
365	def _emit_zero_authority_canary(rows: list[dict]) -> None:
366	    """B9(a) fail-loud canary: with the redesign ON, an all-zero / all-missing authority_score across
367	    rows means the prior join no-oped — a WIRING BREAK, not a legitimate zero. Always emits the LOUD
368	    warning (LAW II); the HARD raise is opt-in via ``PG_REQUIRE_NONZERO_AUTHORITY`` so this detection
369	    can never itself drop a source (the FAIL-OPEN rule)."""
370	    if not rows:
371	        return
372	    any_nonzero = any(
373	        isinstance(r.get("authority_score"), (int, float))
374	        and not isinstance(r.get("authority_score"), bool)
375	        and float(r.get("authority_score") or 0.0) > 0.0
376	        for r in rows
377	    )
378	    if any_nonzero:
379	        return
380	    import logging as _logging  # noqa: PLC0415
381	    msg = (
382	        "[credibility-pass] B9(a) WIRING-BREAK CANARY: authority_score is zero/missing across ALL "
383	        f"{len(rows)} rows with the credibility redesign ON — weight_mass will render 0.0 everywhere. "
384	        "The tier-authority prior join did not fire (check PG_CREDIBILITY_TIER_AUTHORITY_JOIN and that "
385	        "rows carry a 'tier'). This is a wiring break, not a legitimate zero."
386	    )
387	    _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(msg)
388	    if os.environ.get(_ENV_REQUIRE_NONZERO_AUTHORITY, "").strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "yes", "on"):
389	        raise CredibilityPassError(
390	            "abort_zero_authority_wiring_break: " + msg + " (PG_REQUIRE_NONZERO_AUTHORITY opt-in raise)"
391	        )
392	
393	
394	def credibility_redesign_enabled() -> bool:
395	    """The master activation slate. OFF ⇒ the runner never calls the pass ⇒ byte-identical.
396	
397	    P0-A20 (I-arch-007, 602->22 funnel): the WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE redesign (CLAUDE.md §-1.3)
398	    is now the COHERENT DEFAULT — UNSET evaluates ON, matching the run-path mirror
399	    ``run_honest_sweep_r3._cred_redesign_on`` (which already defaults ``"on"``). Pre-fix this
400	    helper (and the other four library mirrors) defaulted to the EMPTY string, which IS a member
401	    of ``_OFF_VALUES`` -> unset evaluated OFF -> the library stayed on the legacy filter-and-cap /
402	    source-DROP path while the orchestrator believed the redesign was on. That split IS the funnel.
403	    An EXPLICIT ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN=0`` (or off/false/no) still returns False -> the
404	    legacy path is preserved byte-for-byte for the regression/escape-hatch case."""
405	    return os.environ.get(_MASTER_FLAG, "on").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES

408	class CredibilityPassError(RuntimeError):
409	    """Activated pass cannot complete faithfully — fail-loud, never a silent false-green advisory."""
410

412	@dataclass
413	class EvidenceCredibility:
414	    evidence_id: str
415	    credibility_weight: float       # POST-P3: P2 weight × supersession multiplier, clamped [0,1]
416	    reliability_score: float
417	    relevance_score: float
418	    origin_cluster_id: str
419	    is_canonical_origin: bool
420	    certainty_downgrade: bool       # carried explicitly from P3 (supersession), not folded into the number
421	    soft_warning: str | None
422	    # I-arch-005 B12 (#1257): True iff THIS source's credibility judge errored (the priors-only
423	    # fallback weight was used). A DISCLOSED LABEL — the source is still scored (priors), the rest
424	    # of the corpus keeps its LLM judgments, and the WHOLE report no longer aborts on one judge
425	    # error. Defaulted False so every legacy construction + the flag-OFF path is byte-identical.
426	    credibility_unscored: bool = False
427

429	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
430	# I-arch-002 [8] / Wave-3 design §5/§6 — the per-claim ClaimBasket.
431	#
432	# A basket is one claim_cluster_id's whole group of supporting sources. Principle
433	# 2 (CONSOLIDATE, don't DROP): ``supporting_members`` keeps ALL sources, never a
434	# representative. Principle 3 (BASKET FAITHFULNESS): the verdict is decided against
435	# the WHOLE basket, but ``verified_support_origin_count`` is computed by ISOLATED
436	# per-member verification — each member verified against its OWN single span, never
437	# a multi-citation union (design §0/§5 FIX-3: a union that passes while a member
438	# fails alone must count that member UNVERIFIED). ``basket_verdict`` is a LABEL only
439	# (design §6): it may downgrade / drop / label, but NEVER resurrect a
440	# strict_verify-dropped sentence.
441	#
442	# These are ADVISORY side-outputs assembled under the master flag. strict_verify +
443	# the 4-role D8 release policy stay the ONLY binding gates — nothing here keeps or
444	# drops a sentence or flips release.
445	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
446	
447	# basket_verdict labels (design §5 — NAMED constants, no inline magic strings).
448	BASKET_VERDICT_FULL = "full"            # every supporting member independently verified on its own span
449	BASKET_VERDICT_PARTIAL = "partial"      # some but not all members verified
450	BASKET_VERDICT_CONTESTED = "contested"  # >=1 refuter edge references this cluster (user judges)
451	BASKET_VERDICT_UNVERIFIED = "unverified"  # no member verified alone
452	
453	# I-deepfix-001 F1-STRUCTURAL (#1344) — kill-switch for the basket-build chrome screen.
454	# The consolidation engine matched two pieces of page-furniture (a truncated DOI/running-header
455	# fragment and a CC-license footer) across two papers and certified them as one claim with two
456	# origins (the chrome x chrome basket behind F1 + A1-A10). When ON (default), each SUPPORTS
457	# member's claim-local span AND the cluster claim_text are screened through the SAME shared
458	# render-seam predicate ``weighted_enrichment.is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable``; a member whose
459	# span is chrome/unrenderable is EXCLUDED from ``verified_origin_ids`` (the strengthening count)
460	# but is KEPT as a basket member (never deleted) and stays in the pool + disclosure. This is
461	# faithfulness-ADJACENT (consolidation layer): it ONLY REMOVES a chrome/unrenderable span from a
462	# corroboration COUNT -- it strengthens faithfulness, can never relax a gate or inflate breadth,
463	# and never hard-drops a source. LAW VI: OFF => byte-identical legacy count.
464	_ENV_BASKET_CHROME_SCREEN = "PG_BASKET_CHROME_SCREEN"
465

469	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_BASKET_CHROME_SCREEN, "1").strip().lower() not in (
470	        "0", "false", "no", "off",

474	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
475	# F3-3b (I-deepfix-001 #1369) — corroboration entailment-judge AVAILABILITY guard.
476	#
477	# ROOT CAUSE (Fable F3-3b, PRIMARY, forensic on the drb_72 real run): the ISOLATED
478	# per-member entailment judge that must confirm a grouped member SUPPORTS its claim
479	# before it counts as a corroboration origin went UNAVAILABLE 176 times this run. A
480	# member whose entailment call errored/timed-out is durably marked
481	# ``entailment_judge_unavailable`` (see BasketMember + Wave-3 P1b) and NEVER counted, so
482	# the multi-source corroboration collapses (4/78 cited). The run used a same-family GLM
483	# entailment judge with POOR OpenRouter provider availability — the SAME class as the D8
484	# judge provider-count render-blocker (the kimi-k2.6 availability lesson).
485	#
486	# THIS BLOCK adds the three availability protections Fable specifies, WITHOUT changing
487	# what COUNTS as support (a real SUPPORTS entailment) and WITHOUT touching the frozen
488	# faithfulness engine (strict_verify / the entailment verifier / 4-role D8 / provenance):
489	#   (i)   active_entailment_judge_model() names the ACTUAL binding judge the per-member verify ran
490	#         on (``PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL``, default the entailment_judge default ``z-ai/glm-5.2``) — the
491	#         model that ACTUALLY counted the corroboration, so the Methods disclosure never makes a
492	#         wrong methods claim (LAW II / §-1.1). corroboration_judge_model() names the HIGH-PROVIDER-
493	#         COUNT open model the corroboration judge SHOULD resolve to (kimi-k2.6), surfaced in the
494	#         Methods disclosure SEPARATELY as the RECOMMENDED mitigation target. The advisory
495	#         corroboration verify shares the process-singleton entailment judge with the binding
496	#         strict_verify (verify_sentence_provenance has NO per-call model seam), so the model is
497	#         ACTUALLY selected by the run-slate ``PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL`` — a faithfulness-adjacent
498	#         wiring above a builder's authority for the frozen binding judge. These resolvers +
499	#         disclosure make BOTH the real judge and the intended target explicit and env-overridable;
500	#         neither mutates the singleton.
501	#   (ii)  bounded retries with backoff on transient/blank/429 ALREADY exist on the shared
502	#         entailment judge path (entailment_judge.py: _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_RETRIES, the U16
503	#         rate-limit floor/backoff, provider rotation). The corroboration verify inherits
504	#         them; no change is made to that frozen path.
505	#   (iii) count_judge_unavailable_members() + build_judge_availability_methods_disclosure()
506	#         SURFACE the report-level ``entailment_judge_unavailable`` member count into the
507	#         Methods disclosure (LAW II: never silently soften the corroboration count).
508	#   (iv)  _enforce_judge_availability_canary() — a FAIL-LOUD canary that always LOUD-warns
509	#         the count/fraction and HARD-RAISES ``CredibilityPassError`` when the unavailable
510	#         fraction exceeds an ENV-DRIVEN threshold (no default magic number drives the
511	#         raise; unset => warn-only, matching the proven _emit_zero_authority_canary
512	#         fail-open posture so a legacy/default run never aborts on a blip).
513	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
514	_ENV_CORROBORATION_JUDGE_MODEL = "PG_CORROBORATION_ENTAILMENT_MODEL"
515	# The high-provider-count open model the corroboration entailment judge SHOULD resolve to
516	# (the D8 availability lesson: pick a model OpenRouter serves via many providers so the
517	# per-member burst does not 429/blank-storm and LOSE verdicts to outages). kimi-k2.6 is a
518	# reasoning open model served by ~21 providers and is NOT in the glm generator family, so
519	# the two-family invariant holds for an advisory corroboration judge. LAW VI: env-overridable.
520	_DEFAULT_CORROBORATION_JUDGE_MODEL = "moonshotai/kimi-k2.6"
521	# (iv) the fail-loud canary threshold — env-driven, no default magic-number hard-fail.
522	_ENV_JUDGE_UNAVAILABLE_MAX_FRAC = "PG_CORROBORATION_JUDGE_UNAVAILABLE_MAX_FRAC"
523	# The env the LIVE per-member entailment judge ACTUALLY resolves its model from (the same
524	# ``verify_sentence_provenance`` -> ``_get_judge()`` singleton the binding strict_verify uses).
525	# The Methods disclosure names THIS model — the one that actually counted the corroboration —
526	# so the report never makes a WRONG methods claim (LAW II / §-1.1). Kept as a local literal
527	# fallback (verified against ``entailment_judge._DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL``); the real default is
528	# lazy-imported at resolve time so the two never drift.
529	_ENV_ACTIVE_ENTAILMENT_MODEL = "PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL"
530	_ENTAILMENT_JUDGE_DEFAULT_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2"
531

571	def _judge_unavailable_max_frac() -> float | None:
572	    """F3-3b(iv): the ENV-DRIVEN fail-loud threshold (a fraction in (0, 1]). UNSET / ``off`` /
573	    ``none`` / non-numeric / out-of-range => ``None`` = the canary is WARN-ONLY (never raises).
574	
575	    No default magic number drives a hard abort (per the F3 spec): the raise fires ONLY when the
576	    operator/run-slate EXPLICITLY configures a ceiling and the run exceeds it — the same
577	    fail-open-by-default posture ``_emit_zero_authority_canary`` uses so a legacy/default run is
578	    never aborted by a transient judge blip. LAW VI."""
579	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_JUDGE_UNAVAILABLE_MAX_FRAC, "").strip().lower()
580	    if raw in ("", "off", "none", "disabled", "no"):
581	        return None
582	    try:
583	        value = float(raw)
584	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
585	        import logging as _logging  # noqa: PLC0415
586	        _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
587	            "[credibility-pass] %s=%r is not a float; the judge-availability canary stays "
588	            "WARN-ONLY (no hard raise).", _ENV_JUDGE_UNAVAILABLE_MAX_FRAC, raw,
589	        )
590	        return None
591	    if not (0.0 < value <= 1.0):
592	        import logging as _logging  # noqa: PLC0415
593	        _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
594	            "[credibility-pass] %s=%s out of (0,1]; the judge-availability canary stays "
595	            "WARN-ONLY (no hard raise).", _ENV_JUDGE_UNAVAILABLE_MAX_FRAC, value,
596	        )
597	        return None
598	    return value

602	    """F3-3b(iii): return ``(judge_unavailable_member_count, total_member_count)`` across every
603	    basket's ``supporting_members``. ``judge_unavailable`` counts a member whose entailment judge
604	    was DURABLY UNAVAILABLE this run (``entailment_judge_unavailable`` — a judge_error / timeout /
605	    transport-hard-drop / deadline-skip), the ONLY signal that means a verdict was LOST to an
606	    outage (a clean NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED is a genuine gap, not counted here). Pure/read-only."""
607	    total = 0
608	    unavailable = 0

611	            total += 1
612	            if bool(getattr(member, "entailment_judge_unavailable", False)):
613	                unavailable += 1
614	    return unavailable, total

667	def _enforce_judge_availability_canary(unavailable: int, total: int) -> None:
668	    """F3-3b(iv): the FAIL-LOUD judge-availability canary.
669	
670	    ALWAYS emits a LOUD warning with the unavailable count/fraction (LAW II — the corroboration
671	    count is never silently softened). HARD-RAISES ``CredibilityPassError`` ONLY when an
672	    ENV-DRIVEN ceiling (``PG_CORROBORATION_JUDGE_UNAVAILABLE_MAX_FRAC``) is set AND the unavailable
673	    fraction exceeds it — a genuine judge-outage STORM (the drb_72 176-outage collapse) that
674	    guts corroboration must FAIL the run rather than ship a deficient report. Unset ceiling =>
675	    warn-only (fail-open, never aborts a legacy/default run). No default magic number drives the
676	    raise."""
677	    if total <= 0:
678	        return
679	    frac = unavailable / total
680	    import logging as _logging  # noqa: PLC0415
681	    _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
682	        "[credibility-pass] F3-3b judge-availability: entailment verification UNAVAILABLE for "
683	        "%d/%d basket member(s) (%.1f%%) this run — those members are disclosed "
684	        "verification-unavailable and NOT counted as corroboration origins (never silently "
685	        "softened). Set %s to a fraction to HARD-FAIL on a judge-outage storm.",
686	        unavailable, total, frac * 100.0, _ENV_JUDGE_UNAVAILABLE_MAX_FRAC,
687	    )
688	    threshold = _judge_unavailable_max_frac()
689	    if threshold is not None and frac > threshold:
690	        raise CredibilityPassError(
691	            "abort_corroboration_judge_unavailable_storm: entailment verification was unavailable "
692	            f"for {unavailable}/{total} basket member(s) ({frac:.1%}), exceeding the configured "
693	            f"ceiling {_ENV_JUDGE_UNAVAILABLE_MAX_FRAC}={threshold:.3f}. The corroboration count is "
694	            "gutted by a judge-outage storm (same class as the drb_72 176-outage collapse) — "
695	            "failing loud rather than shipping a deficient report (never victory-on-deficient). "
696	            "Point the entailment judge at a higher-provider-count open model "
697	            f"(corroboration_judge_model()={corroboration_judge_model()!r}) or raise the ceiling."
698	        )

827	def _claim_local_span(claim_text: str, row_text: str) -> str:
828	    """ISSUE #1279 P1#2 — the CLAIM-LOCAL span text for the basket member's ``direct_quote``.
829	
830	    The downstream verified-compose acceptance region (``verified_compose._member_global_span``) is
831	    recovered by LOCATING the member's ``direct_quote`` inside the global row text. If ``direct_quote``
832	    is the WHOLE row (``_row_span_text(row)``), the region becomes ``(0, len(row))`` — the entire row —
833	    and a composed sentence citing a DIFFERENT in-row claim's offsets passes the cross-claim region gate
834	    (the P1#2 hole). The claim's OWN ``text`` (the extractor's ``context_snippet`` — a verbatim substring
835	    of the row for numeric/qualitative claims) is the claim-LOCAL span, so the region is claim-specific.
836	
837	    Returns the claim-local span IFF it is a NON-EMPTY substring of ``row_text`` (so it resolves
838	    downstream); otherwise returns ``""`` (FAIL-CLOSED: the member defines NO acceptance region and NO
839	    verbatim-fallback span — never the whole row). This is strictly MORE restrictive than the full row.
840	    """
841	    local = str(claim_text or "").strip()
842	    haystack = str(row_text or "")
843	    if local and haystack and local in haystack:
844	        return local
845	    return ""

848	_ENTAILMENT_FAILURE_PREFIXES = (
849	    # I-arch-010 FIX-2 Step 0 — the COMPLETE set of failure-reason prefixes the
850	    # ENTAILMENT block of ``verify_sentence_provenance`` can append (verified against
851	    # provenance_generator.py: the NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED drops at :2076/:2182/:2216 use
852	    # ``entailment_failed:``; the legacy transport hard-drop at :2243 uses
853	    # ``entailment_judge_error_fail_closed:``). A member whose failure_reasons are ALL
854	    # in this set passed the deterministic (a)-(e) engine and only entailment is
855	    # unsatisfied/inconclusive — grounded-but-weak (DETERMINISTIC_ONLY), DISTINCT from a
856	    # member whose OWN span fails (a)-(e) (deterministic garbage, UNVERIFIED).
857	    "entailment_failed:",
858	    "entailment_judge_error_fail_closed:",
859	)

861	# I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 PART 2 ARM B P1b (#1344): the SUBSET of entailment failure prefixes that mean the
862	# judge was DURABLY UNAVAILABLE (errored / timed out / transport-hard-dropped) this run — as opposed to a
863	# CLEAN NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED verdict (``entailment_failed:``, where the judge RAN and answered). Only a
864	# judge-UNAVAILABLE member may drive the ARM-B "entailment verification was unavailable" disclosure; a
865	# clean non-entailment is a GENUINE evidence gap, not a transient outage (Codex Wave-3 P1b). Paired with
866	# the durable ``result.judge_error`` boolean (the machine-readable marker per provenance_generator.py).
867	_ENTAILMENT_JUDGE_ERROR_PREFIX = "entailment_judge_error_fail_closed:"

870	def _classify_member_tier(result: Any) -> tuple[str, str, bool]:
871	    """Map a full ``SentenceVerification`` result onto ``(span_verdict, member_tier, judge_unavailable)``.
872	
873	    ``span_verdict`` stays STRICTLY BINARY (the binding invariant
874	    ``span_verdict == "SUPPORTS"`` iff ``member_tier == ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED``).
875	    Reads ONLY ``is_verified`` / ``judge_error`` / ``failure_reasons`` — all already on
876	    the object; the deterministic (a)-(e) engine and the entailment judge verdict logic
877	    are NEVER touched (FROZEN, §-1.4).
878	
879	    I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 PART 2 ARM B P1b (#1344): ``judge_unavailable`` is the DURABLE
880	    third signal — ``True`` iff the member's entailment tier is DETERMINISTIC_ONLY BECAUSE the
881	    judge errored / timed out / transport-hard-dropped this run (``result.judge_error`` OR an
882	    ``entailment_judge_error_fail_closed:`` reason), ``False`` for a CLEAN NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED
883	    (``entailment_failed:``, judge ran) and for every non-DETERMINISTIC_ONLY tier. It NEVER
884	    changes the span_verdict / member_tier (byte-identical to the pre-P1b 2-tuple for those two);
885	    it only lets the ARM-B disclosure distinguish a transient judge OUTAGE from a genuine gap.
886	    """
887	    is_verified = bool(getattr(result, "is_verified", False))
888	    judge_error = bool(getattr(result, "judge_error", False))
889	    if is_verified and not judge_error:
890	        # genuine ENTAILED (judge ran, no error) — the ONLY counted/rendered tier.
891	        return "SUPPORTS", MEMBER_TIER_ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED, False
892	    if is_verified and judge_error:
893	        # FIX-1 transport-keep: passed (a)-(e), entailment INCONCLUSIVE (judge errored).
894	        # NOT genuinely entailed → must NOT count/render as verified support (closes the
895	        # judge_error leak). Grounded-but-weak: an I-arch-011 keep-with-label candidate.
896	        # P1b: judge_error=True → the judge was UNAVAILABLE (durable outage signal).
897	        return "UNSUPPORTED", MEMBER_TIER_DETERMINISTIC_ONLY, True
898	    # is_verified == False below.
899	    failure_reasons = list(getattr(result, "failure_reasons", None) or [])
900	    if failure_reasons and all(
901	        any(str(r).startswith(p) for p in _ENTAILMENT_FAILURE_PREFIXES)
902	        for r in failure_reasons
903	    ):
904	        # deterministic (a)-(e) PASS but entailment NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED (or any
905	        # entailment failure when FIX-1 is off) — grounded-but-weak, same tier as the
906	        # row above for I-arch-011's purpose (it cleared the deterministic engine).
907	        # P1b: judge-UNAVAILABLE only when the DURABLE outage signal is present — the durable
908	        # ``judge_error`` boolean OR an ``entailment_judge_error_fail_closed:`` reason. A CLEAN
909	        # NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED (only ``entailment_failed:`` reasons, judge ran) is judge_unavailable
910	        # == False → a genuine gap, never disclosed as "verification unavailable".
911	        judge_unavailable = judge_error or any(
912	            str(r).startswith(_ENTAILMENT_JUDGE_ERROR_PREFIX) for r in failure_reasons
913	        )
914	        return "UNSUPPORTED", MEMBER_TIER_DETERMINISTIC_ONLY, judge_unavailable
915	    # the member's OWN span genuinely lacks the claim's number / content-overlap (or no
916	    # failure reasons at all) — deterministic garbage; NEVER surfaced.
917	    return "UNSUPPORTED", MEMBER_TIER_UNVERIFIED, False

925	) -> tuple[str, str, bool]:
926	    """Verify ONE member against ITS OWN single span — never a union (design §5 FIX-3).
927	
928	    Builds a single-provenance-token sentence (``<claim_text> [#ev:<eid>:0-<len>]``)
929	    so ``verify_sentence_provenance`` has EXACTLY ONE token. The per-token union loop
930	    inside the verifier (which aggregates decimals/text across MULTIPLE tokens) is the
931	    laundering path; one token means no union, so a member whose own span lacks the
932	    claim's number/content fails ALONE — even if a multi-citation union would pass.
933	
934	    Returns the 3-tuple ``(span_verdict, member_tier, judge_unavailable)`` (I-arch-010 FIX-2
935	    Step 0 + I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 P1b). ``span_verdict`` is ``"SUPPORTS"`` iff the isolated
936	    sentence is genuinely entailment-verified, else ``"UNSUPPORTED"``; ``member_tier`` is the
937	    additive 3-value classification; ``judge_unavailable`` is the DURABLE judge-outage signal
938	    (see ``_classify_member_tier``). The verifier is INJECTED (production
939	    ``verify_sentence_provenance`` by default; a deterministic fake in tests) and is NEVER
940	    re-run as a gate — this is advisory.
941	    """
942	    eid = str((member_row or {}).get("evidence_id") or "")
943	    span = _row_span_text(member_row)
944	    if not eid or not span:
945	        # No evidence to verify against — the safest non-counted, never-surfaced tier.
946	        return "UNSUPPORTED", MEMBER_TIER_UNVERIFIED, False
947	    # Defensive single-token guarantee (the anti-laundering invariant, design §5
948	    # FIX-3): strip ANY stray provenance / calc token already in the claim text so
949	    # the appended one is the ONLY token. With exactly one token the verifier's
950	    # per-token union loop cannot aggregate this member's span with any other — a
951	    # member whose own span lacks the claim fails ALONE.
952	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
953	        _CALC_TOKEN_RE,
954	        _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE,
955	    )
956	    safe_text = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub(" ", str(claim_text or ""))
957	    safe_text = _CALC_TOKEN_RE.sub(" ", safe_text).strip()
958	    # ONE token spanning the member's whole own span.
959	    sentence = f"{safe_text} [#ev:{eid}:0-{len(span)}]"
960	    pool = {eid: dict(member_row)}
961	    try:
962	        result = verify_fn(sentence, pool)
963	    except Exception:
964	        # Advisory path: a verifier failure on one member is conservatively the safest
965	        # tier (never resurrects the member, never aborts the basket) — fail-closed for
966	        # the strengthening count, which can only ever UNDERCOUNT, never inflate. A verifier
967	        # crash is NOT a judge outage (judge_unavailable=False → a genuine gap, never disclosed
968	        # as "verification unavailable").
969	        return "UNSUPPORTED", MEMBER_TIER_UNVERIFIED, False
970	    return _classify_member_tier(result)

975	# by direction — "UNSUPPORTED" can only UNDERCOUNT corroboration (the member never joins a SUPPORTS
976	# basket, never surfaces, never inflates breadth); ``judge_unavailable=True`` is the existing durable
977	# verification-unavailable label (Wave-3 P1b) so the skip is DISCLOSED, never silent. The members
978	# verified BEFORE the deadline keep their genuine ENFORCE-entailment verdicts and are BANKED — the
979	# frozen faithfulness engine (verify_fn) is called-not-edited and never re-run as a gate.

983	def _run_member_verifies(
984	    tasks: list[tuple[str, dict]],
985	    *,
986	    verify_fn: Callable,
987	    max_inflight: int,
988	    deadline_monotonic: float | None = None,
989	) -> list[tuple[str, str, bool]]:
990	    """Run the per-member isolated verifies for ``tasks`` and return their verdicts IN ORDER.
991	
992	    Each verdict is the 3-tuple ``(span_verdict, member_tier, judge_unavailable)`` from
993	    ``_verify_member_in_isolation`` (I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 P1b). The tuple is opaque here —
994	    it is passed through unchanged, so the serial and bounded-parallel paths stay
995	    verdict-identical.
996	
997	    ``tasks`` is the FLAT, deterministically-ordered list of ``(claim_text, member_row)`` pairs
998	    (built in the ORIGINAL ``sorted(clusters)`` → member order). The returned verdict list is
999	    index-aligned with ``tasks`` (``results[i]`` is the verdict for ``tasks[i]``), so the caller
1000	    reassembles baskets in EXACTLY the serial order — the parallel path is verdict-identical to the
1001	    serial path, only faster.
1002	
1003	    ``max_inflight == 1`` (the default) takes the SERIAL path verbatim — byte-identical to the
1004	    pre-1b inline loop. For ``max_inflight >= 2`` a BOUNDED ``ThreadPoolExecutor`` caps concurrency at
1005	    exactly ``max_inflight``; each worker runs inside a COPIED ``contextvars.copy_context()`` so:
1006	      * the run-scoped JUDGE TELEMETRY ContextVar (FX-09, a MUTABLE dict) is the SAME object in the
1007	        copy, so the judge's in-place ``calls/judge_error`` ticks land in the parent's counter (mirrors
1008	        ``provenance_generator._verify_in_context``);
1009	      * the run-scoped COST ContextVar (a float — rebinds don't propagate across a copy) is isolated to
1010	        zero in the worker and its per-task delta is re-added to the parent counter + the budget
1011	        re-checked (mirrors ``credibility_skill.score_source_credibility``), so the parallel advisory
1012	        verifies never silently lose the run's cost accounting (Codex P2).
1013	    A worker exception PROPAGATES out (fail-loud) exactly as the serial loop's ``verify_fn`` would —
1014	    the existing ``_verify_member_in_isolation`` already maps a verifier failure to ``UNSUPPORTED``
1015	    internally, so only a programming/budget defect ever escapes here.
1016	
1017	    This is ADVISORY — ``_verify_member_in_isolation`` is never re-run as a gate, and parallelism
1018	    changes WALL-CLOCK only, never any verdict.
1019	
1020	    ``deadline_monotonic`` (I-deepfix-001 BANK-BEFORE-WALL): an OPTIONAL ``time.monotonic()``
1021	    deadline. When it expires mid-pass, the verdicts computed SO FAR are BANKED verbatim and every
1022	    not-yet-verified member gets ``_DEADLINE_SKIP_VERDICT`` (disclosed verification-unavailable —
1023	    an UNDERCOUNT-only degrade, mirroring ``credibility_skill``'s proven priors-fill wall pattern).
1024	    The drb_72 box1 defect this fixes: this loop had NO deadline, so the CALLER's all-or-nothing
1025	    ``asyncio.wait_for`` wall discarded the ENTIRE analysis (baskets included) on a rich corpus and
1026	    the "Corroborated Weighted Findings" breadth layer silently vanished from report.md (§-1.3
1027	    funnel reassertion; breadth-enrichment canary rc=1). ``None`` (every legacy caller/test) =>
1028	    byte-identical unbounded behavior.
1029	    """
1030	    n = len(tasks)
1031	    if max_inflight <= 1 or n <= 1:
1032	        # SERIAL fast path (default / single task): byte-identical to the pre-1b inline loop when no
1033	        # deadline is threaded; with a deadline, bank the verified prefix + sentinel-fill the rest.
1034	        serial_results: list[tuple[str, str, bool]] = []
1035	        for _i, (claim_text, member_row) in enumerate(tasks):
1036	            if deadline_monotonic is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline_monotonic:
1037	                import logging as _logging  # noqa: PLC0415
1038	                _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
1039	                    "[credibility-pass] BANK-BEFORE-WALL: member-verify soft deadline expired at "
1040	                    "%d/%d (serial); BANKING the %d verified verdict(s) and filling the remaining %d "
1041	                    "with the disclosed verification-unavailable sentinel (UNDERCOUNT-only; the "
1042	                    "banked SUPPORTS members still surface the breadth layer — never discarded).",
1043	                    _i, n, _i, n - _i,
1044	                )
1045	                serial_results.extend([_DEADLINE_SKIP_VERDICT] * (n - _i))
1046	                break
1047	            serial_results.append(
1048	                _verify_member_in_isolation(claim_text, member_row, verify_fn=verify_fn)
1049	            )
1050	        return serial_results
1051	
1052	    import concurrent.futures  # noqa: PLC0415 (lazy: zero cost on the serial default path)
1053	    import contextvars  # noqa: PLC0415
1054	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1055	        _add_run_cost,
1056	        check_run_budget,
1057	        current_run_cost,
1058	        reset_run_cost,
1059	    )
1060	
1061	    def _verify_one(
1062	        idx: int, claim_text: str, member_row: dict, ctx: contextvars.Context
1063	    ) -> tuple[int, tuple[str, str, bool], float]:
1064	        def _run() -> tuple[tuple[str, str, bool], float]:
1065	            reset_run_cost()  # isolate THIS member's spend in the copied context (parent re-adds a clean delta)
1066	            verdict = _verify_member_in_isolation(claim_text, member_row, verify_fn=verify_fn)
1067	            return verdict, current_run_cost()
1068	        verdict, delta = ctx.run(_run)
1069	        return idx, verdict, delta
1070	
1071	    results: list[tuple[str, str, bool] | None] = [None] * n
1072	    pool = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_inflight)
1073	    deadline_expired = False
1074	    try:
1075	        futures = [
1076	            pool.submit(_verify_one, i, claim_text, member_row, contextvars.copy_context())
1077	            for i, (claim_text, member_row) in enumerate(tasks)
1078	        ]
1079	        # I-deepfix-001 BANK-BEFORE-WALL: bound the join by the remaining soft-deadline budget (None =>
1080	        # unbounded, byte-identical). Mirrors credibility_skill._judge_rows_pooled's PROVEN
1081	        # as_completed(timeout=...)-then-drain wall pattern — bank what finished, never discard.
1082	        _join_timeout: float | None = None
1083	        if deadline_monotonic is not None:
1084	            _join_timeout = max(0.001, deadline_monotonic - time.monotonic())
1085	        try:
1086	            for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures, timeout=_join_timeout):
1087	                idx, verdict, delta = future.result()  # re-raises BudgetExceededError / worker exc (fail closed)
1088	                results[idx] = verdict
1089	                _add_run_cost(delta)   # thread the per-member spend into the single run counter (no lost ticks)
1090	                check_run_budget(0)    # raises BudgetExceededError -> bounded overspend (~max_inflight in flight)
1091	        except concurrent.futures.TimeoutError:
1092	            # The soft deadline fired before every member verified. Drain any future that DID finish
1093	            # (a real verdict / a real BudgetExceededError is never lost), then sentinel-fill the rest
1094	            # below. The banked SUPPORTS verdicts keep the corroboration/breadth layer ALIVE — the
1095	            # all-or-nothing discard (the drb_72 box1 rc=1 root cause) is structurally gone.
1096	            deadline_expired = True
1097	            for _i, future in enumerate(futures):
1098	                if results[_i] is not None or not future.done():
1099	                    continue
1100	                try:
1101	                    idx, verdict, delta = future.result()
1102	                except BudgetExceededError:
1103	                    raise  # a real cap breach MUST still abort the sweep, even on the deadline path
1104	                except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a genuinely-failed worker takes the sentinel below
1105	                    continue
1106	                results[idx] = verdict
1107	                _add_run_cost(delta)
1108	                check_run_budget(0)  # enforce the aggregate cap on the drain path too (fail-closed)
1109	    except BaseException:
1110	        pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
1111	        raise
1112	    else:
1113	        # Deadline path: don't block on still-running verifies (wait=False) and cancel the queued
1114	        # ones; the healthy path joins normally (wait=True). Orphaned worker cost ticks are recovered
1115	        # by the caller's process-global ledger reconcile (multi_section_generator's finally block).
1116	        pool.shutdown(wait=not deadline_expired, cancel_futures=deadline_expired)
1117	    if deadline_expired:
1118	        _banked = sum(1 for v in results if v is not None)
1119	        import logging as _logging  # noqa: PLC0415
1120	        _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
1121	            "[credibility-pass] BANK-BEFORE-WALL: member-verify soft deadline expired with %d/%d "
1122	            "verified; BANKING the verified verdicts and filling the remaining %d with the disclosed "
1123	            "verification-unavailable sentinel (UNDERCOUNT-only; banked SUPPORTS members still "
1124	            "surface the breadth layer — the analysis is never discarded).",
1125	            _banked, n, n - _banked,
1126	        )
1127	        return [v if v is not None else _DEADLINE_SKIP_VERDICT for v in results]
1128	    for i, verdict in enumerate(results):
1129	        if verdict is None:
1130	            raise CredibilityPassError(
1131	                f"abort_credibility_pass_error: basket member index {i} produced no verdict from the "
1132	                f"compute pool (fail-closed — a dropped future must never silently undercount)."
1133	            )
1134	    return [v for v in results if v is not None]

1152	def _regroup_graph_by_finding_dedup(

1154	    annotated: list[dict],
1155	    *,
1156	    gov_suffixes: tuple,
1157	    domain: str | None,
1158	) -> Any:
1159	    """HOP-A KEYSTONE (#1265): MERGE claim_graph's existing claim partition using

1161	    ONE basket. Returns a NEW graph-shaped view (same ``claims`` objects, MERGED ``clusters``,
1162	    REMAPPED ``edges``); the input ``graph`` is never mutated.
1163	
1164	    FAITHFULNESS — grouping + relabel ONLY (the HARD constraint):
1165	      * The ``claims`` list (and every ``AtomicClaim.text`` / span the isolated verify reads) is
1166	        UNCHANGED — passed through by reference. So the set of members that pass
1167	        ``_verify_member_in_isolation`` downstream is byte-identical to the legacy grouping: same
1168	        claim text, same span, same verify_fn. No member newly passes any gate.
1169	      * Clusters are MERGED, never split and never dropped: the rebuilt partition covers EXACTLY the
1170	        same claim indices as the input (a partition refinement in reverse — union only). A
1171	        qualitative / raw / non-clinical claim that finding_dedup does not cluster keeps its OWN
1172	        legacy singleton id (finding_dedup only groups finding-bearing rows; everything else is left
1173	        exactly as claim_graph clustered it).
1174	      * ``verified_support_origin_count`` rises ONLY because distinct ALREADY-verified origins now
1175	        share a basket — never because acceptance was relaxed.
1176	      * EDGES are remapped to the merged ids so a CONTESTED basket (a refuter edge references it)
1177	        still renders CONTESTED — relabeling cluster ids without remapping edges would SILENTLY hide
1178	        a contradiction (clinical-lethal). Each edge's ``claim_cluster_ids`` is rewritten through the
1179	        same old->new map.
1180	
1181	    ``finding_dedup`` is on the LIVE run path already (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:8079); this consumes its
1182	    PURE grouping (no network, no LLM) with the SAME ``gov_suffixes`` + ``domain`` the rest of the

1184	    imports credibility_pass inside its own function, so a function-local import here avoids the cycle.

1186	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.finding_dedup import dedup_by_finding  # noqa: PLC0415
1187	
1188	    claims = list(getattr(graph, "claims", None) or [])
1189	    if not claims:

1191	        return graph
1192	
1193	    # claim_graph emits AT MOST ONE numeric AtomicClaim per evidence_id (extract_numeric_claims emits

1195	    # index so a finding cluster's member rows resolve to the right atom to union.
1196	    #
1197	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1344) KEYSTONE gap: finding_dedup ALSO forms QUALITATIVE baskets

1243	    #    new id — the residual old basket holding j SILENTLY lost its contested state (a contradiction

1245	    #    first makes a whole cluster move ATOMICALLY: no existing cluster is ever split.
1246	    for _existing_members in (getattr(graph, "clusters", None) or {}).values():

1285	    rep_id_by_root: dict[int, str] = {}

1295	        new_id = rep_id_by_root[_find(idx)]

1299	        # the cluster_id_by_evidence binding from new_id, and _assemble_baskets reads members from the
1300	        # clusters dict + the binding — never re-reads claim.claim_cluster_id for the basket id. But to

1332	def _assemble_baskets(
1333	    graph: Any,
1334	    weight_mass: list,
1335	    annotated: list[dict],
1336	    credibility_by_evidence: dict,
1337	    *,
1338	    verify_fn: Callable,
1339	    max_inflight: int = _DEFAULT_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT,
1340	    deadline_monotonic: float | None = None,

1342	    """Assemble one ClaimBasket per claim cluster (design §5/§6).
1343	
1344	    Principle 2: ALL members of a cluster are kept (``supporting_members`` is the full
1345	    group, never a representative). Principle 3: ``verified_support_origin_count`` is the
1346	    count of DISTINCT origin clusters whose member passed ISOLATED per-member verification
1347	    (NOT the raw passing-member count, and NOT ``weight_mass.independent_origin_count`` —
1348	    that is the clustered, not-verified count, surfaced ONLY as the ADVISORY
1349	    ``total_clustered_origin_count``). ``basket_verdict`` is a pure LABEL.
1350	
1351	    I-arch-007 ITEM 1b (#1264): the per-member isolated verifies (the expensive entailment-judge
1352	    calls) are gathered into a FLAT, deterministically-ordered task list, dispatched through a BOUNDED
1353	    pool (``max_inflight``), and reassembled in the ORIGINAL ``sorted(clusters)`` → member order. The
1354	    verdict each member receives is unchanged from the serial path — only wall-clock differs.
1355	    """
1356	    row_by_eid = {str(r.get("evidence_id", "")): r for r in (annotated or [])}
1357	    wm_by_cluster = {
1358	        str(getattr(w, "claim_cluster_id", "") or ""): w for w in (weight_mass or [])
1359	    }
1360	
1361	    # Refuter references (design §5): for each cluster, the OTHER cluster ids it is
1362	    # joined to by a ContradictionEdge. REFERENCE only — never duplicated into the basket.
1363	    refuters_by_cluster: dict[str, set] = {}
1364	    for edge in (getattr(graph, "edges", None) or []):
1365	        ids = [str(c) for c in (getattr(edge, "claim_cluster_ids", ()) or ()) if str(c)]
1366	        uniq = set(ids)
1367	        if len(uniq) == 1:
1368	            # I-deepfix-001 P1#1 (keystone contested-edge, FAITHFULNESS-CRITICAL): a CONTRADICTION
1369	            # edge (every ``graph.edges`` entry is a ContradictionEdge) whose two endpoints resolve to
1370	            # the SAME cluster id collapses to a SELF-LOOP (e.g. ``('clm_a',)``). This happens either
1371	            # natively (``_edge_cluster_pair`` returns a 1-element tuple when both contradicting rows
1372	            # sit in ONE cluster) OR after ``_regroup_graph_by_finding_dedup`` merges the two contested
1373	            # clusters into one. The ``other != cid`` loop below recorded NO refuter for such an edge,
1374	            # so the basket fell through to full/partial/unverified and STOPPED rendering ``contested``
1375	            # — a contradiction silently hidden by the merge (violates hard constraint 3). An
1376	            # intra-basket contradiction is the STRONGEST contested signal and is NEVER dropped: record
1377	            # the cluster as its OWN refuter so ``basket_verdict`` stays ``contested`` and every
1378	            # downstream consumer that keys on ``refuter_cluster_ids`` (the disclosure contested-count,
1379	            # the consensus-quantifier guard) stays consistent. Holds for numeric AND qualitative merges.
1380	            (self_cid,) = tuple(uniq)

1382	            continue
1383	        for cid in ids:
1384	            for other in ids:
1385	                if other != cid:
1386	                    refuters_by_cluster.setdefault(cid, set()).add(other)
1387

1389	    claims = getattr(graph, "claims", None) or []
1390

1393	    # skipped exactly as the serial loop does, so the index alignment is identical to a serial pass.

1395	    verify_tasks: list[tuple[str, dict]] = []
1396	    for cluster_id in sorted_cluster_ids:
1397	        for claim in [claims[i] for i in clusters[cluster_id]]:
1398	            eid = str(getattr(claim, "evidence_id", "") or "")
1399	            row = row_by_eid.get(eid, {})
1400	            verify_tasks.append((str(getattr(claim, "text", "") or ""), row))
1401

1470	                            is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable as _is_render_chrome,

1492	                    # reconstruction. The member is KEPT in the basket (§-1.3 no-drop); only its chrome
1493	                    # span is held out of the corroboration count + the downstream cross-source set.
1494	                    import logging as _logging  # noqa: PLC0415

1564	def run_credibility_analysis(
1565	    research_question: str,
1566	    rows: list[dict],
1567	    *,
1568	    gov_suffixes: tuple,
1569	    domain: str | None = None,
1570	    judge: Callable | None = None,
1571	    now_year: int | None = None,
1572	    max_inflight: int | None = None,
1573	    deadline_monotonic: float | None = None,
1574	) -> CredibilityAnalysis:
1575	    """Run the P4→P3→P2→P5→P6 chain over the EFFECTIVE evidence pool.
1576	
1577	    Fail-loud on a MISSING evidence_id, a P4 independence-annotation gap, or a wired-module crash (real
1578	    integrity holes). The two INFRA conditions that used to abort the WHOLE report no longer hold it
1579	    (I-arch-005 B12-P1, operator-locked 2026-06-14 "nothing shall hold the report"): a PER-SOURCE P2
1580	    ``judge_error`` and a MISSING production judge (``judge=None``) each LABEL the affected sources
1581	    ``credibility_unscored`` (priors-only weight — a real, honest deterministic weight, never fabricated)
1582	    and the rest keep scoring, so the report ships with the disclosed gap. ``rows`` MUST already be the
1583	    generator's effective pool (post-M-52, post-dissent); ``gov_suffixes`` is the PSL gov-suffix tuple the
1584	    rest of the pipeline uses (dependency-injected, no global). ``judge`` is the production credibility
1585	    judge (injected); None ⇒ the whole pool ships priors-only + LABELED ``credibility_unscored`` (a
1586	    disclosed gap surfaced LOUD per LAW II), NOT a silent false-green and NOT a hold.
1587	
1588	    ``max_inflight`` (I-arch-007 ITEM 1b, #1264) bounds the per-member isolated-verify concurrency in
1589	    ``_assemble_baskets`` (LAW VI). ``None`` (the default) reads the ``PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT``
1590	    env knob, which itself defaults to 1 = the byte-identical SERIAL path. The bound changes WALL-CLOCK
1591	    only — the per-member verdicts and the resulting baskets are identical to the serial pass.
1592	
1593	    ``deadline_monotonic`` (I-deepfix-001 BANK-BEFORE-WALL): an OPTIONAL ``time.monotonic()`` soft
1594	    deadline the caller sets INSIDE its own hard wall. The chain budgets it across the two LLM-bound
1595	    phases (P2 source scoring / per-member basket verifies) so the pass RETURNS a real
1596	    ``CredibilityAnalysis`` — with every verdict computed so far BANKED and the rest disclosed
1597	    verification-unavailable — BEFORE the caller's all-or-nothing wall can discard the whole analysis.
1598	    ``None`` (every legacy caller) => byte-identical unbounded behavior.
1599	    """
1600	    if max_inflight is None:
1601	        max_inflight = _pass_max_inflight()
1602	    if not rows:
1603	        return CredibilityAnalysis({}, {}, [], [], [])
1604	    if judge is None:
1605	        # I-arch-005 B12-P1 (#1257, operator-locked 2026-06-14 "VERIFY = LABEL, NEVER HOLD"): a missing
1606	        # production credibility judge is an INFRA/config condition (the pass is ADVISORY — strict_verify
1607	        # + the 4-role D8 release policy stay the ONLY binding gates), NOT a faithfulness finding. It must
1608	        # NOT abort the whole report. The chain runs priors-only (every source carries its real
1609	        # deterministic authority weight, never fabricated) and LABELS every source credibility_unscored —
1610	        # a disclosed gap. LOUD log (LAW II: no silent downgrade) so the operator sees it.
1611	        import logging as _logging  # noqa: PLC0415
1612	        _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
1613	            "[credibility-pass] no production credibility judge wired (judge=None); running priors-only "
1614	            "and LABELING all %d source(s) credibility_unscored (disclosed gap) — the report ships with "
1615	            "the gap, never aborts (operator-locked 'nothing shall hold the report').",
1616	            len(rows),
1617	        )
1618	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import BudgetExceededError
1619	    try:
1620	        return _run_chain(
1621	            research_question, rows,
1622	            gov_suffixes=gov_suffixes, domain=domain, judge=judge, now_year=now_year,
1623	            max_inflight=max_inflight, deadline_monotonic=deadline_monotonic,
1624	        )
1625	    except (CredibilityPassError, BudgetExceededError):
1626	        # CredibilityPassError = fail-loud abort; BudgetExceededError (Codex #012a P1-2) must reach the
1627	        # sweep's budget-abort path cleanly, NOT be masked as a generic credibility-pass error.
1628	        raise
1629	    except Exception as exc:  # ANY OTHER wired-module failure → fail-loud abort, never a silent false-green
1630	        raise CredibilityPassError(
1631	            f"abort_credibility_pass_error: a wired credibility module failed "
1632	            f"({type(exc).__name__}): {exc}"
1633	        ) from exc
