1636	def _run_chain(
1637	    research_question: str,
1638	    rows: list[dict],
1639	    *,
1640	    gov_suffixes: tuple,
1641	    domain: str | None,
1642	    judge: Callable | None,
1643	    now_year: int | None,
1644	    max_inflight: int = _DEFAULT_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT,
1645	    deadline_monotonic: float | None = None,
1646	) -> CredibilityAnalysis:
1647	    """The P4→P3→P2→P5→P6 chain body; wrapped by run_credibility_analysis for the fail-loud posture."""
1648	    # ── I-deepfix-001 B9(a): join a deterministic tier-authority prior onto any row missing an
1649	    # authority_score, on COPIED rows, BEFORE collapse so independence_collapse's undated-canonical
1650	    # selection AND weight_mass.cluster_mass read the SAME non-zero authority. A row that already
1651	    # carries a real computed authority_score is preserved verbatim. WEIGHT, never a DROP (§-1.3); the
1652	    # faithfulness engine is untouched. Then the fail-loud canary: an all-zero authority_score across
1653	    # rows (with the redesign ON) is a wiring break, surfaced LOUD (raise only on opt-in). ──
1654	    rows = _join_tier_authority_prior(rows)
1655	    _emit_zero_authority_canary(rows)
1656	    # ── P4: independent-origin collapse → per-row assignment, on COPIED rows (never mutate the caller) ──
1657	    collapse = collapse_independent_origins(rows, gov_suffixes=gov_suffixes)
1658	    if len(collapse.assignments) != len(rows):
1659	        raise CredibilityPassError(
1660	            "abort_independence_annotation_gap: P4 returned "
1661	            f"{len(collapse.assignments)} assignments for {len(rows)} rows"
1662	        )
1663	    annotated: list[dict] = []
1664	    origin_by_evidence: dict = {}
1665	    for i, row in enumerate(rows):
1666	        eid = _require_evidence_id(row, i)
1667	        assignment = collapse.assignments[i]
1668	        new_row = dict(row)  # COPY
1669	        new_row["origin_cluster_id"] = assignment.origin_cluster_id
1670	        new_row["is_canonical_origin"] = assignment.is_canonical_origin
1671	        annotated.append(new_row)
1672	        origin_by_evidence[eid] = assignment.origin_cluster_id
1673	
1674	    # ── P3: supersession multiplier per source ──
1675	    supers_by_evidence = {
1676	        _require_evidence_id(row, i): supersession_adjustment(row, now_year=now_year)
1677	        for i, row in enumerate(annotated)
1678	    }
1679	
1680	    # ── P2: credibility judgments — judge_error / judge=None LABEL the source, never abort ──
1681	    # I-arch-005 B12 (#1257, operator-locked 2026-06-14 "VERIFY = LABEL, NEVER HOLD"): the pass
1682	    # is ADVISORY (its own docstring). The two INFRA conditions must NOT abort the WHOLE report —
1683	    # each LABELS the affected source(s) ``credibility_unscored`` (a disclosed gap) and KEEPS the
1684	    # rest scoring with real weights:
1685	    #   * a PER-SOURCE judge_error — ``score_source_credibility`` already isolates the error to ONE
1686	    #     row and falls back to that source's DETERMINISTIC priors (a real, honest weight, never
1687	    #     fabricated), flagging it ``judge_error=True``;
1688	    #   * judge=None (no production judge wired) — ``score_source_credibility(judge=None)`` returns
1689	    #     priors-only for EVERY row but with ``judge_error=False`` (it is not a per-source error, it
1690	    #     is a global infra condition). So the ``errored_ids`` set is EMPTY in that case; we OR in
1691	    #     ``judge_missing`` below so EVERY source is correctly labeled ``credibility_unscored`` (NOT a
1692	    #     silent priors-only false-green — that EXACT trap is why the explicit OR exists).
1693	    # The credibility WEIGHT math is unchanged — a labeled row simply carries its priors-only weight +
1694	    # the disclosed label. (The genuine provenance hole — a CITED evidence_id with NO credibility row
1695	    # at all — is still fail-loud, caught downstream by ``apply_disclosure_to_svs``'s coverage
1696	    # assertion; that is a real coverage gap, NOT a recoverable infra condition.)
1697	    judge_missing = judge is None
1698	    # I-deepfix-001 BANK-BEFORE-WALL: budget phase A (P2 source scoring) to a FRACTION of the
1699	    # remaining soft-deadline budget so phase B (the per-member basket verifies that feed the
1700	    # SUPPORTS baskets / breadth enrichment) is GUARANTEED a share. score_source_credibility takes
1701	    # min(env pool wall, this budget) and already partial-banks at its wall (drain + priors-fill,
1702	    # never a discard). None deadline (legacy) => None budget => env-only, byte-identical.
1703	    _phase_a_wall_s: float | None = None
1704	    if deadline_monotonic is not None:
1705	        _remaining_s = deadline_monotonic - time.monotonic()
1706	        _phase_a_wall_s = max(_MIN_PHASE_BUDGET_S, _remaining_s * _phase_a_budget_frac())
1707	    judgments = score_source_credibility(
1708	        research_question, annotated, domain=domain, judge=judge,
1709	        pool_wall_s=_phase_a_wall_s,
1710	    )
1711	    errored_ids = {j.evidence_id for j in judgments if getattr(j, "judge_error", False)}
1712	    if errored_ids:
1713	        example = sorted(errored_ids)[:5]
1714	        # LOUD log (LAW II: no silent downgrade) — the run still ships, but the operator sees it.
1715	        import logging as _logging  # noqa: PLC0415
1716	        _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
1717	            "[credibility-pass] credibility judge errored for %d/%d source(s) (e.g. %s); "
1718	            "LABELING them credibility_unscored (priors-only weight) and continuing to score the "
1719	            "rest — the report ships with the disclosed gap (never aborts the basket).",
1720	            len(errored_ids), len(judgments), example,
1721	        )
1722	
1723	    # ── POST-P3 credibility = P2 weight × supersession multiplier (certainty carried, not folded away) ──
1724	    credibility_by_evidence: dict = {}
1725	    for row, judgment in zip(annotated, judgments):
1726	        eid = judgment.evidence_id
1727	        supersession = supers_by_evidence.get(eid)
1728	        multiplier = supersession.multiplier if supersession else 1.0
1729	        credibility_by_evidence[eid] = EvidenceCredibility(
1730	            evidence_id=eid,
1731	            credibility_weight=_clamp01(judgment.credibility_weight * multiplier),
1732	            reliability_score=judgment.reliability_score,
1733	            relevance_score=judgment.relevance_score,
1734	            origin_cluster_id=origin_by_evidence.get(eid, ""),
1735	            is_canonical_origin=bool(row.get("is_canonical_origin")),
1736	            certainty_downgrade=bool(supersession.certainty_downgrade) if supersession else False,
1737	            soft_warning=(supersession.soft_warning if supersession else None),
1738	            # OR in ``judge_missing``: with judge=None EVERY source is priors-only with
1739	            # judge_error=False, so ``errored_ids`` is empty — without the OR every source would ship
1740	            # credibility_unscored=False (a silent priors-only false-green, the EXACT trap B12-P1 fixes).
1741	            credibility_unscored=(eid in errored_ids) or judge_missing,
1742	        )
1743	
1744	    # POST-P3 judgments for downstream — P6 disclosure must use the post-P3 credibility, not raw P2.
1745	    adjusted_judgments = [
1746	        dataclasses.replace(j, credibility_weight=credibility_by_evidence[j.evidence_id].credibility_weight)
1747	        for j in judgments
1748	    ]
1749	
1750	    # ── P5: claim graph (atomic claims + contradiction edges) ──
1751	    graph = build_claim_graph(annotated, domain=domain)
1752	
1753	    # ── I-arch-008 HOP-A KEYSTONE (#1265): consume finding_dedup's already-computed grouping ──
1754	    # DEFAULT OFF => byte-identical (the legacy claim_graph fragmentation stands). ON => MERGE the
1755	    # claim partition using finding_dedup membership so same-finding members share ONE basket, BEFORE
1756	    # weight-mass aggregation so the mass keys agree with the merged ids. Grouping + relabel + edge
1757	    # remap ONLY — the claims/spans the isolated verify reads are untouched, so no member newly passes.
1758	    if basket_consume_finding_dedup_enabled():
1759	        graph = _regroup_graph_by_finding_dedup(
1760	            graph, annotated, gov_suffixes=gov_suffixes, domain=domain,
1761	        )
1762	
1763	    # ── P6: origin-cluster weight-mass (mass = authority(canonical) ONLY; credibility disclosed) ──
1764	    weight_mass = aggregate_weight_mass(graph.claims, annotated, adjusted_judgments)
1765	
1766	    # ── I-arch-002 [8] — assemble per-claim baskets with ISOLATED per-member verification.
1767	    # The verifier is the PRODUCTION single-sentence callable, lazy-imported here so this
1768	    # module's import graph stays decoupled from the big provenance module (mirrors the
1769	    # local imports at run_credibility_analysis / apply_disclosure_to_svs). It is used
1770	    # ADVISORY only — strict_verify itself is never re-run as a gate.
1771	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1772	        verify_sentence_provenance,
1773	    )
1774	    # I-arch-011 (#1268): the per-member basket verify runs under entailment-ENFORCE (the
1775	    # PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT default). It MUST stay enforce: ``span_verdict==SUPPORTS`` is
1776	    # consumed at render WITHOUT re-verification — both as inline corroborator citations
1777	    # (provenance_generator.py B6/B8 ``_verified_corroborators_for_tokens``) and as the breadth
1778	    # enrichment section — so an entailment-OFF advisory verdict would ship un-entailed corroborator
1779	    # citations (the rejected F2b; Codex P0-1). The serial-entailment HANG is avoided NOT by
1780	    # disabling the judge but by the architecture's bounded parallelism + wall: ``max_inflight``
1781	    # (PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT, slate=16) runs the verify 16-way over a PER-THREAD judge
1782	    # client (I-arch-007 ITEM 2a, entailment_judge.py threading.local — deadlock-safe), and the
1783	    # caller's PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_WALL_S=3000 wall bounds the whole pass. Faithfulness STRENGTHENED.
1784	    baskets = _assemble_baskets(
1785	        graph, weight_mass, annotated, credibility_by_evidence,
1786	        verify_fn=verify_sentence_provenance,
1787	        max_inflight=max_inflight,
1788	        # I-deepfix-001 BANK-BEFORE-WALL: phase B gets the WHOLE remaining budget (phase A was
1789	        # capped to its fraction above). At expiry the verified prefix is BANKED and the rest is
1790	        # sentinel-filled (disclosed, UNDERCOUNT-only) — the analysis is returned, never discarded.
1791	        deadline_monotonic=deadline_monotonic,
1792	    )
1793	
1794	    # ── F3-3b (I-deepfix-001 #1369): corroboration entailment-judge AVAILABILITY guard ──
1795	    # Count the members whose entailment judge was DURABLY UNAVAILABLE this run (the outage
1796	    # signal, not a clean NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED), SURFACE it into the Methods disclosure (iii),
1797	    # and run the FAIL-LOUD canary (iv) — which always LOUD-warns and HARD-RAISES only when the
1798	    # ENV-DRIVEN ceiling is set and exceeded. This runs BEFORE the return so a judge-outage storm
1799	    # that guts corroboration FAILS the run rather than shipping a deficient CredibilityAnalysis.
1800	    _judge_unavailable, _basket_members = count_judge_unavailable_members(baskets)
1801	    _methods_disclosure = build_judge_availability_methods_disclosure(
1802	        _judge_unavailable, _basket_members,
1803	        # Name the ACTUAL binding judge (PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL) that counted the corroboration —
1804	        # never the recommended target (that would be a wrong methods claim, LAW II). kimi-k2.6 is
1805	        # surfaced SEPARATELY inside the builder as the recommended higher-provider mitigation.
1806	        judge_model=active_entailment_judge_model(),
1807	        recommended_model=corroboration_judge_model(),
1808	    )
1809	    _enforce_judge_availability_canary(_judge_unavailable, _basket_members)
1810	
1811	    # ── sentence -> claim_cluster_id binding (design §6): evidence_id -> the cluster
1812	    #    id(s) its atomic claim(s) belong to. The resolve sites today carry only cited
1813	    #    tokens (each an evidence_id), so this lets the render layer (P5.x) map a cited
1814	    #    token to the basket whose verified count it should surface. Reference data only.
1815	    cluster_id_by_evidence: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
1816	    for claim in (graph.claims or []):
1817	        eid = str(getattr(claim, "evidence_id", "") or "")
1818	        ccid = str(getattr(claim, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
1819	        if not eid or not ccid:
1820	            continue
1821	        bucket = cluster_id_by_evidence.setdefault(eid, [])
1822	        if ccid not in bucket:
1823	            bucket.append(ccid)
1824	
1825	    return CredibilityAnalysis(
1826	        credibility_by_evidence=credibility_by_evidence,
1827	        origin_by_evidence=origin_by_evidence,
1828	        claims=graph.claims,
1829	        edges=graph.edges,
1830	        weight_mass=weight_mass,
1831	        baskets=baskets,
1832	        cluster_id_by_evidence=cluster_id_by_evidence,
1833	        entailment_judge_unavailable_member_count=_judge_unavailable,
1834	        basket_member_count=_basket_members,
1835	        methods_disclosure=_methods_disclosure,
1836	    )
1837	
1838	
1839	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1840	# I-cred-008b (#1162) — the SHARED per-claim disclosure populate+carrier+coverage
1841	# helper, called at ALL FOUR cited-prose resolve sites (legacy _run_section,
1842	# fact-dedup re-resolve, V30 contract runner, quantified-analysis). ONE copy of
1843	# this faithfulness-critical logic so it cannot drift across the four sites.
1844	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1845	
1846	def _cited_evidence_ids_for_coverage(sv: Any) -> list[str]:
1847	    """The cited evidence_ids on a RESOLVER-EMITTED SentenceVerification (its tokens)."""
1848	    out: list[str] = []
1849	    for token in (getattr(sv, "tokens", None) or []):
1850	        eid = str(getattr(token, "evidence_id", "") or "")
1851	        if eid:
1852	            out.append(eid)
1853	    return out
1854	
1855	
1856	def apply_disclosure_to_svs(svs: list, analysis: "CredibilityAnalysis") -> list:
1857	    """Populate the four advisory disclosure fields on each resolver-emitted SV, then carry the
1858	    P3 certainty downgrade — ONE shared implementation for all four cited-prose resolve sites.
1859	
1860	    Steps (ADVISORY only — never re-runs strict_verify, never flips ``is_verified``):
1861	      1. COVERAGE ASSERTION (fail-LOUD): every cited token's evidence_id on these SVs MUST have
1862	         credibility + origin coverage in ``analysis`` (both maps are co-built per-row in
1863	         ``_run_chain``). A cited token with none ⇒ ``CredibilityPassError(abort_credibility_coverage_gap)``.
1864	         Scoped to RESOLVER-EMITTED cited SVs (the SVs handed to ``resolve_provenance_to_citations``),
1865	         NOT every ``[N]`` marker in deterministic tables/timelines — those never become SVs at the
1866	         resolve sites, so they are excluded for free (Codex I-cred-012 iter-5 P2-3).
1867	      2. POPULATE: the EvidenceCredibility→float adaptation
1868	         (``{eid: ec.credibility_weight}``) feeds ``populate_disclosure`` (which expects FLOAT weights,
1869	         not the EvidenceCredibility object), populating span_verdict / credibility_weight /
1870	         independent_origin_count / certainty_label.
1871	      3. P3 CERTAINTY CARRIER (Codex I-cred-012 iter-5 P2): ``populate_disclosure`` derives certainty
1872	         from credibility/origins ONLY; it does NOT see the P3 supersession downgrade. So for each
1873	         populated SV whose cited evidence carries ``certainty_downgrade=True``, cap its certainty_label
1874	         (never above "moderate") and surface the source's ``soft_warning`` on the SV's ``soft_warnings``.
1875	
1876	    Inputs are NOT mutated; ``populate_disclosure`` returns NEW SVs via ``dataclasses.replace``.
1877	    """
1878	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.disclosure_population import populate_disclosure
1879	
1880	    cred_by_ev = analysis.credibility_by_evidence or {}
1881	    origin_by_ev = analysis.origin_by_evidence or {}
1882	
1883	    # ── Step 1: coverage assertion (fail-loud BEFORE populate) ──
1884	    for sv in (svs or []):
1885	        for eid in _cited_evidence_ids_for_coverage(sv):
1886	            if eid not in cred_by_ev or eid not in origin_by_ev:
1887	                raise CredibilityPassError(
1888	                    "abort_credibility_coverage_gap: a cited evidence_id "
1889	                    f"({eid!r}) emitted by the resolver has no credibility/origin coverage "
1890	                    "in the credibility analysis; refusing to disclose a claim whose source "
1891	                    "the activated pass never scored (fail-loud, never a false-green advisory)"
1892	                )
1893	
1894	    # ── Step 2: EvidenceCredibility → FLOAT adaptation, then populate ──
1895	    cred_floats = {
1896	        eid: ec.credibility_weight for eid, ec in cred_by_ev.items()
1897	    }
1898	    # I-arch-002 [10] / design §5 FIX-4 (Reading A) — Codex Slice-B P1: thread the
1899	    # per-claim baskets + the evidence_id->claim_cluster_id binding (both built by
1900	    # _run_chain on this CredibilityAnalysis) into populate_disclosure so the
1901	    # OVERWRITE of independent_origin_count -> verified_support_origin_count actually
1902	    # reaches the operator-visible claim_disclosure.json emit
1903	    # (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:353 / quantified_analysis.py:539 both read this field
1904	    # off kept_sentences_pre_resolve). Pre-fix apply_disclosure_to_svs omitted these,
1905	    # so the clustered (not-verified) count still leaked to the JSON. OFF byte-identity
1906	    # is structural: this whole function only runs when credibility_analysis is not
1907	    # None (itself gated on PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN at every call site); when the
1908	    # flag is OFF baskets is empty / the binding is empty, so populate_disclosure's
1909	    # _surfaced_verified_count returns None and the legacy clustered count is preserved.
1910	    populated = populate_disclosure(
1911	        svs, cred_floats, origin_by_ev,
1912	        baskets=getattr(analysis, "baskets", None),
1913	        cluster_id_by_evidence=getattr(analysis, "cluster_id_by_evidence", None),
1914	    )
1915	
1916	    # ── Step 3: P3 certainty carrier (downgrade + soft_warning surface) ──
1917	    out: list = []
1918	    for sv in populated:
1919	        downgrade = False
1920	        warnings: list[str] = []
1921	        for eid in _cited_evidence_ids_for_coverage(sv):
1922	            ec = cred_by_ev.get(eid)
1923	            if ec is None:
1924	                continue
1925	            if bool(getattr(ec, "certainty_downgrade", False)):
1926	                downgrade = True
1927	                warn = getattr(ec, "soft_warning", None)
1928	                if warn:
1929	                    warnings.append(str(warn))
1930	        if not downgrade:
1931	            out.append(sv)
1932	            continue
1933	        # Cap certainty at "moderate" (never "high") when any cited source was P3-downgraded.
1934	        new_label = sv.certainty_label
1935	        if new_label == "high":
1936	            new_label = "moderate"
1937	        existing_warnings = list(getattr(sv, "soft_warnings", None) or [])
1938	        for w in warnings:
1939	            if w not in existing_warnings:
1940	                existing_warnings.append(w)
1941	        out.append(dataclasses.replace(
1942	            sv,
1943	            certainty_label=new_label,
1944	            soft_warnings=existing_warnings,
1945	        ))
1946	    return out
1947

content.text: This file is thorough and professional throughout — no unprofessional names. Now the remaining files.

content.content:
1	"""
2	MoST Phase R: Cross-Section Self-Reflection (Hydrogen Bond).
3	
4	Implements the self-reflection bond from "The Molecular Structure of Thought"
5	(arXiv 2601.06002). Each section reflects on other sections to detect
6	contradictions, redundancy, and missed cross-references.
7	
8	Cost: ~1 LLM call per section (~15 calls, ~$0.008, ~2-3 min at concurrency=3)
9	"""
10	
11	import asyncio
12	import json
13	import logging
14	import os
15	import re
16	from typing import Any
17	
18	from src.polaris_graph.schemas import SectionDraft
19	
20	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
21	
22	
23	async def reflect_across_sections(
24	    client,
25	    sections: list[SectionDraft],
26	    evidence: list[dict],
27	    query: str,
28	    concurrency: int = 3,
29	    bond_analysis: dict | None = None,
30	) -> list[SectionDraft]:
31	    """Run cross-section reflection on all sections.
32	
33	    M-12: Enhanced with structured bond analysis input. When bond_analysis
34	    is provided, feeds covalent/ionic/disulfide/peptide findings into
35	    the revision prompt for targeted, evidence-based edits.
36	
37	    Guard: If revised section word count < 80% or > 130% of original,
38	    keep original (prevents CASE_2 regression or data loss).
39	
40	    Args:
41	        client: OpenRouter LLM client.
42	        sections: All SectionDraft objects.
43	        evidence: Verified evidence list.
44	        query: Original research query.
45	        concurrency: Max parallel reflection calls.
46	        bond_analysis: Optional dict with covalent/ionic/disulfide/peptide results.
47	
48	    Returns:
49	        Updated list of SectionDraft objects (some may be revised).
50	    """
51	    if len(sections) < 2:
52	        logger.info("[MoST-R] Only %d section(s), skipping reflection", len(sections))
53	        return sections
54	
55	    max_context = int(os.getenv("PG_REFLECTION_MAX_CONTEXT", "4"))
56	
57	    # Build evidence map: section_id -> list of evidence_ids cited
58	    section_evidence_map = {}
59	    for sec in sections:
60	        cited = set(re.findall(r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]', getattr(sec, 'content', '')))
61	        section_evidence_map[getattr(sec, 'section_id', '')] = cited
62	
63	    # Build index map: section_id -> position in list (for adjacency)
64	    section_index_map = {}
65	    for i, sec in enumerate(sections):
66	        section_index_map[getattr(sec, 'section_id', '')] = i
67	
68	    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)
69	    results = list(sections)  # Shallow copy
70	
71	    async def _reflect_one(idx: int, sec: SectionDraft) -> tuple[int, SectionDraft | None]:
72	        async with semaphore:
73	            try:
74	                context = _build_reflection_context(
75	                    target=sec,
76	                    target_index=idx,
77	                    all_sections=sections,
78	                    section_evidence_map=section_evidence_map,
79	                    section_index_map=section_index_map,
80	                    max_context=max_context,
81	                )
82	                if not context:
83	                    return idx, None
84	
85	                # M-12: Build structural analysis context from bond analysis
86	                structural_context = _build_bond_context(
87	                    bond_analysis, getattr(sec, 'section_id', '')
88	                )
89	
90	                prompt = (
91	                    "You are a research report editor performing cross-section quality review.\n\n"
92	                    f"RESEARCH QUESTION: {query}\n\n"
93	                    f"TARGET SECTION: {getattr(sec, 'title', '')}\n"
94	                    f"{getattr(sec, 'content', '')[:3000]}\n\n"
95	                    f"RELATED SECTIONS:\n{context}\n\n"
96	                )
97	                if structural_context:
98	                    prompt += f"STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS (from automated bond verification):\n{structural_context}\n\n"
99	                prompt += (
100	                    "Analyze the target section against the related sections"
101	                    + (" and structural analysis" if structural_context else "") + ":\n"
102	                    "1. CONTRADICTIONS: Claims in target that conflict with related sections\n"
103	                    "2. REDUNDANCIES: Statistics/findings already covered in related sections\n"
104	                    "3. CROSS_REFERENCES: Connections that should be made explicit\n\n"
105	                    "Output JSON:\n"
106	                    '{"contradictions": [{"claim": "...", "conflicts_with": "...", "resolution": "..."}], '
107	                    '"redundancies": [{"claim": "...", "already_in": "..."}], '
108	                    '"cross_references": [{"from_claim": "...", "relates_to_section": "...", "connection": "..."}], '
109	                    '"revision_needed": true/false}'
110	                )
111	
112	                resp = await client.generate(
113	                    prompt, max_tokens=int(os.getenv("PG_REFLECTOR_MAX_TOKENS", "8192")),
114	                )
115	                content = getattr(resp, 'content', '') or ''
116	
117	                # Parse JSON from response
118	                reflection = _parse_reflection_json(content)
119	                if not reflection or not reflection.get("revision_needed", False):
120	                    return idx, None
121	
122	                contradictions = _detect_contradictions(reflection)
123	                if not contradictions and not reflection.get("redundancies") and not reflection.get("cross_references"):
124	                    return idx, None
125	
126	                # Revise the section (M-13: targeted revision with bond context)
127	                revised = await _revise_with_reflection(
128	                    client, sec, reflection, evidence, bond_analysis,
129	                )
130	                if revised is None:
131	                    return idx, None
132	
133	                # M-03: CASE_2 guard: reject if revised text is <80% or >130% of original
134	                orig_words = len(getattr(sec, 'content', '').split())
135	                revised_words = len(getattr(revised, 'content', '').split())
136	                if orig_words > 0 and (
137	                    revised_words < orig_words * 0.8
138	                    or revised_words > orig_words * 1.3
139	                ):
140	                    logger.warning(
141	                        "[MoST-R] CASE_2 guard: section '%s' revision out of bounds "
142	                        "(%d -> %d words, %.0f%%), keeping original",
143	                        getattr(sec, 'title', '')[:40],
144	                        orig_words, revised_words,
145	                        (revised_words / orig_words) * 100,
146	                    )
147	                    return idx, None
148	
149	                logger.info(
150	                    "[MoST-R] Revised section '%s': %d contradictions, %d redundancies, %d cross-refs",
151	                    getattr(sec, 'title', '')[:40],
152	                    len(reflection.get("contradictions", [])),
153	                    len(reflection.get("redundancies", [])),
154	                    len(reflection.get("cross_references", [])),
155	                )
156	                return idx, revised
157	
158	            except Exception as exc:
159	                logger.warning(
160	                    "[MoST-R] Reflection failed for section '%s': %s",
161	                    getattr(sec, 'title', '')[:40], str(exc)[:200],
162	                )
163	                return idx, None
164	
165	    tasks = [_reflect_one(i, sec) for i, sec in enumerate(sections)]
166	    completed = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
167	
168	    revised_count = 0
169	    for result in completed:
170	        if isinstance(result, Exception):
171	            continue
172	        idx, revised_sec = result
173	        if revised_sec is not None:
174	            results[idx] = revised_sec
175	            revised_count += 1
176	
177	    logger.info("[MoST-R] Reflection complete: %d/%d sections revised", revised_count, len(sections))
178	    return results
179	
180	
181	def _build_reflection_context(
182	    target: SectionDraft,
183	    target_index: int,
184	    all_sections: list[SectionDraft],
185	    section_evidence_map: dict[str, set],
186	    section_index_map: dict[str, int],
187	    max_context: int = 4,
188	) -> str:
189	    """Select most relevant other sections as reflection context.
190	
191	    Relevance criteria:
192	    1. Evidence overlap (Jaccard similarity on evidence_id sets)
193	    2. Adjacency (immediately before/after in report order)
194	
195	    Args:
196	        target: The section being reflected on.
197	        target_index: Position of target in the sections list.
198	        all_sections: All sections in the report.
199	        section_evidence_map: Map of section_id -> set of cited evidence IDs.
200	        section_index_map: Map of section_id -> list index position.
201	        max_context: Maximum context sections to include.
202	
203	    Returns:
204	        Formatted string with related section titles + first 500 chars of content.
205	    """
206	    target_id = getattr(target, 'section_id', '')
207	    target_evidence = section_evidence_map.get(target_id, set())
208	
209	    # Score other sections
210	    scored = []
211	    for sec in all_sections:
212	        sid = getattr(sec, 'section_id', '')
213	        if sid == target_id:
214	            continue
215	
216	        score = 0.0
217	        sec_evidence = section_evidence_map.get(sid, set())
218	
219	        # Jaccard similarity on evidence
220	        if target_evidence and sec_evidence:
221	            intersection = len(target_evidence & sec_evidence)
222	            union = len(target_evidence | sec_evidence)
223	            if union > 0:
224	                score += (intersection / union) * 2.0  # Weight evidence overlap heavily
225	
226	        # Adjacency bonus (use list index position, not schema order field)
227	        sec_index = section_index_map.get(sid, -1)
228	        if sec_index >= 0 and abs(sec_index - target_index) <= 1:
229	            score += 1.0  # Adjacent sections get bonus
230	
231	        scored.append((sec, score))
232	
233	    # Sort by score descending, take top max_context
234	    scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
235	    selected = scored[:max_context]
236	
237	    if not selected:
238	        return ""
239	
240	    parts = []
241	    for sec, score in selected:
242	        title = getattr(sec, 'title', 'Untitled')
243	        content = getattr(sec, 'content', '')[:500]
244	        parts.append(f"### {title}\n{content}\n")
245	
246	    return "\n".join(parts)
247	
248	
249	def _detect_contradictions(reflection: dict) -> list[dict]:
250	    """Parse contradictions from reflection LLM output.
251	
252	    Args:
253	        reflection: Parsed JSON dict from LLM.
254	
255	    Returns:
256	        List of contradiction dicts with keys: claim, conflicts_with, resolution.
257	    """
258	    contradictions = reflection.get("contradictions", [])
259	    if not isinstance(contradictions, list):
260	        return []
261	    return [
262	        c for c in contradictions
263	        if isinstance(c, dict) and c.get("claim")
264	    ]
265	
266	
267	def _parse_reflection_json(content: str) -> dict | None:
268	    """Extract and parse JSON from LLM response content."""
269	    if not content:
270	        return None
271	    # Try direct parse
272	    try:
273	        return json.loads(content)
274	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
275	        pass
276	    # Try extracting from code fence
277	    match = re.search(r'```(?:json)?\s*(\{.*?\})\s*```', content, re.DOTALL)
278	    if match:
279	        try:
280	            return json.loads(match.group(1))
281	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
282	            pass
283	    # Try finding first { ... }
284	    match = re.search(r'\{.*\}', content, re.DOTALL)
285	    if match:
286	        try:
287	            return json.loads(match.group(0))
288	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
289	            pass
290	    return None
291	
292	
293	async def _revise_with_reflection(
294	    client,
295	    section: SectionDraft,
296	    reflection: dict,
297	    evidence: list[dict],
298	    bond_analysis: dict | None = None,
299	) -> SectionDraft | None:
300	    """M-13: Revise a single section based on reflection + bond findings.
301	
302	    Uses targeted edit instructions from structural bond analysis
303	    plus LLM reflection to make precise, evidence-based edits.
304	
305	    Args:
306	        client: OpenRouter LLM client.
307	        section: The section to revise.
308	        reflection: Parsed reflection results.
309	        evidence: Available evidence for citations.
310	        bond_analysis: Optional bond analysis results for targeted edits.
311	
312	    Returns:
313	        Revised SectionDraft, or None if revision fails.
314	    """
315	    contradictions = reflection.get("contradictions", [])
316	    redundancies = reflection.get("redundancies", [])
317	    cross_refs = reflection.get("cross_references", [])
318	
319	    instructions = []
320	    if contradictions:
321	        for c in contradictions[:3]:
322	            instructions.append(
323	                f"- RESOLVE contradiction: '{c.get('claim', '')[:100]}' "
324	                f"conflicts with '{c.get('conflicts_with', '')[:100]}'. "
325	                f"Resolution: {c.get('resolution', 'align with strongest evidence')[:100]}"
326	            )
327	    if redundancies:
328	        for r in redundancies[:3]:
329	            instructions.append(
330	                f"- REMOVE redundancy: '{r.get('claim', '')[:100]}' "
331	                f"already covered in '{r.get('already_in', '')[:50]}'"
332	            )
333	    if cross_refs:
334	        for cr in cross_refs[:3]:
335	            instructions.append(
336	                f"- ADD cross-reference: '{cr.get('from_claim', '')[:100]}' "
337	                f"relates to section '{cr.get('relates_to_section', '')[:50]}'"
338	            )
339	
340	    if not instructions:
341	        return None
342	
343	    prompt = (
344	        "Revise the following research section based on cross-section reflection.\n\n"
345	        f"SECTION TITLE: {getattr(section, 'title', '')}\n"
346	        f"CURRENT CONTENT:\n{getattr(section, 'content', '')}\n\n"
347	        "REVISION INSTRUCTIONS:\n"
348	        + "\n".join(instructions)
349	        + "\n\nIMPORTANT RULES:\n"
350	        "- Keep ALL existing [CITE:ev_xxx] markers\n"
351	        "- Do NOT remove content that is not explicitly redundant\n"
352	        "- Do NOT add new claims without [CITE:evidence_id] markers\n"
353	        "- CRITICAL: Preserve ALL specific numbers, dollar amounts, percentages, and quantities exactly as written\n"
354	        "- Do NOT paraphrase or omit any numerical data points\n"
355	        "- Do NOT remove sentences that contain specific dollar amounts or statistics\n"
356	        "- Maintain the same academic tone and depth\n"
357	        "- Output ONLY the revised section content (no title, no metadata)\n"
358	    )
359	
360	    try:
361	        resp = await client.generate(
362	            prompt, max_tokens=int(os.getenv("PG_REDUNDANCY_REWRITE_MAX_TOKENS", "8192")),
363	        )
364	        revised_content = getattr(resp, 'content', '') or ''
365	        if not revised_content or len(revised_content) < 50:
366	            return None
367	
368	        # M-01: Sync evidence_ids from actual CITE markers in revised content
369	        from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.section_utils import sync_evidence_ids_from_content
370	        return sync_evidence_ids_from_content(SectionDraft(
371	            section_id=getattr(section, 'section_id', ''),
372	            title=getattr(section, 'title', ''),
373	            content=revised_content,
374	            claims_made=getattr(section, 'claims_made', []),
375	            evidence_ids=getattr(section, 'evidence_ids', []),
376	        ))
377	    except Exception as exc:
378	        logger.warning(
379	            "[MoST-R] Revision LLM call failed for '%s': %s",
380	            getattr(section, 'title', '')[:40], str(exc)[:200],
381	        )
382	        return None
383	
384	
385	def _build_bond_context(
386	    bond_analysis: dict | None,
387	    section_id: str,
388	) -> str:
389	    """M-12: Build structural analysis context from bond results.
390	
391	    Formats findings from all 4 bond types for injection into
392	    the Phase R reflection prompt.
393	
394	    Args:
395	        bond_analysis: Dict with covalent/ionic/disulfide/peptide results.
396	        section_id: The section being revised.
397	
398	    Returns:
399	        Formatted structural analysis string, or empty string.
400	    """
401	    if not bond_analysis:
402	        return ""
403	
404	    parts = []
405	
406	    # Covalent findings
407	    covalent = bond_analysis.get("covalent", {})
408	    if covalent:
409	        weak = [b for b in covalent.get("weak_bonds", []) if b.get("section_id") == section_id]
410	        broken = [b for b in covalent.get("broken_bonds", []) if b.get("section_id") == section_id]
411	        if weak or broken:
412	            parts.append("CLAIM-EVIDENCE BINDING:")
413	            for b in broken[:3]:
414	                parts.append(
415	                    f"  - BROKEN: \"{b['sentence'][:60]}\" cites {b['evidence_id']} "
416	                    f"(similarity={b['similarity']} — likely misattributed)"
417	                )
418	            for b in weak[:3]:
419	                parts.append(
420	                    f"  - WEAK: \"{b['sentence'][:60]}\" cites {b['evidence_id']} "
421	                    f"(similarity={b['similarity']})"
422	                )
423	
424	    # Ionic findings
425	    ionic = bond_analysis.get("ionic", {})
426	    if ionic:
427	        try:
428	            from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.evidence_section_affinity import (
429	                format_ionic_findings_for_phase_r,
430	            )
431	            ionic_text = format_ionic_findings_for_phase_r(ionic, section_id)
432	            if ionic_text and "No evidence" not in ionic_text:
433	                parts.append(f"EVIDENCE PLACEMENT:\n{ionic_text}")
434	        except ImportError:
435	            pass
436	
437	    # Disulfide findings
438	    disulfide = bond_analysis.get("disulfide", {})
439	    if disulfide:
440	        try:
441	            from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.cross_section_source_consistency import (
442	                format_disulfide_findings_for_phase_r,
443	            )
444	            disulfide_text = format_disulfide_findings_for_phase_r(disulfide, section_id)
445	            if disulfide_text and "No cross-section" not in disulfide_text:
446	                parts.append(f"SOURCE CONSISTENCY:\n{disulfide_text}")
447	        except ImportError:
448	            pass
449	
450	    # Peptide findings
451	    peptide = bond_analysis.get("peptide", {})
452	    if peptide:
453	        try:
454	            from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.narrative_flow_analyzer import (
455	                format_peptide_findings_for_phase_r,
456	            )
457	            peptide_text = format_peptide_findings_for_phase_r(peptide, section_id)
458	            if peptide_text and "No narrative" not in peptide_text:
459	                parts.append(f"NARRATIVE FLOW:\n{peptide_text}")
460	        except ImportError:
461	            pass
462	
463	    return "\n\n".join(parts)
464

content.text: This file uses a chemistry-bond metaphor theme ("covalent", "ionic", "disulfide", "peptide", "hydrogen bond") consistently as a genuine architectural naming scheme for the "Molecular Structure of Thought" paper reference. These are used consistently as domain terms tied to an actual paper citation, not marketing fluff — but they're unusual/cryptic for an external review. Let me note this pattern (it recurs across other files too — evidence_section_affinity.py has `format_ionic_findings_for_phase_r`, cross_section_source_consistency.py has disulfide). This is a systemic naming choice. Given the strict bar, I'll flag it as KEEP-NOTED since it's a real, consistently-implemented conceptual metaphor tied to a cited paper (not slang/joke), but note it as unusual. Let's continue reading remaining files first.

content.content:
1	"""
2	MoST Disulfide Bond: Cross-Section Source Consistency.
3	
4	Same source cited in multiple sections must not contradict itself.
5	Builds a source-section citation graph and verifies structural consistency.
6	Zero LLM cost (pure embedding computation).
7	
8	Cost: 0 LLM calls, ~2-3 seconds.
9	"""
10	
11	import logging
12	import os
13	import re
14	from collections import defaultdict
15	
16	import numpy as np
17	
18	from src.polaris_graph.schemas import SectionDraft
19	from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	# Thresholds (LAW VI)
24	_CONTRADICTION_THRESHOLD = float(os.getenv("PG_DISULFIDE_CONTRADICTION_THRESHOLD", "0.30"))
25	_REDUNDANCY_THRESHOLD = float(os.getenv("PG_DISULFIDE_REDUNDANCY_THRESHOLD", "0.85"))
26	
27	
28	def analyze_disulfide_bridges(
29	    sections: list[SectionDraft],
30	    evidence: list[dict],
31	) -> dict:
32	    """Run disulfide bridge analysis: check same-source cross-section consistency.
33	
34	    Builds source-section citation graph. For sources cited in 2+ sections,
35	    extracts claims from each section and checks for contradictions and
36	    verbatim redundancies.
37	
38	    Args:
39	        sections: All SectionDraft objects.
40	        evidence: Full verified evidence pool.
41	
42	    Returns:
43	        Dict with keys: consistent_bridges, contradictions, redundancies, stats.
44	    """
45	    if not sections or not evidence:
46	        return _empty_result()
47	
48	    # Build evidence lookup: evidence_id -> evidence dict
49	    ev_lookup = {
50	        e.get("evidence_id", ""): e
51	        for e in evidence
52	        if e.get("evidence_id")
53	    }
54	
55	    # Build source-section citation graph
56	    # source_url -> {section_id -> [(evidence_id, statement_in_context)]}
57	    source_sections: dict[str, dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]]] = defaultdict(
58	        lambda: defaultdict(list)
59	    )
60	
61	    for sec in sections:
62	        sid = getattr(sec, "section_id", "")
63	        content = getattr(sec, "content", "")
64	        cited_ids = set(re.findall(r"\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]", content))
65	
66	        for eid in cited_ids:
67	            ev = ev_lookup.get(eid)
68	            if not ev:
69	                continue
70	            source_url = ev.get("source_url", "")
71	            if not source_url:
72	                continue
73	            statement = ev.get("statement", "")
74	            source_sections[source_url][sid].append((eid, statement))
75	
76	    # Filter to sources cited in 2+ sections
77	    multi_section_sources = {
78	        url: sections_map
79	        for url, sections_map in source_sections.items()
80	        if len(sections_map) >= 2
81	    }
82	
83	    if not multi_section_sources:
84	        return _empty_result()
85	
86	    contradictions: list[dict] = []
87	    redundancies: list[dict] = []
88	    consistent_bridges: list[dict] = []
89	
90	    for source_url, sections_map in multi_section_sources.items():
91	        # Collect all (section_id, evidence_id, statement) tuples
92	        all_claims: list[tuple[str, str, str]] = []
93	        for sid, items in sections_map.items():
94	            for eid, stmt in items:
95	                if stmt:
96	                    all_claims.append((sid, eid, stmt))
97	
98	        if len(all_claims) < 2:
99	            continue
100	
101	        # Embed all claims from this source
102	        claim_texts = [c[2][:200] for c in all_claims]
103	        try:
104	            vecs = np.array(embed_texts(claim_texts))
105	            sim = vecs @ vecs.T
106	        except Exception as exc:
107	            logger.debug(
108	                "[MoST-Disulfide] Embedding failed for source '%s': %s",
109	                source_url[:60],
110	                str(exc)[:100],
111	            )
112	            continue
113	
114	        # Compare pairwise across different sections
115	        found_issue = False
116	        for i in range(len(all_claims)):
117	            for j in range(i + 1, len(all_claims)):
118	                sid_i, eid_i, stmt_i = all_claims[i]
119	                sid_j, eid_j, stmt_j = all_claims[j]
120	
121	                # Only compare across different sections
122	                if sid_i == sid_j:
123	                    continue
124	
125	                pair_sim = float(sim[i][j])
126	
127	                if pair_sim >= _REDUNDANCY_THRESHOLD:
128	                    redundancies.append({
129	                        "source_url": source_url[:100],
130	                        "section_a": sid_i,
131	                        "section_b": sid_j,
132	                        "evidence_a": eid_i,
133	                        "evidence_b": eid_j,
134	                        "claim_a": stmt_i[:120],
135	                        "claim_b": stmt_j[:120],
136	                        "similarity": round(pair_sim, 3),
137	                    })
138	                    found_issue = True
139	                elif pair_sim < _CONTRADICTION_THRESHOLD:
140	                    contradictions.append({
141	                        "source_url": source_url[:100],
142	                        "section_a": sid_i,
143	                        "section_b": sid_j,
144	                        "evidence_a": eid_i,
145	                        "evidence_b": eid_j,
146	                        "claim_a": stmt_i[:120],
147	                        "claim_b": stmt_j[:120],
148	                        "similarity": round(pair_sim, 3),
149	                    })
150	                    found_issue = True
151	
152	        if not found_issue:
153	            consistent_bridges.append({
154	                "source_url": source_url[:100],
155	                "sections": list(sections_map.keys()),
156	                "claim_count": len(all_claims),
157	            })
158	
159	    stats = {
160	        "multi_section_sources": len(multi_section_sources),
161	        "contradictions": len(contradictions),
162	        "redundancies": len(redundancies),
163	        "consistent_bridges": len(consistent_bridges),
164	    }
165	
166	    logger.info(
167	        "[MoST-Disulfide] Bridge analysis: %d multi-section sources, "
168	        "%d contradictions, %d redundancies, %d consistent",
169	        stats["multi_section_sources"],
170	        stats["contradictions"],
171	        stats["redundancies"],
172	        stats["consistent_bridges"],
173	    )
174	
175	    return {
176	        "consistent_bridges": consistent_bridges,
177	        "contradictions": contradictions,
178	        "redundancies": redundancies,
179	        "stats": stats,
180	    }
181	
182	
183	def format_disulfide_findings_for_phase_r(
184	    disulfide_result: dict,
185	    target_section_id: str,
186	) -> str:
187	    """Format disulfide findings as context for Phase R prompt.
188	
189	    Args:
190	        disulfide_result: Output of analyze_disulfide_bridges().
191	        target_section_id: The section being revised.
192	
193	    Returns:
194	        Formatted string for Phase R prompt injection.
195	    """
196	    contradictions = disulfide_result.get("contradictions", [])
197	    redundancies = disulfide_result.get("redundancies", [])
198	
199	    relevant_contradictions = [
200	        c for c in contradictions
201	        if c["section_a"] == target_section_id or c["section_b"] == target_section_id
202	    ]
203	    relevant_redundancies = [
204	        r for r in redundancies
205	        if r["section_a"] == target_section_id or r["section_b"] == target_section_id
206	    ]
207	
208	    parts = []
209	    if relevant_contradictions:
210	        parts.append("Same-source CONTRADICTIONS (must resolve):")
211	        for c in relevant_contradictions[:3]:
212	            other = c["section_b"] if c["section_a"] == target_section_id else c["section_a"]
213	            parts.append(
214	                f"  - Source: {c['source_url'][:60]}"
215	            )
216	            parts.append(f"    This section: \"{c['claim_a'][:80]}\"")
217	            parts.append(f"    Section {other}: \"{c['claim_b'][:80]}\"")
218	            parts.append(f"    Similarity: {c['similarity']} (LOW = likely contradiction)")
219	
220	    if relevant_redundancies:
221	        parts.append("Same-source REDUNDANCIES (consolidate):")
222	        for r in relevant_redundancies[:3]:
223	            other = r["section_b"] if r["section_a"] == target_section_id else r["section_a"]
224	            parts.append(
225	                f"  - \"{r['claim_a'][:80]}\" repeated in section {other}"
226	            )
227	
228	    return "\n".join(parts) if parts else "No cross-section source issues detected."
229	
230	
231	def _empty_result() -> dict:
232	    """Return empty disulfide result."""
233	    return {
234	        "consistent_bridges": [],
235	        "contradictions": [],
236	        "redundancies": [],
237	        "stats": {
238	            "multi_section_sources": 0,
239	            "contradictions": 0,
240	            "redundancies": 0,
241	            "consistent_bridges": 0,
242	        },
243	    }
244

content.text: Same "MoST" chemistry-bond metaphor theme, consistently implemented (disulfide bridge = same-source cross-section consistency check). This is a real conceptual scheme tied to an actual paper ("Molecular Structure of Thought"), consistently and functionally implemented. Still, for an EXTERNAL client review, whimsical chemistry-metaphor names (`analyze_disulfide_bridges`, `format_disulfide_findings_for_phase_r`) read as unprofessional/cryptic. I'll flag the module-level naming pattern as RENAME given the strict bar, while keeping straightforward names. Let's continue.

1	"""I-cred-008 (Phase 8, L7) — per-claim disclosure POPULATION (pure module).
2	
3	Populate the four inert Phase-1 disclosure fields on each post-``strict_verify`` ``SentenceVerification``
4	from the already-computed upstream signals — WITHOUT re-running or touching the verifier:
5	  * ``span_verdict``            = "SUPPORTS" if the sentence is verified, else "UNSUPPORTED" (SUPPORTS,
6	                                  not "EXISTS" — operator).
7	  * ``independent_origin_count`` = number of DISTINCT Phase-4 origin clusters among the sentence's cited
8	                                  evidence (unmapped evidence counts as its own origin).
9	  * ``credibility_weight``      = MIN Phase-2 credibility weight over the cited evidence (a sentence is
10	                                  only as credible as its weakest cited source); absent → None (unknown,
11	                                  never fabricated).
12	  * ``certainty_label``         = a deterministic, env-overridable bucket; UNKNOWN credibility → "low"
13	                                  (unknown must never inflate certainty — Codex #1157).
14	
15	POSTURE (binding):
16	  * ADVISORY ONLY. NEVER changes ``is_verified`` / ``failure_reasons`` / ``tokens`` / ``sentence`` or any
17	    of ``strict_verify``'s six checks — they remain the only binding faithfulness gate. The four fields
18	    are side-outputs (Phase-1 proved them inert).
19	  * DEFAULT-OFF byte-identical: ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_DISCLOSURE`` (no production caller; the RENDER that
20	    surfaces these fields is the flag-gated follow-up I-cred-008b).
21	  * PURE: inputs are NOT mutated — new verifications are produced with ``dataclasses.replace`` (so the
22	    module never imports ``provenance_generator`` / couples to the faithfulness path). LAW VI; snake_case.
23	"""
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import dataclasses
27	import os
28	from typing import Any
29	
30	_FLAG = "PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_DISCLOSURE"
31	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
32	
33	_ENV_HIGH_CRED = "PG_DISCLOSURE_HIGH_CRED"
34	_ENV_LOW_CRED = "PG_DISCLOSURE_LOW_CRED"
35	_ENV_HIGH_MIN_ORIGINS = "PG_DISCLOSURE_HIGH_MIN_ORIGINS"
36	_DEFAULT_HIGH_CRED = 0.7
37	_DEFAULT_LOW_CRED = 0.4
38	_DEFAULT_HIGH_MIN_ORIGINS = 2
39	
40	# A8 (I-arch-006) — SOFT, SURFACED-ONLY credibility flags. ADVISORY: they append a string to the SV's
41	# ``soft_warnings`` list (the existing disclosure carrier that already rides out to
42	# ``claim_disclosure.json``); they NEVER touch ``is_verified`` / ``span_verdict`` / the verifier, NEVER
43	# drop a source, NEVER gate or hold release (mirror of the supersession ``soft_warning`` pattern —
44	# §-1.3 WEIGHT/CONSOLIDATE, credibility is a LABEL not a DROP). Both default OFF (no caller passes the
45	# optional signal => byte-identical) and the env knobs only tune the (off-by-default) flag once enabled.
46	_ENV_MIN_HIGH_CRED_CORROBORATORS = "PG_DISCLOSURE_LEGAL_MIN_HIGH_CRED_CORROBORATORS"
47	_DEFAULT_MIN_HIGH_CRED_CORROBORATORS = 1
48	# The marker strings are stable identifiers (a renderer maps them to human text), kept short for the
49	# screen-reader / inline render and so a §-1.1 auditor can grep them.
50	WARN_LEGAL_NO_HIGH_CRED_CORROBORATOR = (
51	    "legal_claim_no_high_credibility_corroborator: this load-bearing legal claim rests only on "
52	    "lower-credibility source(s); no high-credibility corroborator was found (advisory flag — the "
53	    "claim is NOT dropped and the report is NOT held)"
54	)
55	WARN_BLANK_BIBLIOGRAPHY_TITLE = (
56	    "metadata_quality_blank_bibliography_title: a cited source has a blank or degenerate "
57	    "bibliography title (metadata-quality label only — does not affect verification or release)"
58	)
59	
60	
61	def _blank_or_degenerate_title(title: Any) -> bool:
62	    """True when a bibliography title is missing / whitespace / a degenerate placeholder.
63	
64	    A pure, deterministic metadata-quality predicate (A8). It is a SURFACE label only; it never
65	    drops the source and never touches verification. ``None`` and non-strings count as blank.
66	    """
67	    if title is None:
68	        return True
69	    text = str(title).strip()
70	    if not text:
71	        return True
72	    lowered = text.lower()
73	    # Common degenerate placeholders observed in scraped bibliographies (soft-404 / chrome furniture).
74	    degenerate = {"untitled", "no title", "n/a", "na", "page not found", "404 not found", "-"}
75	    return lowered in degenerate
76	
77	
78	def legal_corroborator_soft_warning(
79	    *,
80	    is_verified: bool,
81	    credibility_values: list,
82	    high_cred_threshold: float,
83	    min_high_cred_corroborators: int,
84	) -> str | None:
85	    """A8 SOFT flag: a load-bearing legal claim that lacks >= N high-credibility corroborator(s).
86	
87	    Returns the stable ``WARN_LEGAL_NO_HIGH_CRED_CORROBORATOR`` marker string, or ``None`` when the
88	    claim DOES have enough high-credibility cited support (or has unknown credibility — unknown never
89	    fabricates a flag). PURE, advisory: the caller appends the string to ``soft_warnings``; this never
90	    drops the low-credibility source, never holds the report, never changes ``is_verified``.
91	
92	    Only a VERIFIED claim is flagged: an unverified claim already carries the stronger "low / unverified"
93	    certainty label, so a second weaker flag would be noise (and §-1.3 says credibility is a WEIGHT, the
94	    verifier remains the only hard gate).
95	    """
96	    if not is_verified:
97	        return None
98	    known = [c for c in (credibility_values or []) if c is not None]
99	    if not known:
100	        # Unknown credibility must NOT fabricate a flag (mirror of certainty: unknown => no inflation).
101	        return None
102	    high_cred_count = sum(1 for c in known if c >= high_cred_threshold)
103	    if high_cred_count >= max(1, int(min_high_cred_corroborators)):
104	        return None
105	    return WARN_LEGAL_NO_HIGH_CRED_CORROBORATOR
106	
107	
108	def blank_title_metadata_warning(titles: list) -> str | None:
109	    """A8 metadata-quality SURFACE: any cited source with a blank / degenerate bibliography title.
110	
111	    Returns the stable ``WARN_BLANK_BIBLIOGRAPHY_TITLE`` marker, or ``None`` when every cited title is
112	    present. PURE, advisory label only — never drops the source, never gates release.
113	    """
114	    for title in (titles or []):
115	        if _blank_or_degenerate_title(title):
116	            return WARN_BLANK_BIBLIOGRAPHY_TITLE
117	    return None
118	
119	
120	def credibility_disclosure_enabled() -> bool:
121	    """True unless ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_DISCLOSURE`` is unset/falsey (default OFF => byte-identical)."""
122	    return os.environ.get(_FLAG, "").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
123	
124	
125	def _float_env(name: str, default: float) -> float:
126	    try:
127	        return float(os.environ.get(name, "") or default)
128	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
129	        return default
130	
131	
132	def _int_env(name: str, default: int) -> int:
133	    try:
134	        return int(os.environ.get(name, "") or default)
135	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
136	        return default
137	
138	
139	def _cited_evidence_ids(sv: Any) -> list[str]:
140	    out: list[str] = []
141	    for token in (getattr(sv, "tokens", None) or []):
142	        eid = str(getattr(token, "evidence_id", "") or "")
143	        if eid:
144	            out.append(eid)
145	    return out
146	
147	
148	def _certainty_label(is_verified: bool, origin_count: int, credibility: float | None) -> str:
149	    """Deterministic, advisory certainty bucket. NEVER consulted by any verifier.
150	
151	    ``origin_count`` is the count the row will SURFACE — i.e. when a basket is threaded
152	    (I-arch-002 [10] / design §5 FIX-4 Reading A) it is the basket's
153	    ``verified_support_origin_count``, so certainty is bucketed on the SAME verified count
154	    the row discloses (never the larger clustered/unverified count — that would let a row
155	    read "high / 1 verified origin", the inflation the redesign exists to remove).
156	    """
157	    if not is_verified:
158	        return "low"
159	    if credibility is None:
160	        return "low"  # unknown credibility must NOT inflate certainty (Codex #1157)
161	    high_cred = _float_env(_ENV_HIGH_CRED, _DEFAULT_HIGH_CRED)
162	    low_cred = _float_env(_ENV_LOW_CRED, _DEFAULT_LOW_CRED)
163	    high_min_origins = _int_env(_ENV_HIGH_MIN_ORIGINS, _DEFAULT_HIGH_MIN_ORIGINS)
164	    if origin_count >= high_min_origins and credibility >= high_cred:
165	        return "high"
166	    if origin_count < 1 or credibility < low_cred:
167	        return "low"
168	    return "moderate"
169	
170	
171	def _verified_count_by_cluster(baskets: Any) -> dict:
172	    """``claim_cluster_id -> verified_support_origin_count`` (the ONLY strengthening count,
173	    design §5). ISOLATED per-member verified origins — NEVER ``total_clustered_origin_count``
174	    (the clustered, not-verified count, which this overwrite exists to stop surfacing)."""
175	    out: dict[str, int] = {}
176	    for basket in (baskets or []):
177	        ccid = str(getattr(basket, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
178	        if not ccid:
179	            continue
180	        out[ccid] = int(getattr(basket, "verified_support_origin_count", 0) or 0)
181	    return out
182	
183	
184	def _surfaced_verified_count(
185	    cited: list,
186	    verified_by_cluster: dict,
187	    cluster_id_by_evidence: dict,
188	) -> int | None:
189	    """The basket ``verified_support_origin_count`` to SURFACE on this sentence.
190	
191	    Multi-cluster-sentence rule (design §5 FIX-4): a sentence whose cited tokens span >1
192	    claim_cluster is verified PER-CLUSTER; the surfaced count is that claim's OWN basket
193	    count, NEVER the sentence-wide origin count and NEVER a sum/union across clusters. When
194	    a sentence cites tokens from several clusters we surface the CONSERVATIVE single-basket
195	    value — the MIN of the cited clusters' verified counts. MIN (not MAX) because the design's
196	    safety posture is "counts go DOWN; when in doubt keep separate" (§0/§9.2): a multi-cluster
197	    sentence is only as corroborated as the weakest cluster it leans on, and over-stating
198	    verified origins is the inflation direction the redesign exists to remove. The checkable
199	    invariant holds either way: ``surfaced <= max single-basket verified count``, never their
200	    sum. Returns ``None`` when NO cited evidence maps to any threaded basket (leaves the legacy
201	    clustered count in place for that row rather than fabricating a zero).
202	    """
203	    counts: list[int] = []
204	    for eid in cited:
205	        for ccid in (cluster_id_by_evidence.get(eid) or []):
206	            ccid = str(ccid)
207	            if ccid in verified_by_cluster:
208	                counts.append(verified_by_cluster[ccid])
209	    if not counts:
210	        return None
211	    return min(counts)
212	
213	
214	def populate_disclosure(
215	    verifications: list,
216	    credibility_by_evidence: dict,
217	    origin_by_evidence: dict,
218	    *,
219	    baskets: list | None = None,
220	    cluster_id_by_evidence: dict | None = None,
221	    load_bearing_by_sentence: dict | None = None,
222	    title_by_evidence: dict | None = None,
223	) -> list:
224	    """Return NEW ``SentenceVerification``s with the four disclosure fields populated — ADVISORY, pure.
225	
226	    ``credibility_by_evidence``: ``evidence_id -> Phase-2 credibility_weight``.
227	    ``origin_by_evidence``: ``evidence_id -> Phase-4 origin_cluster_id``.
228	    Inputs are NOT mutated; ``strict_verify`` is NOT re-run; ``is_verified`` is NEVER changed.
229	
230	    I-arch-002 [10] / design §5 FIX-4 (Reading A): when the per-claim ``baskets`` AND the
231	    ``cluster_id_by_evidence`` binding (both from ``CredibilityAnalysis``, P3.2) are threaded
232	    in, ``independent_origin_count`` is OVERWRITTEN with the sentence's basket
233	    ``verified_support_origin_count`` (ISOLATED per-member verified origins) instead of the
234	    clustered, not-verified origin count — and ``certainty_label`` is bucketed on that SAME
235	    verified count. This is a single OVERWRITE of the existing field (NOT a parallel new field),
236	    so it propagates to BOTH operator JSON emitters that read ``independent_origin_count``
237	    (``quantified_analysis.py``:539 and ``run_honest_sweep_r3.py``:353). When ``baskets`` /
238	    ``cluster_id_by_evidence`` are absent (default OFF), or a row's cited evidence maps to no
239	    threaded basket, the legacy clustered ``origin_count`` is surfaced BYTE-IDENTICALLY.
240	
241	    A8 (I-arch-006) SOFT flags — both OPTIONAL, default None => byte-identical:
242	      * ``load_bearing_by_sentence``: ``id(sv) -> bool`` (or any caller key it indexes per-sv; see the
243	        per-sv lookup below) marking a claim as a LOAD-BEARING LEGAL claim. When True AND the claim's
244	        cited credibility lacks a high-credibility corroborator, append
245	        ``WARN_LEGAL_NO_HIGH_CRED_CORROBORATOR`` to ``soft_warnings``. Advisory ONLY — never drops the
246	        low-credibility source, never holds release, never touches ``is_verified``.
247	      * ``title_by_evidence``: ``evidence_id -> bibliography title`` so a blank/degenerate title surfaces
248	        ``WARN_BLANK_BIBLIOGRAPHY_TITLE`` as a metadata-quality label. Advisory ONLY.
249	    Both flags only ADD to the ``soft_warnings`` list (the existing supersession carrier); they touch no
250	    other field. With both None the loop never computes a flag and the output is byte-identical.
251	    """
252	    cred_map = {str(k): v for k, v in (credibility_by_evidence or {}).items()}
253	    origin_map = {str(k): str(v) for k, v in (origin_by_evidence or {}).items()}
254	    title_map = {str(k): v for k, v in (title_by_evidence or {}).items()}
255	    # ``load_bearing_by_sentence`` is keyed by ``id(sv)`` of the INPUT verification objects (the caller
256	    # builds it from the same list it passes in). Absent => no claim is load-bearing => no legal flag.
257	    load_bearing_map = dict(load_bearing_by_sentence or {})
258	    high_cred_threshold = _float_env(_ENV_HIGH_CRED, _DEFAULT_HIGH_CRED)
259	    min_high_cred = _int_env(_ENV_MIN_HIGH_CRED_CORROBORATORS, _DEFAULT_MIN_HIGH_CRED_CORROBORATORS)
260	    # OVERWRITE source (design §5 FIX-4 Reading A). Empty unless a real basket is threaded =>
261	    # _surfaced_verified_count returns None => legacy clustered count, byte-identical.
262	    verified_by_cluster = _verified_count_by_cluster(baskets)
263	    binding = {
264	        str(k): [str(c) for c in (v or [])]
265	        for k, v in (cluster_id_by_evidence or {}).items()
266	    }
267	
268	    out: list = []
269	    for sv in (verifications or []):
270	        is_verified = bool(getattr(sv, "is_verified", False))
271	        cited = _cited_evidence_ids(sv)
272	        # Distinct origin clusters among cited evidence; an unmapped evidence is its own origin.
273	        origin_count = len({origin_map.get(eid, f"singleton::{eid}") for eid in cited})
274	        # I-arch-002 [10]: overwrite with the basket's ISOLATED-verified count when threaded;
275	        # None => leave the legacy clustered count (default-OFF byte-identity).
276	        surfaced = _surfaced_verified_count(cited, verified_by_cluster, binding)
277	        if surfaced is not None:
278	            origin_count = surfaced
279	        # MIN credibility over cited evidence (conservative — weakest cited source); absent -> None.
280	        creds = [cred_map[eid] for eid in cited if eid in cred_map and cred_map[eid] is not None]
281	        credibility = min(creds) if creds else None
282	        # A8 SOFT flags — append-only to soft_warnings; both no-op unless the caller threaded the
283	        # optional signal (default OFF => byte-identical). NEVER touch is_verified / span_verdict.
284	        new_warnings: list[str] = []
285	        if load_bearing_map.get(id(sv), False):
286	            legal_warn = legal_corroborator_soft_warning(
287	                is_verified=is_verified,
288	                credibility_values=[cred_map.get(eid) for eid in cited],
289	                high_cred_threshold=high_cred_threshold,
290	                min_high_cred_corroborators=min_high_cred,
291	            )
292	            if legal_warn:
293	                new_warnings.append(legal_warn)
294	        if title_map:
295	            blank_warn = blank_title_metadata_warning(
296	                [title_map.get(eid) for eid in cited if eid in title_map]
297	            )
298	            if blank_warn:
299	                new_warnings.append(blank_warn)
300	        replace_kwargs: dict = dict(
301	            span_verdict="SUPPORTS" if is_verified else "UNSUPPORTED",
302	            credibility_weight=credibility,
303	            independent_origin_count=origin_count,
304	            certainty_label=_certainty_label(is_verified, origin_count, credibility),
305	        )
306	        if new_warnings:
307	            existing = list(getattr(sv, "soft_warnings", None) or [])
308	            for w in new_warnings:
309	                if w not in existing:
310	                    existing.append(w)
311	            replace_kwargs["soft_warnings"] = existing
312	        out.append(dataclasses.replace(sv, **replace_kwargs))
313	    return out
314

1	"""
2	MoST Phase E: Evidence Self-Exploration (Van der Waals Bond).
3	
4	Implements the self-exploration bond from "The Molecular Structure of Thought"
5	(arXiv 2601.06002). Redistributes unused evidence to sections that could benefit,
6	bridging distant concept clusters.
7	
8	Cost: ~1 LLM call per enriched section (~3-5 calls, ~$0.004-0.009, ~1-3 min)
9	"""
10	
11	import asyncio
12	import logging
13	import os
14	import re
15	from typing import Any
16	
17	from src.polaris_graph.schemas import SectionDraft
18	
19	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
20	
21	
22	async def explore_unused_evidence(
23	    client,
24	    sections: list[SectionDraft],
25	    all_evidence: list[dict],
26	    section_evidence_map: dict[str, list[str]],
27	    query: str,
28	) -> list[SectionDraft]:
29	    """Redistribute unused evidence to sections that could benefit.
30	
31	    Finds evidence not cited in any section, matches to best sections
32	    via content similarity, enriches affected sections with new sentences.
33	
34	    Args:
35	        client: OpenRouter LLM client.
36	        sections: All SectionDraft objects.
37	        all_evidence: Full verified evidence pool.
38	        section_evidence_map: Map of section_id -> evidence_id list.
39	        query: Original research query.
40	
41	    Returns:
42	        Updated list of SectionDraft (some enriched with new evidence).
43	    """
44	    if not sections or not all_evidence:
45	        return sections
46	
47	    threshold = float(os.getenv("PG_EXPLORE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD", "0.55"))
48	    max_per_section = int(os.getenv("PG_EXPLORE_MAX_NEW_PER_SECTION", "5"))
49	
50	    # Step 1: Find unused evidence
51	    unused = _find_unused_evidence(sections, all_evidence)
52	    if not unused:
53	        logger.info("[MoST-E] All evidence is cited, skipping exploration")
54	        return sections
55	
56	    logger.info(
57	        "[MoST-E] Found %d unused evidence pieces out of %d total",
58	        len(unused), len(all_evidence),
59	    )
60	
61	    # Step 2: Match unused evidence to sections by content similarity
62	    matches = _match_evidence_to_sections(
63	        unused=unused,
64	        sections=sections,
65	        threshold=threshold,
66	        max_per_section=max_per_section,
67	    )
68	
69	    if not matches:
70	        logger.info("[MoST-E] No good matches found above threshold %.2f", threshold)
71	        return sections
72	
73	    # Step 3: Enrich sections that received matches
74	    results = list(sections)
75	    enriched_count = 0
76	    total_redistributed = 0
77	
78	    for i, sec in enumerate(sections):
79	        sid = getattr(sec, 'section_id', '')
80	        new_ev = matches.get(sid, [])
81	        if not new_ev:
82	            continue
83	
84	        try:
85	            enriched = await _enrich_section(client, sec, new_ev, query)
86	            if enriched is not None:
87	                # CASE_2 guard: reject if enriched is shorter than original
88	                orig_words = len(getattr(sec, 'content', '').split())
89	                enriched_words = len(getattr(enriched, 'content', '').split())
90	                if enriched_words >= orig_words:
91	                    results[i] = enriched
92	                    enriched_count += 1
93	                    total_redistributed += len(new_ev)
94	                    logger.info(
95	                        "[MoST-E] Enriched section '%s' with %d new evidence (%d -> %d words)",
96	                        getattr(sec, 'title', '')[:40],
97	                        len(new_ev),
98	                        orig_words,
99	                        enriched_words,
100	                    )
101	                else:
102	                    logger.warning(
103	                        "[MoST-E] Enrichment shortened section '%s' (%d -> %d words), keeping original",
104	                        getattr(sec, 'title', '')[:40], orig_words, enriched_words,
105	                    )
106	        except Exception as exc:
107	            logger.warning(
108	                "[MoST-E] Enrichment failed for section '%s': %s",
109	                getattr(sec, 'title', '')[:40], str(exc)[:200],
110	            )
111	
112	    logger.info(
113	        "[MoST-E] Exploration complete: %d sections enriched, %d evidence redistributed",
114	        enriched_count, total_redistributed,
115	    )
116	    return results
117	
118	
119	def _find_unused_evidence(
120	    sections: list[SectionDraft],
121	    all_evidence: list[dict],
122	) -> list[dict]:
123	    """Collect evidence pieces not cited in any section.
124	
125	    Scans all [CITE:ev_xxx] markers across all section content.
126	    Returns evidence pieces not found in any citation, sorted by
127	    relevance_score descending.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        sections: All report sections.
131	        all_evidence: Full evidence pool.
132	
133	    Returns:
134	        List of unused evidence dicts, sorted by relevance.
135	    """
136	    # Collect all cited evidence IDs across all sections
137	    cited_ids = set()
138	    for sec in sections:
139	        content = getattr(sec, 'content', '')
140	        found = re.findall(r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]', content)
141	        cited_ids.update(found)
142	
143	    # Filter to unused, sorted by relevance
144	    unused = [
145	        e for e in all_evidence
146	        if e.get("evidence_id", "") not in cited_ids
147	    ]
148	    unused.sort(key=lambda e: e.get("relevance_score", 0.0), reverse=True)
149	    return unused
150	
151	
152	def _match_evidence_to_sections(
153	    unused: list[dict],
154	    sections: list[SectionDraft],
155	    threshold: float = 0.55,
156	    max_per_section: int = 5,
157	) -> dict[str, list[dict]]:
158	    """M-02: Match unused evidence to best-fit sections via embedding cosine.
159	
160	    Replaces Jaccard word-overlap (max ~0.08 for 20-word evidence vs 70-word
161	    section text, making threshold 0.45 impossible). Uses embedding cosine
162	    similarity which operates in semantic space.
163	
164	    Args:
165	        unused: Unused evidence pieces.
166	        sections: All report sections.
167	        threshold: Minimum cosine similarity (default 0.55).
168	        max_per_section: Max new evidence per section.
169	
170	    Returns:
171	        Dict mapping section_id -> list of matched evidence dicts.
172	    """
173	    if not unused or not sections:
174	        return {}
175	
176	    import numpy as np
177	    from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
178	
179	    # Build texts for embedding
180	    ev_texts = []
181	    ev_filtered = []
182	    for e in unused:
183	        stmt = e.get("statement", "")
184	        if stmt:
185	            ev_texts.append(stmt[:200])
186	            ev_filtered.append(e)
187	
188	    sec_texts = []
189	    sec_ids = []
190	    for sec in sections:
191	        title = getattr(sec, "title", "")
192	        content = getattr(sec, "content", "")[:500]
193	        sec_texts.append(f"{title} {content}")
194	        sec_ids.append(getattr(sec, "section_id", ""))
195	
196	    if not ev_texts or not sec_texts:
197	        return {}
198	
199	    # Embed all texts in one batch
200	    all_texts = ev_texts + sec_texts
201	    embeddings = np.array(embed_texts(all_texts))
202	    ev_vecs = embeddings[: len(ev_texts)]
203	    sec_vecs = embeddings[len(ev_texts) :]
204	
205	    # Cosine similarity matrix (evidence x sections)
206	    # Embeddings are already L2-normalized by embedding_service
207	    similarity = ev_vecs @ sec_vecs.T
208	
209	    matches: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}
210	    for i, ev in enumerate(ev_filtered):
211	        best_j = int(np.argmax(similarity[i]))
212	        best_score = float(similarity[i][best_j])
213	        if best_score >= threshold:
214	            sid = sec_ids[best_j]
215	            existing = matches.setdefault(sid, [])
216	            if len(existing) < max_per_section:
217	                existing.append(ev)
218	
219	    logger.info(
220	        "[MoST-E] Embedding matching: %d evidence -> %d sections matched "
221	        "(threshold=%.2f)",
222	        len(ev_filtered),
223	        sum(len(v) for v in matches.values()),
224	        threshold,
225	    )
226	    return matches
227	
228	
229	async def _enrich_section(
230	    client,
231	    section: SectionDraft,
232	    new_evidence: list[dict],
233	    query: str,
234	) -> SectionDraft | None:
235	    """Enrich a section with newly matched evidence.
236	
237	    Asks LLM to integrate 1-2 sentences per evidence piece at the most
238	    relevant location. All inserted sentences MUST have [CITE:evidence_id].
239	
240	    Args:
241	        client: OpenRouter LLM client.
242	        section: Section to enrich.
243	        new_evidence: Evidence pieces to integrate.
244	        query: Research query for context.
245	
246	    Returns:
247	        Enriched SectionDraft, or None if enrichment fails.
248	    """
249	    # Format new evidence for prompt
250	    ev_formatted = []
251	    for ev in new_evidence[:5]:  # Cap at 5 per section
252	        ev_id = ev.get("evidence_id", "")
253	        statement = ev.get("statement", "")[:200]
254	        source = ev.get("source_title", "") or ev.get("source_url", "")[:50]
255	        ev_formatted.append(f"- [{ev_id}] {statement} (Source: {source})")
256	
257	    prompt = (
258	        "You are enriching a research section with additional evidence.\n\n"
259	        f"RESEARCH QUESTION: {query}\n\n"
260	        f"SECTION: {getattr(section, 'title', '')}\n"
261	        f"{getattr(section, 'content', '')}\n\n"
262	        "NEW EVIDENCE TO INTEGRATE:\n"
263	        + "\n".join(ev_formatted)
264	        + "\n\nTASK: Add 1-2 sentences per evidence piece at the most relevant location.\n"
265	        "RULES:\n"
266	        "- Use [CITE:evidence_id] for ALL new claims (e.g., [CITE:ev_abc123])\n"
267	        "- Do NOT rewrite or remove existing content\n"
268	        "- Do NOT change existing [CITE:] markers\n"
269	        "- Maintain academic tone\n"
270	        "- Output the COMPLETE revised section content only (no title)\n"
271	    )
272	
273	    try:
274	        resp = await client.generate(
275	            prompt, max_tokens=int(os.getenv("PG_EVIDENCE_ENRICH_MAX_TOKENS", "8192")),
276	        )
277	        content = getattr(resp, 'content', '') or ''
278	        if not content or len(content) < 50:
279	            return None
280	
281	        # Verify new citations were actually added
282	        new_ev_ids = {ev.get("evidence_id", "") for ev in new_evidence}
283	        found_new = set(re.findall(r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]', content)) & new_ev_ids
284	        if not found_new:
285	            logger.warning(
286	                "[MoST-E] LLM did not add any [CITE:] markers for new evidence in '%s'",
287	                getattr(section, 'title', '')[:40],
288	            )
289	            return None
290	
291	        # M-01: Sync evidence_ids from actual CITE markers in enriched content
292	        from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.section_utils import sync_evidence_ids_from_content
293	        return sync_evidence_ids_from_content(SectionDraft(
294	            section_id=getattr(section, 'section_id', ''),
295	            title=getattr(section, 'title', ''),
296	            content=content,
297	            claims_made=getattr(section, 'claims_made', []),
298	            evidence_ids=getattr(section, 'evidence_ids', []),
299	        ))
300	    except Exception as exc:
301	        logger.warning(
302	            "[MoST-E] Enrichment LLM call failed for '%s': %s",
303	            getattr(section, 'title', '')[:40], str(exc)[:200],
304	        )
305	        return None
306
