content.content:
1	"""
2	Embedding-based evidence routing for section assignment.
3	
4	Replaces the LLM-based _assign_evidence_globally() with deterministic
5	embedding similarity. Zero LLM cost, 100% assignment, ~2 seconds.
6	
7	Problem: _assign_evidence_globally() uses a single LLM call with 200-char
8	truncated summaries. Failed on TEST_002 (52->0 assigned). Costs ~$0.05/call,
9	stochastic, fragile ID mapping.
10	
11	Solution: Batch embed evidence statements and section descriptions.
12	Cosine similarity matrix assigns each evidence to its best section.
13	Evidence relevant to multiple sections (small delta) marked cross-section.
14	"""
15	
16	import logging
17	import os
18	
19	import numpy as np
20	
21	from dotenv import load_dotenv
22	
23	load_dotenv()
24	
25	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
26	
27	# Feature flag (LAW VI)
28	PG_EMBEDDING_ROUTING = os.getenv("PG_EMBEDDING_ROUTING", "1") == "1"
29	
30	# Evidence with top-2 section similarity delta below this threshold
31	# is treated as cross-section (visible to all sections).
32	PG_CROSS_SECTION_SIMILARITY_DELTA = float(
33	    os.getenv("PG_CROSS_SECTION_SIMILARITY_DELTA", "0.05")
34	)
35	
36	# Minimum similarity to be assigned to any section.
37	# Evidence below this threshold is unassigned (goes to cross-section pool).
38	PG_EMBEDDING_ROUTING_MIN_SIMILARITY = float(
39	    os.getenv("PG_EMBEDDING_ROUTING_MIN_SIMILARITY", "0.20")
40	)
41	
42	
43	def route_evidence_to_sections(
44	    evidence: list[dict],
45	    sections: list,
46	    query: str,
47	) -> tuple[dict[str, list[str]], list[str]]:
48	    """Deterministic embedding-based evidence-to-section routing.
49	
50	    Each evidence piece is assigned to its best-matching section by cosine
51	    similarity between the evidence statement and section title+description.
52	
53	    Evidence whose top-2 section similarity delta is below
54	    PG_CROSS_SECTION_SIMILARITY_DELTA is flagged as cross-section
55	    (visible to all sections).
56	
57	    Args:
58	        evidence: List of evidence dicts with evidence_id, statement.
59	        sections: List of SectionOutlineItem (or dicts with section_id, title, description).
60	        query: Original research query (used as context in section embeddings).
61	
62	    Returns:
63	        (section_assignments, cross_section_ids) — same tuple type as
64	        _assign_evidence_globally() for drop-in replacement.
65	        - section_assignments: {section_id: [ev_xxx, ...]}
66	        - cross_section_ids: [ev_xxx, ...] evidence visible to all sections
67	    """
68	    if not evidence or not sections:
69	        logger.warning("[evidence_router] Empty evidence or sections, skipping routing")
70	        return {}, []
71	
72	    try:
73	        from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
74	    except ImportError:
75	        logger.warning("[evidence_router] embedding_service unavailable, skipping routing")
76	        return {}, []
77	
78	    try:
79	        # Build section texts: title + description (+ query context)
80	        section_ids = []
81	        section_texts = []
82	        for sec in sections:
83	            sid = getattr(sec, "section_id", None) or sec.get("section_id", "")
84	            title = getattr(sec, "title", None) or sec.get("title", "")
85	            desc = getattr(sec, "description", None) or sec.get("description", "")
86	            section_ids.append(sid)
87	            section_texts.append(f"{title}. {desc}. Research question: {query}")
88	
89	        # Build evidence texts: statement (primary signal)
90	        evidence_ids = []
91	        evidence_texts = []
92	        for ev in evidence:
93	            eid = ev.get("evidence_id", "")
94	            stmt = ev.get("statement", "")
95	            evidence_ids.append(eid)
96	            evidence_texts.append(stmt)
97	
98	        if not section_texts or not evidence_texts:
99	            return {}, []
100	
101	        # Batch embed — uses existing infrastructure (384-dim, pre-normalized)
102	        section_vecs = np.array(embed_texts(section_texts))
103	        evidence_vecs = np.array(embed_texts(evidence_texts))
104	
105	        # Cosine similarity matrix: (num_evidence, num_sections)
106	        # Embeddings are pre-normalized, so dot product = cosine similarity
107	        similarity_matrix = evidence_vecs @ section_vecs.T
108	
109	        # Primary assignment: argmax per evidence row
110	        best_section_indices = np.argmax(similarity_matrix, axis=1)
111	        best_scores = similarity_matrix[np.arange(len(evidence_ids)), best_section_indices]
112	
113	        # Cross-section detection: evidence where top-2 delta is small
114	        cross_section_ids = []
115	        section_assignments: dict[str, list[str]] = {sid: [] for sid in section_ids}
116	
117	        for i, eid in enumerate(evidence_ids):
118	            best_score = best_scores[i]
119	
120	            # Skip evidence below minimum similarity threshold
121	            if best_score < PG_EMBEDDING_ROUTING_MIN_SIMILARITY:
122	                cross_section_ids.append(eid)
123	                continue
124	
125	            best_idx = best_section_indices[i]
126	            best_sid = section_ids[best_idx]
127	
128	            # Check if this evidence is borderline between sections
129	            if len(section_ids) >= 2:
130	                sorted_scores = np.sort(similarity_matrix[i])[::-1]
131	                delta = sorted_scores[0] - sorted_scores[1]
132	                if delta < PG_CROSS_SECTION_SIMILARITY_DELTA:
133	                    cross_section_ids.append(eid)
134	
135	            # Always assign to primary section (even if also cross-section)
136	            section_assignments[best_sid].append(eid)
137	
138	        # Remove empty sections from assignments
139	        section_assignments = {
140	            sid: ids for sid, ids in section_assignments.items() if ids
141	        }
142	
143	        # Diagnostics
144	        assigned_total = sum(len(ids) for ids in section_assignments.values())
145	        logger.info(
146	            "[evidence_router] Embedding routing: %d evidence -> %d sections "
147	            "(%d assigned, %d cross-section, similarity range %.3f-%.3f)",
148	            len(evidence),
149	            len(section_assignments),
150	            assigned_total,
151	            len(cross_section_ids),
152	            float(np.min(best_scores)),
153	            float(np.max(best_scores)),
154	        )
155	
156	        return section_assignments, cross_section_ids
157	
158	    except Exception as exc:
159	        logger.warning(
160	            "[evidence_router] Embedding routing failed: %s — "
161	            "falling back to per-section filtering",
162	            str(exc)[:200],
163	        )
164	        return {}, []
165

1	"""
2	MoST Ionic Bond: Evidence-Section Affinity Rebalancing.
3	
4	Checks if evidence pieces are cited in the section where they have highest
5	semantic affinity. Evidence "attracted" more strongly to a different section
6	than where it currently resides should migrate. Zero LLM cost.
7	
8	Cost: 0 LLM calls, ~3-5 seconds.
9	"""
10	
11	import logging
12	import os
13	import re
14	
15	import numpy as np
16	
17	from src.polaris_graph.schemas import SectionDraft
18	from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
19	
20	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
21	
22	# Minimum affinity delta to recommend migration (LAW VI)
23	_MIN_AFFINITY_DELTA = float(os.getenv("PG_IONIC_MIN_DELTA", "0.15"))
24	
25	
26	def analyze_ionic_bonds(
27	    sections: list[SectionDraft],
28	    evidence: list[dict],
29	) -> dict:
30	    """Run ionic bond analysis: check evidence-section affinity.
31	
32	    For each section, collect cited evidence, compute embedding similarity
33	    against ALL sections. Flag evidence where affinity to another section
34	    exceeds current section by > delta.
35	
36	    Args:
37	        sections: All SectionDraft objects.
38	        evidence: Full verified evidence pool.
39	
40	    Returns:
41	        Dict with keys: migrations, confirmed, stats.
42	    """
43	    if len(sections) < 2 or not evidence:
44	        return {"migrations": [], "confirmed": [], "stats": {"migrations": 0, "confirmed": 0}}
45	
46	    # Build evidence lookup
47	    ev_lookup = {
48	        e.get("evidence_id", ""): e
49	        for e in evidence
50	        if e.get("evidence_id") and e.get("statement")
51	    }
52	
53	    # Build section texts for embedding
54	    sec_texts = []
55	    sec_ids = []
56	    sec_id_to_idx = {}
57	    for i, sec in enumerate(sections):
58	        sid = getattr(sec, "section_id", "")
59	        title = getattr(sec, "title", "")
60	        content = getattr(sec, "content", "")[:500]
61	        sec_texts.append(f"{title} {content}")
62	        sec_ids.append(sid)
63	        sec_id_to_idx[sid] = i
64	
65	    # Embed section texts
66	    sec_vecs = np.array(embed_texts(sec_texts))
67	
68	    # Collect all cited evidence per section
69	    section_evidence: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
70	    for sec in sections:
71	        sid = getattr(sec, "section_id", "")
72	        content = getattr(sec, "content", "")
73	        cited = re.findall(r"\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]", content)
74	        section_evidence[sid] = list(set(cited))
75	
76	    # Collect all unique evidence IDs to analyze
77	    all_cited_eids = set()
78	    for eids in section_evidence.values():
79	        all_cited_eids.update(eids)
80	
81	    ev_items = [
82	        (eid, ev_lookup[eid])
83	        for eid in all_cited_eids
84	        if eid in ev_lookup
85	    ]
86	
87	    if not ev_items:
88	        return {"migrations": [], "confirmed": [], "stats": {"migrations": 0, "confirmed": 0}}
89	
90	    # Embed evidence statements
91	    ev_texts = [e["statement"][:200] for _, e in ev_items]
92	    ev_vecs = np.array(embed_texts(ev_texts))
93	
94	    # Compute evidence x section similarity
95	    similarity = ev_vecs @ sec_vecs.T
96	
97	    migrations: list[dict] = []
98	    confirmed: list[str] = []
99	
100	    for i, (eid, ev) in enumerate(ev_items):
101	        # Find which section currently cites this evidence
102	        current_sections = [
103	            sid for sid, eids in section_evidence.items()
104	            if eid in eids
105	        ]
106	
107	        for current_sid in current_sections:
108	            current_idx = sec_id_to_idx.get(current_sid)
109	            if current_idx is None:
110	                continue
111	
112	            current_affinity = float(similarity[i][current_idx])
113	            best_idx = int(np.argmax(similarity[i]))
114	            best_affinity = float(similarity[i][best_idx])
115	            best_sid = sec_ids[best_idx]
116	
117	            delta = best_affinity - current_affinity
118	            if best_sid != current_sid and delta >= _MIN_AFFINITY_DELTA:
119	                migrations.append({
120	                    "evidence_id": eid,
121	                    "from_section": current_sid,
122	                    "to_section": best_sid,
123	                    "current_affinity": round(current_affinity, 3),
124	                    "best_affinity": round(best_affinity, 3),
125	                    "delta": round(delta, 3),
126	                })
127	            else:
128	                confirmed.append(eid)
129	
130	    stats = {
131	        "migrations": len(migrations),
132	        "confirmed": len(confirmed),
133	        "total_analyzed": len(ev_items),
134	    }
135	
136	    logger.info(
137	        "[MoST-Ionic] Affinity analysis: %d migrations flagged, "
138	        "%d confirmed in place (out of %d evidence)",
139	        stats["migrations"],
140	        stats["confirmed"],
141	        stats["total_analyzed"],
142	    )
143	
144	    return {
145	        "migrations": migrations,
146	        "confirmed": confirmed,
147	        "stats": stats,
148	    }
149	
150	
151	def format_ionic_findings_for_phase_r(
152	    ionic_result: dict,
153	    target_section_id: str,
154	) -> str:
155	    """Format ionic findings as context for Phase R prompt.
156	
157	    Args:
158	        ionic_result: Output of analyze_ionic_bonds().
159	        target_section_id: The section being revised.
160	
161	    Returns:
162	        Formatted string for Phase R prompt injection.
163	    """
164	    migrations = ionic_result.get("migrations", [])
165	    if not migrations:
166	        return "No evidence misplacements detected."
167	
168	    # Evidence that should LEAVE this section
169	    outgoing = [
170	        m for m in migrations
171	        if m["from_section"] == target_section_id
172	    ]
173	    # Evidence that should ARRIVE in this section
174	    incoming = [
175	        m for m in migrations
176	        if m["to_section"] == target_section_id
177	    ]
178	
179	    parts = []
180	    if outgoing:
181	        parts.append("Evidence that belongs in OTHER sections (consider removing):")
182	        for m in outgoing[:3]:
183	            parts.append(
184	                f"  - {m['evidence_id']} -> better fit in section {m['to_section']} "
185	                f"(delta={m['delta']:.2f})"
186	            )
187	    if incoming:
188	        parts.append("Evidence from OTHER sections that belongs HERE (consider adding):")
189	        for m in incoming[:3]:
190	            parts.append(
191	                f"  - {m['evidence_id']} from section {m['from_section']} "
192	                f"(delta={m['delta']:.2f})"
193	            )
194	
195	    return "\n".join(parts) if parts else "No evidence misplacements detected."
196

content.content:
1	"""I-meta-005 Phase 5 (#989) — dedup-by-finding + corroboration.
2	
3	Clusters generator-visible evidence rows by the numeric FINDING they assert,
4	collapses rehashes of the SAME finding to one representative row, and attaches
5	``corroboration_count`` = the number of INDEPENDENT registrable-domains carrying
6	that finding. This is Knowledge-Based Trust (gap D of the re-architecture plan):
7	the sovereign, domain-general, self-computed trust signal — trust a finding the
8	rest of the corpus independently confirms, with no external authority service.
9	
10	CONSERVATIVE-SINGLETON safety rule (brief §2.4 — clinical-lethal if violated):
11	two findings merge ONLY when subject is KNOWN (not the ``"unknown"`` fallback)
12	and equal, predicate equal, value (rounded) + unit equal, AND every qualifier the
13	extractor exposes (dose, arm, endpoint_phrase) is equal — comparing raw field
14	values so ABSENT==ABSENT matches but ABSENT-vs-PRESENT does not. Any unknown
15	subject or any qualifier difference keeps the findings SEPARATE. The default on
16	ambiguity is always "keep separate" — we never drop a distinct finding.
17	
18	DOCUMENTED RESIDUAL 1 (over-merge bound): ``ExtractedNumericClaim`` does NOT
19	extract population or comparator. Two findings identical on every extracted field
20	but differing only in an UNEXTRACTED qualifier (e.g. a T2D vs an obesity
21	population that share "-2.1%") could merge. This is bounded to a corroboration
22	OVER-count — a TRUST signal, never a safety gate — and NEVER causes unique-claim
23	LOSS: the finding the representative asserts (subject/predicate/value/unit/dose/
24	arm/endpoint) is identical across all members by construction, and all
25	``member_indices`` + ``member_hosts`` are preserved on the cluster for audit
26	(manifest + conflict surfacing). A future phase may add a population/comparator
27	extractor to tighten the key.
28	
29	RESIDUAL 2 — NOW CLOSED (I-deepfix-001 C1, #1344; supersedes the stale
30	"deferred to a follow-up" note): the field-agnostic numeric-finding extractor
31	that this docstring once deferred is LIVE. ``extract_numeric_claims`` routes a
32	NON-clinical row (deterministic ``is_clinical_domain`` signal) to the
33	DOMAIN-AGNOSTIC extractor (B9, commit ac039560), so a GDP / emissions /
34	model-accuracy numeric now yields a REAL claim key instead of nothing. But the
35	merged run still measured ``collapsed=0`` on non-clinical corpora — the traced
36	non-firing seam was the MERGE KEY, not the extractor: ``_finding_key`` keyed the
37	subject on the RAW surface string, so two sources paraphrasing the SAME subject
38	with a different surface form ("e-commerce" vs "ecommerce" vs "E-Commerce") got
39	DISTINCT keys and never consolidated. C1 STRENGTHENS the non-clinical key to a
40	folded subject SIGNATURE (``_fold_nonclinical_subject`` — case/punctuation-folded,
41	so surface variants of one subject collapse) while keeping predicate + value +
42	unit as hard discriminators, so two DISTINCT facts that merely share a number
43	NEVER collapse. The CLINICAL key is kept VERBATIM (the conservative-singleton
44	subject/predicate/value/unit/dose/arm/endpoint guard) — a clinical row is routed
45	by its own ``is_clinical_domain`` probe and takes the byte-identical strict key,
46	so a dose/population can never wrongly merge. (The multi-claim-per-row retention
47	logic below is still defensive/future-proof against an extractor that emits >1
48	claim per row.)
49	
50	Pure: constructs no client, no network, no LLM. snake_case; explicit imports.
51	"""
52	from __future__ import annotations
53	
54	import logging
55	import math
56	import os
57	import re
58	import time
59	from concurrent.futures import (
60	    FIRST_COMPLETED,
61	    ThreadPoolExecutor,
62	    wait as futures_wait,
63	)
64	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
65	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
66	from urllib.parse import urlparse
67	
68	from src.polaris_graph.authority.corroboration import (
69	    count_independent_hosts,
70	    registrable_domain,
71	)
72	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.contradiction_detector import (
73	    extract_numeric_claims,
74	)
75	
76	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.finding_dedup")
77	
78	# The fallback subject `extract_numeric_claims` returns when it cannot identify
79	# the entity nearest the numeric value. Such claims are NEVER mergeable.
80	_UNKNOWN_SUBJECT = "unknown"
81	
82	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
83	# Non-clinical subject-signature fold — I-deepfix-001 C1 (#1344)
84	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
85	# §-1.3 CONSOLIDATE, don't DROP: the B9 domain-agnostic extractor is live, but the
86	# numeric merge key keyed the subject on the RAW surface string, so two NON-clinical
87	# sources paraphrasing the SAME subject in a different surface form never clustered
88	# (the measured `collapsed=0`). C1 folds ONLY the NON-clinical subject slot into a
89	# case/punctuation-normalized SIGNATURE so surface variants of one subject collapse,
90	# while predicate + value + unit stay hard discriminators (two DISTINCT facts that
91	# merely share a number never merge). CLINICAL rows keep the VERBATIM strict key.
92	# FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: this only groups more same-claim corroborators into one
93	# basket (corroboration_count is a Signal-D WEIGHT, never a verify gate); it drops
94	# no row and touches no faithfulness engine. LAW VI kill-switch (default ON).
95	_NONCLINICAL_SUBJECT_FOLD_ENV = "PG_FINDING_DEDUP_NONCLINICAL_SUBJECT_FOLD"
96	_NONALNUM_FOLD_RE = re.compile(r"[^a-z0-9]+")
97	
98	
99	def _nonclinical_fold_enabled() -> bool:
100	    """``PG_FINDING_DEDUP_NONCLINICAL_SUBJECT_FOLD`` kill switch (LAW VI). DEFAULT-ON:
101	    the C1 non-clinical subject signature. Set to ``0`` to restore the byte-identical
102	    raw-surface-subject key (no folding — the pre-C1 behavior). Clinical rows are
103	    unaffected either way (they never fold)."""
104	    return os.getenv(_NONCLINICAL_SUBJECT_FOLD_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in (
105	        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",
106	    )
107	
108	
109	def _fold_nonclinical_subject(subject: str) -> str:
110	    """The folded NON-clinical subject signature: lowercased, every non-alphanumeric
111	    run stripped. Collapses surface variants of ONE subject ("e-commerce" / "ecommerce"
112	    / "E-Commerce" -> "ecommerce") WITHOUT merging genuinely different subjects ("gdp"
113	    stays distinct from "gnp"). Returns ``""`` for a subject that folds to nothing
114	    (pure punctuation) — the caller then treats it as the UNKNOWN sentinel (safe
115	    singleton, never a false merge)."""
116	    return _NONALNUM_FOLD_RE.sub("", (subject or "").strip().lower())
117	
118	
119	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
120	# Same-work consolidation — I-beatboth-011 #7 CORE (#1289)
121	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
122	#
123	# §-1.1 audit of a rendered report (outputs/p6_postfix_resume/workforce/
124	# drb_72_ai_labor/report.md) → DO_NOT_SHIP. The faithfulness engine is correct
125	# (zero fabricated findings) — the defect is at THIS consolidation layer: the
126	# SAME WORK appearing at multiple URLs was counted as N INDEPENDENT sources,
127	# padding breadth ~2-3x. Real examples: Autor [1][2][3][4] = ONE work;
128	# Frey & Osborne [8][9][10] = ONE; Acemoglu & Restrepo [5][6] = ONE.
129	#
130	# Fix (§-1.3 "consolidate, keep-all, never drop a corroborator"): GROUP rows
131	# that are the SAME WORK — same normalized DOI; else same folded title — into
132	# ONE same-work unit that KEEPS ALL its URLs as corroborating locators (multi-
133	# URL corroboration, never delete a real corroborator) but COUNTS / PRESENTS as
134	# ONE source, and that counts as ONE independent origin in a finding cluster's
135	# `corroboration_count` (so 4 URLs of one Autor paper across 4 domains stop
136	# inflating the independent-host tally to 4). Also DROP non-functional members:
137	# a CAPTCHA / anti-bot security stub (text contains "Just a moment" /
138	# "Performing security verification") and a truncated-intro duplicate that is a
139	# strict prefix of a longer member of the SAME work.
140	#
141	# FAITHFULNESS LOCK: this changes only how same-work members are GROUPED /
142	# COUNTED. strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding are
143	# untouched. `corroboration_count` is a credibility WEIGHT (Signal D), never a
144	# gate — de-padding it is faithfulness-neutral. Two GENUINELY different works
145	# (different DOI AND different folded title) are NEVER merged.
146	#
147	# COORDINATION (#1289): `generator/weighted_enrichment.py` does the ENRICHMENT-
148	# side / render-side same-work consolidation in parallel. The two MUST agree, so
149	# the same-work key computation below is the SHARED canonical contract and is
150	# duplicated BYTE-FOR-BYTE in both files (the no-new-source-file rule forbids a
151	# shared module, so the logic is copied and pinned here as the contract):
152	#   DOI first  → lowercased, strip a leading "doi:" / "https://doi.org/" /
153	#                "http://dx.doi.org/" (and https) prefix, trim, rstrip "/";
154	#                a USABLE DOI must start with the "10." registrant prefix
155	#                (anything else is noise). Non-empty wins → merge (DOI is a
156	#                strong unique identifier).
157	#   else TITLE → lowercased, drop non-alphanumeric (punctuation→space), collapse
158	#                runs of whitespace to one, strip; a foldable title must be
159	#                >= 12 chars (guards against an over-merge on a tiny/generic
160	#                title).
161	#
162	# I-beatboth-011 #4 (#1289) — P1 OVER-MERGE FIX (§-1.3: NEVER merge distinct
163	# works; under-merge is safe, over-merge corrupts breadth/attribution): the
164	# no-DOI branch MUST NOT merge on folded TITLE ALONE — two genuinely DIFFERENT
165	# works can share a normalized title and would be wrongly collapsed, losing
166	# distinct corroborators. So the no-DOI key requires folded title PLUS the FIRST
167	# PRESENT corroborating discriminator the records share, in this fixed priority
168	# order: publication YEAR → first-author SURNAME → VENUE/journal → URL HOST.
169	# A priority-ordered composite (NOT pairwise OR / union-find: OR-over-signals is
170	# non-transitive and over-merges through chains — A~B on year, A~C on author then
171	# B,C collapse though they share only a title) is a plain equality key: transitive
172	# by construction, drops into the existing single-key grouping, biased to
173	# UNDER-merge. If the title folds but NO discriminator is present, the row gets a
174	# title-only fingerprint that is NOT a same-work key → it stays its own singleton
175	# work (distinct), never merged on title alone.
176	# A row with neither a usable DOI nor (foldable title + a present discriminator)
177	# gets NO same-work key (it is its own singleton work — never merged on emptiness).
178	# CAPTCHA / anti-bot stub detection (I-beatboth-011 #7 P1, #1289). The bare phrase
179	# "just a moment" is NOT enough to drop a member — real prose can carry it ("Just a
180	# moment — the data show wages rose 5% in 2023"). Dropping such prose would violate
181	# §-1.3 keep-all. A drop requires the trigger phrase AND a STRONG WAF / security
182	# co-token (BYTE-IDENTICAL predicate shared with weighted_enrichment._is_captcha_stub
183	# so consolidation and render agree). The co-tokens are high-precision multi-word /
184	# branded anchors a genuine clinical sentence (any language) never contains.
185	_CAPTCHA_STUB_TRIGGER = "just a moment"
186	_WAF_CO_TOKENS = (
187	    "performing security verification",   # Cloudflare / generic WAF
188	    "checking your browser",              # Cloudflare "checking your browser before accessing"
189	    "cloudflare",                         # Cloudflare attribution / interstitial brand
190	    "ray id",                             # Cloudflare error footer "Ray ID: ..."
191	    "cf-ray",                             # Cloudflare response-header / footer token
192	    "enable javascript and cookies",      # Cloudflare retry prompt
193	    "ddos protection",                    # Cloudflare attribution stub
194	    "attention required",                 # Cloudflare 1020 / block interstitial title
195	    "verifying you are human",            # hCaptcha / Cloudflare Turnstile
196	    "needs to review the security of your connection",  # Cloudflare interstitial body
197	)
198	_DOI_PREFIX_RE = re.compile(
199	    r"^(?:doi:|https?://(?:dx\.)?doi\.org/)", re.IGNORECASE
200	)
201	_TITLE_NONALNUM_RE = re.compile(r"[^a-z0-9]+")
202	_WHITESPACE_RUN_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
203	# Minimum folded-title length to be a usable same-work title (over-merge guard on
204	# a tiny/generic title). SHARED with weighted_enrichment._normalize_title.
205	_MIN_TITLE_LEN = 12
206	# Publication-year validity bounds (SHARED with the selector's _row_year convention
207	# at evidence_selector.py:769-770). A year outside this range is treated as absent.
208	_MIN_YEAR = 1900
209	_MAX_YEAR = 2100
210	
211	
212	def _normalize_doi(doi: Any) -> str:
213	    """Canonical DOI for same-work grouping (SHARED contract — see module note).
214	
215	    Lowercase → strip a leading ``doi:`` / ``https://doi.org/`` /
216	    ``http://dx.doi.org/`` (http+https) prefix → trim → ``rstrip("/")``. A usable
217	    DOI starts with the ``10.`` registrant prefix; anything else is noise.
218	    Returns ``""`` for a missing / blank / non-``10.`` DOI (it never groups two
219	    works). Matches ``weighted_enrichment._normalize_doi`` byte-for-byte.
220	    """
221	    text = str(doi or "").strip().lower()
222	    if not text:
223	        return ""
224	    text = _DOI_PREFIX_RE.sub("", text).strip().rstrip("/")
225	    return text if text.startswith("10.") else ""
226	
227	
228	def _fold_title(title: Any) -> str:
229	    """Case/punct/whitespace-folded title for same-work grouping (SHARED
230	    contract — see module note).
231	
232	    Lowercase → every non-alphanumeric run → single space → collapse whitespace
233	    → strip. Returns ``""`` when the folded title is shorter than
234	    ``_MIN_TITLE_LEN`` (a tiny/generic title is an over-merge risk and never
235	    groups two works). Matches ``weighted_enrichment._normalize_title``.
236	    """
237	    text = str(title or "").strip().lower()
238	    if not text:
239	        return ""
240	    text = _TITLE_NONALNUM_RE.sub(" ", text)
241	    text = _WHITESPACE_RUN_RE.sub(" ", text).strip()
242	    return text if len(text) >= _MIN_TITLE_LEN else ""
243	
244	
245	def _row_title(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
246	    """The row's title across the schema aliases (``source_title`` is canonical;
247	    ``title`` / ``page_title`` / ``name`` are the validator-mapped variants)."""
248	    for key in ("source_title", "title", "page_title", "name"):
249	        value = row.get(key)
250	        if value:
251	            return str(value)
252	    return ""
253	
254	
255	def _row_year(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
256	    """Publication year as a discriminator token ('' when absent/invalid).
257	
258	    Reads ``row['year']`` else ``row['metadata']['year']`` and validates the
259	    [1900, 2100] range — the SHARED convention with the selector's ``_row_year``
260	    (evidence_selector.py:793-809) and ``weighted_enrichment._record_year``.
261	    """
262	    val = row.get("year")
263	    if val is None:
264	        meta = row.get("metadata")
265	        if isinstance(meta, dict):
266	            val = meta.get("year")
267	    if val is None:
268	        return ""
269	    try:
270	        year = int(val)
271	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
272	        return ""
273	    return str(year) if _MIN_YEAR <= year <= _MAX_YEAR else ""
274	
275	
276	def _first_author_surname(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
277	    """First-author surname (folded) as a discriminator token ('' when absent).
278	
279	    Records carry ``authors`` (a list, family-name-first, e.g. ``["Autor D", ...]``)
280	    or a singular ``author`` string. The surname is the FIRST whitespace token of
281	    the first author, lowercased + non-alphanumerics stripped. SHARED with
282	    ``weighted_enrichment._first_author_surname``.
283	    """
284	    raw = row.get("authors")
285	    first = ""
286	    if isinstance(raw, (list, tuple)):
287	        for entry in raw:
288	            if entry and str(entry).strip():
289	                first = str(entry).strip()
290	                break
291	    elif raw:
292	        first = str(raw).strip()
293	    if not first:
294	        single = row.get("author")
295	        if single and str(single).strip():
296	            first = str(single).strip()
297	    if not first:
298	        return ""
299	    surname = first.split()[0] if first.split() else ""
300	    surname = _TITLE_NONALNUM_RE.sub("", surname.lower())
301	    return surname
302	
303	
304	def _row_venue(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
305	    """Venue/journal (folded) as a discriminator token ('' when absent).
306	
307	    Reads ``venue`` else ``journal`` (the two schema aliases), lowercased with
308	    non-alphanumeric runs collapsed to a single space and trimmed. SHARED with
309	    ``weighted_enrichment._record_venue``.
310	    """
311	    raw = row.get("venue") or row.get("journal") or ""
312	    text = str(raw).strip().lower()
313	    if not text:
314	        return ""
315	    text = _TITLE_NONALNUM_RE.sub(" ", text)
316	    return _WHITESPACE_RUN_RE.sub(" ", text).strip()
317	
318	
319	def _row_host(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
320	    """URL host (no leading ``www.``) as the WEAKEST discriminator token.
321	
322	    Same-work fetches usually span DIFFERENT hosts (the Autor example spans 4
323	    domains), so host merges almost nothing — it is last in the priority order
324	    purely as a safety net. SHARED with ``weighted_enrichment._record_host``.
325	    """
326	    return _host_of(str(row.get("source_url", "") or row.get("url", "") or ""))
327	
328	
329	def _title_discriminator(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
330	    """The STRICT corroborating discriminator for the no-DOI title branch.
331	
332	    I-beatboth-011 #4 P2 hardening (#1289): the no-DOI key MUST be strong enough that
333	    two DISTINCT works sharing a title cannot merge on a single weak signal. A single
334	    weak signal alone (year-only or host-only) is NOT enough. The token requires the
335	    folded title PLUS either:
336	      * a STRONG discriminator (first-author surname and/or venue) — every present
337	        STRONG/year signal is folded in (year → author → venue, fixed order), so a
338	        differing year OR differing author OR differing venue yields a DIFFERENT token
339	        and the two works do NOT merge; OR
340	      * two INDEPENDENT WEAK signals (year AND host) when no strong signal is present.
341	
342	    HOST IS ENABLING-ONLY, NEVER BLOCKING. Same-work members are the same work fetched
343	    at DIFFERENT URLs, so they (almost) always differ on host (the ``_row_host``
344	    safety-net premise + §-1.3). Host therefore appears ONLY as the SECOND weak signal
345	    alongside year, and NEVER in the strong-path token — otherwise every legitimate
346	    same-work merge (which spans different hosts) would be blocked.
347	
348	    Returns '' when neither a strong signal nor (year AND host) is present, so the row
349	    stays a title-only singleton and is never merged on title alone. SHARED contract with
350	    ``weighted_enrichment._title_discriminator`` (byte-identical key string).
351	    """
352	    year = _row_year(row)
353	    surname = _first_author_surname(row)
354	    venue = _row_venue(row)
355	    host = _row_host(row)
356	    if surname or venue:
357	        parts: list[str] = []
358	        if year:
359	            parts.append("y:" + year)
360	        if surname:
361	            parts.append("a:" + surname)
362	        if venue:
363	            parts.append("v:" + venue)
364	        return "|".join(parts)
365	    if year and host:
366	        return "y:" + year + "|h:" + host
367	    return ""
368	
369	
370	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
371	# Same-URL / same-file consolidation — I-deepfix-003 STEP 4 (#1374)
372	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
373	# §-1.3 CONSOLIDATE-don't-DROP: a chunked PDF (e.g. "reb-t-9-2-2026.pdf") with NO
374	# DOI and only weak/varying per-chunk titles produced an EMPTY ``_same_work_key`` for
375	# every chunk, so ~18 chunks of the SAME file each became their OWN singleton work —
376	# 18 phantom independent sources padding breadth/attribution. The DOI + title legs
377	# below cannot catch it (no DOI; the per-chunk title folds to nothing / lacks a
378	# discriminator), so nothing groups them.
379	#
380	# THE FIX (a FIRST, highest-precedence leg): two rows fetched from the SAME document —
381	# the SAME normalized source_url — are the SAME WORK, period. A shared fetch URL
382	# identifies one document more reliably than a missing/noisy extracted DOI or a weak
383	# per-chunk title. A usable normalized URL yields a ``url:<normalized>`` key that every
384	# chunk of one file shares => ONE same-work group. KEEP-ALL: each member's evidence_id /
385	# URL is kept as a corroborating locator (counted/presented as ONE source, never dropped).
386	# FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: same-work grouping is a WEIGHT / render concern — strict_verify /
387	# NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding are untouched, and a shared-URL group
388	# counts as exactly ONE independent origin in a finding cluster's ``corroboration_count``.
389	#
390	# NORMALIZATION (§-1.3 "over-merge corrupts attribution; under-merge is safe"): scheme +
391	# lowercased host (leading ``www.`` stripped) + path (trailing ``/`` stripped), FRAGMENT
392	# dropped. A usable URL key requires a NON-EMPTY path — a host-only URL (e.g.
393	# "https://site.com/") is far too coarse (it would fold every page of a site into one
394	# work) and falls through to the DOI/title legs. A bare filename with no host
395	# ("reb-t-9-2-2026.pdf") uses the path alone (the task's "for a PDF the filename is
396	# sufficient").
397	#
398	# QUERY IS KEPT — a §-1.3-mandated refinement of the STEP-4 brief's "drop the query".
399	# Because this leg is HIGHEST precedence, DROPPING the query would over-merge two
400	# GENUINELY DIFFERENT works served by the SAME endpoint that differ ONLY in an
401	# identity-bearing query param — e.g. "site/download?doi=10.1/a" vs "?doi=10.2/b", or
402	# "viewer?docid=123" vs "?docid=456" — folding them into one work even though their DOIs
403	# differ. That is exactly the distinct-work over-merge §-1.3 (operator-locked) forbids.
404	# Keeping the query still fixes the reported bug (the 18 chunks carry the SAME full
405	# source_url, query included, so they still merge); the only cost is a SAFE UNDER-merge —
406	# two fetches of one doc that differ solely by fetch-time tracking noise (utm_* etc.) stay
407	# separate, which is the §-1.3-preferred failure side. (The finding_dedup independent-host
408	# tally already collapses same-URL rows to ONE origin via ``count_independent_hosts``; the
409	# URL leg's decisive effect is de-padding the same-work / breadth / render layer.)
410	#
411	# NOTE — SHARED CONTRACT: the DOI/title legs are duplicated byte-for-byte in
412	# ``weighted_enrichment._work_identity`` (the render/enrichment-side consolidator), and the
413	# SAME URL leg is now mirrored THERE too (Fable gate P1-C, GH I-deepfix-003 #1374) behind the
414	# SAME ``PG_SAMEWORK_URL_LEG`` switch — so BOTH the corroboration-side and render/enrichment-
415	# side consolidators collapse a no-DOI / weak-title multi-chunk PDF to one work (full lockstep
416	# parity, verified by tests/polaris_graph/test_work_identity_url_mirror.py).
417	#
418	# LAW VI kill-switch ``PG_SAMEWORK_URL_LEG`` (default ON). OFF => the URL leg is skipped and
419	# the key is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the pre-STEP-4 DOI/title-only keying.
420	_SAMEWORK_URL_LEG_ENV = "PG_SAMEWORK_URL_LEG"
421	
422	
423	def _samework_url_leg_enabled() -> bool:
424	    """``PG_SAMEWORK_URL_LEG`` kill switch (LAW VI). DEFAULT-ON: the same-URL same-work
425	    leg (STEP 4). Set to ``0`` to restore the byte-identical DOI/title-only
426	    ``_same_work_key`` (no URL leg — the pre-STEP-4 behavior)."""
427	    return os.getenv(_SAMEWORK_URL_LEG_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in (
428	        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",
429	    )
430	
431	
432	def _normalize_source_url(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
433	    """The NORMALIZED same-work URL identity of a row (``""`` when there is no usable URL).
434	
435	    scheme + lowercased host (leading ``www.`` stripped) + path (trailing ``/`` stripped),
436	    with the FRAGMENT dropped and the QUERY KEPT (see the module note above — dropping the
437	    query would over-merge identity-in-query works; keeping it only risks a §-1.3-safe
438	    under-merge). A usable key requires a NON-EMPTY path (a host-only URL is too coarse and
439	    returns ``""``); a bare filename with no host uses the path alone. A blank / unparseable
440	    URL returns ``""`` so the caller falls through to the DOI / title legs.
441	    """
442	    raw = str(row.get("source_url", "") or row.get("url", "") or "").strip()
443	    if not raw:
444	        return ""
445	    parsed = urlparse(raw)
446	    host = (parsed.hostname or "").lower().strip()
447	    if host.startswith("www."):
448	        host = host[4:]
449	    path = (parsed.path or "").rstrip("/")
450	    if not path:
451	        # Host-only (or empty) URL: too coarse to identify ONE document — fall through
452	        # to the DOI / title legs rather than fold an entire host into one work.
453	        return ""
454	    scheme = (parsed.scheme or "").lower()
455	    prefix = (scheme + "://" + host) if host else ""
456	    query = parsed.query
457	    return prefix + path + (("?" + query) if query else "")
458	
459	
460	def _same_work_key(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
461	    """The SHARED same-work key: normalized source_url FIRST (STEP 4), else normalized
462	    DOI, else folded title PLUS a corroborating discriminator, else ``""`` (no same-work
463	    grouping — the row is its own singleton work).
464	
465	    I-deepfix-003 STEP 4 (#1374): a FIRST, highest-precedence leg — a normalized
466	    source_url (``_normalize_source_url``). Two rows fetched from the SAME document are the
467	    SAME work regardless of a missing/noisy DOI or a weak per-chunk title (the ~18-chunk
468	    PDF breadth-padding bug). Behind the ``PG_SAMEWORK_URL_LEG`` kill switch (default ON);
469	    OFF => this leg is skipped and the key is byte-identical DOI/title-only keying.
470	
471	    I-beatboth-011 #4 (#1289): the no-DOI branch NEVER merges on folded title
472	    ALONE (two different works can share a title). It requires the folded title
473	    AND the FIRST present discriminator (year → first-author surname → venue →
474	    host). Title-with-no-discriminator → ``""`` → singleton. The DOI + title legs
475	    match ``weighted_enrichment._work_identity`` so those two consolidators put the same
476	    members in the same work; the STEP-4 URL leg is added HERE only (see the module note).
477	    """
478	    if _samework_url_leg_enabled():
479	        url_key = _normalize_source_url(row)
480	        if url_key:
481	            return "url:" + url_key
482	    doi = _normalize_doi(row.get("doi"))
483	    if doi:
484	        return "doi:" + doi
485	    folded = _fold_title(_row_title(row))
486	    if folded:
487	        discriminator = _title_discriminator(row)
488	        if discriminator:
489	            return "title:" + folded + "|" + discriminator
490	    return ""
491	
492	
493	def _row_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
494	    """The row's body text for CAPTCHA-stub + prefix-duplicate detection."""
495	    for key in ("direct_quote", "statement", "evidence_summary", "text"):
496	        value = row.get(key)
497	        if value:
498	            return str(value)
499	    return ""
500	
501	
502	def _is_captcha_stub(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
503	    """True iff the row's body is a CAPTCHA / anti-bot security stub.
504	
505	    Self-contained literal check (no cross-package import — finding_dedup defers
506	    imports specifically to dodge cycles). Unconditional: a stub is dropped
507	    whether or not it has same-work siblings (it carries no real claim).
508	
509	    I-beatboth-011 #7 P1 (#1289): the bare trigger phrase ("just a moment") is NOT
510	    sufficient — a genuinely substantive sentence can contain it. A drop requires the
511	    trigger AND a strong WAF / security co-token (BYTE-IDENTICAL predicate shared with
512	    ``weighted_enrichment._is_captcha_stub``). §-1.3 keep-all: real prose carrying a
513	    bare "just a moment" with no security co-token is never dropped.
514	    """
515	    low = _row_text(row).lower()
516	    return (_CAPTCHA_STUB_TRIGGER in low) and any(tok in low for tok in _WAF_CO_TOKENS)
517	
518	
519	def _host_of(url: str) -> str:
520	    """Bare hostname for independent-host counting: urlparse → lowercase →
521	    strip leading ``www.``. Empty string on an unparseable/missing URL.
522	
523	    `count_independent_hosts` / `registrable_domain` expect HOSTS, not full
524	    URLs, so this reduction MUST happen before they are called (else two paths
525	    on the same domain would count as separate institutions).
526	    """
527	    if not url:
528	        return ""
529	    host = (urlparse(url).hostname or "").lower().strip()
530	    if host.startswith("www."):
531	        host = host[4:]
532	    return host
533	
534	
535	def _finding_key(
536	    claim: Any,
537	    evidence_id: str,
538	    claim_index: int,
539	    *,
540	    exact_value: bool = False,
541	    clinical: bool = True,
542	) -> tuple:
543	    """Conservative finding key. An ``unknown`` subject yields a per-CLAIM
544	    sentinel (evidence_id + claim_index) so it can never collide — even two
545	    unknown claims on the SAME row stay distinct singletons.
546	
547	    ``exact_value`` (I-arch-002 (#1246) P3.3, design §2) — under
548	    ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN`` the value slot is the EXACT float (no
549	    ``round(..., 3)``), matching ``claim_graph._normalized_key_numeric`` (L238)
550	    so basket clustering keys agree across the two consolidators (a shared
551	    type-consistency requirement of the design). OFF keeps ``round(value, 3)``
552	    byte-for-byte (the legacy survivor-selection key).
553	
554	    ``clinical`` (I-deepfix-001 C1, #1344) — DEFAULT True keeps the byte-identical
555	    strict subject key (the conservative-singleton clinical guard). When the row is
556	    NON-clinical (routed by ``is_clinical_domain`` exactly as the extractor routes)
557	    AND the C1 fold is enabled, the subject slot is a case/punctuation-folded
558	    SIGNATURE (``_fold_nonclinical_subject``) so surface variants of ONE subject
559	    consolidate while predicate + value + unit still keep two DISTINCT facts apart.
560	    A subject that folds to nothing collapses to the UNKNOWN sentinel (safe
561	    singleton). NO other slot changes, so the tuple shape / downstream consumers
562	    (``_cluster_value_bucket``, basket routing) are unaffected.
563	    """
564	    subject = getattr(claim, "subject", "") or ""
565	    if not clinical and _nonclinical_fold_enabled():
566	        # Fold ONLY the non-clinical subject to a surface-invariant signature.
567	        # An empty fold (pure-punctuation subject) becomes the UNKNOWN sentinel.
568	        subject = _fold_nonclinical_subject(subject) or _UNKNOWN_SUBJECT
569	    if not subject or subject == _UNKNOWN_SUBJECT:
570	        return ("__unknown__", evidence_id, claim_index)
571	    raw_value = float(getattr(claim, "value", 0.0) or 0.0)
572	    value_slot = raw_value if exact_value else round(raw_value, 3)
573	    return (
574	        subject,
575	        getattr(claim, "predicate", "") or "",
576	        value_slot,
577	        getattr(claim, "unit", "") or "",
578	        getattr(claim, "dose", "") or "",
579	        getattr(claim, "arm", "") or "",
580	        getattr(claim, "endpoint_phrase", "") or "",
581	    )
582	
583	
584	@dataclass
585	class SameWorkGroup:
586	    """One same-work unit: N rows that are the SAME work (same DOI / folded
587	    title) appearing at >=1 URL. KEEPS ALL the URLs as corroborating locators
588	    (§-1.3 keep-all) but COUNTS as ONE source."""
589	
590	    same_work_id: str                   # the SHARED same-work key
591	    canonical_index: int                # the representative row index for the work
592	    member_indices: list[int]           # all surviving row indices for this work
593	    member_evidence_ids: list[str]      # all member evidence_ids (corroborators)
594	    member_urls: list[str]              # all member source_urls (kept locators)
595	
596	
597	@dataclass
598	class SameWorkResult:
599	    """Result of `consolidate_same_work`."""

612	def _row_rank_key(row: dict[str, Any], index: int) -> tuple:
613	    """Canonical-representative rank for a same-work group: highest authority,
614	    then relevance, then LONGEST body (the most complete copy), then lowest
615	    original index for determinism."""
616	    return (
617	        float(row.get("authority_score", 0.0) or 0.0),
618	        float(row.get("selection_relevance", 0.0) or 0.0),
619	        len(_row_text(row)),
620	        -index,
621	    )
622	
623	
624	def consolidate_same_work(rows: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> SameWorkResult:
625	    """Group same-work rows; drop non-functional members. PURE (no net/LLM).
626	
627	    Two passes, both faithfulness-neutral (§-1.3 — group/count only, never relax
628	    a verify gate, never merge two genuinely different works):
629	
630	    1. DROP CAPTCHA / anti-bot security stubs UNCONDITIONALLY (a stub carries no
631	       real claim; ``_is_captcha_stub`` — content literal, no same-work sibling
632	       required).
633	    2. GROUP the surviving rows by ``_same_work_key`` (DOI first, else folded
634	       title). Within each work pick a canonical row (highest authority /
635	       relevance / longest body), then DROP any member whose body is a strict
636	       PREFIX of a LONGER member of the SAME work (a truncated-intro duplicate).
637	       A row with NO same-work key is its own singleton work (never merged).
638	
639	    Returns a SameWorkResult mapping each ORIGINAL row index to its same-work id
640	    + canonical index, the per-work groups (all member evidence_ids + URLs kept
641	    as corroborators), and the dropped (CAPTCHA + prefix-dupe) original indices.
642	    """
643	    dropped_captcha: set[int] = set()
644	    work_members: dict[str, list[int]] = {}
645	    for ri, row in enumerate(rows):
646	        if _is_captcha_stub(row):
647	            dropped_captcha.add(ri)
648	            continue
649	        key = _same_work_key(row)
650	        if not key:
651	            # No same-work key: a per-row singleton key so it can never collide.
652	            key = "__singleton__:%d" % ri
653	        work_members.setdefault(key, []).append(ri)
654	
655	    dropped_prefix: set[int] = set()
656	    groups: list[SameWorkGroup] = []
657	    work_id_by_index: dict[int, str] = {}
658	    canonical_index_by_index: dict[int, int] = {}
659	
660	    for key, member_ris in work_members.items():
661	        # Strict-prefix drop WITHIN this work: a member whose stripped body is a
662	        # strict prefix of a strictly-longer sibling's body is a truncated dup.
663	        texts = {ri: _row_text(rows[ri]).strip() for ri in member_ris}
664	        prefix_dup: set[int] = set()
665	        for a in member_ris:
666	            ta = texts[a]
667	            if not ta:
668	                continue
669	            for b in member_ris:
670	                if a is b:
671	                    continue
672	                tb = texts[b]
673	                # a is a strict prefix of the LONGER b -> a is a truncated dup.
674	                if len(tb) > len(ta) and tb.startswith(ta):
675	                    prefix_dup.add(a)
676	                    break
677	        dropped_prefix |= prefix_dup
678	        survivors = [ri for ri in member_ris if ri not in prefix_dup]
679	        if not survivors:
680	            # Degenerate (all equal-length mutual prefixes): keep the lowest idx.
681	            survivors = [min(member_ris)]
682	            dropped_prefix -= {survivors[0]}
683	
684	        canonical = max(survivors, key=lambda ri: _row_rank_key(rows[ri], ri))
685	        # Only emit a real same-work id for genuine same-work keys (a URL / DOI /
686	        # title group). The per-row "__singleton__" keys carry no cross-row meaning,
687	        # so they get no same_work annotation (a row with no URL/DOI/title is its own
688	        # work and must not look "consolidated"). STEP 4 (#1374): a ``url:`` key IS a
689	        # genuine same-work group (chunks of one fetched document) — it MUST populate
690	        # ``work_id_by_index`` / ``canonical_index_by_index`` so the origin-host fold
691	        # and the render annotations treat the shared-URL chunks as ONE source.
692	        is_real_work = (
693	            key.startswith("url:")
694	            or key.startswith("doi:")
695	            or key.startswith("title:")
696	        )
697	        member_evidence_ids = [
698	            str(rows[ri].get("evidence_id", ri)) for ri in survivors
699	        ]
700	        member_urls = sorted({
701	            str(rows[ri].get("source_url", "") or "") for ri in survivors
702	        } - {""})
703	        groups.append(SameWorkGroup(
704	            same_work_id=key,
705	            canonical_index=canonical,
706	            member_indices=sorted(survivors),
707	            member_evidence_ids=member_evidence_ids,
708	            member_urls=member_urls,
709	        ))
710	        if is_real_work:
711	            for ri in survivors:
712	                work_id_by_index[ri] = key
713	                canonical_index_by_index[ri] = canonical
714	
715	    return SameWorkResult(
716	        groups=groups,
717	        work_id_by_index=work_id_by_index,
718	        canonical_index_by_index=canonical_index_by_index,
719	        dropped_indices=dropped_captcha | dropped_prefix,
720	        dropped_captcha_indices=dropped_captcha,
721	        dropped_prefix_indices=dropped_prefix,
722	    )
723

779	def _claim_sentence(row: dict[str, Any], bucket_value: Any) -> str:
780	    """The focused CLAIM SENTENCE for NLI — the full sentence containing the cluster's
781	    numeric value (expanded from ``ExtractedNumericClaim.context_snippet``), NOT the full
782	    ``direct_quote`` body. Feeding the whole document (often title + abstract + URL
783	    boilerplate, thousands of chars > the cross-encoder's ~512-token limit) makes two
784	    unrelated papers weakly "entail" on shared boilerplate — a §-1.1 false-merge. The
785	    focused claim sentence is what the bake-off scored (P=1.0); it makes genuinely
786	    different claims (e.g. dexamethasone-preterm vs protein-older-men) non-entailing.
787	
788	    Picks the claim whose value matches ``bucket_value`` (the cluster's value); falls
789	    back to the first claim, then to the row body if no claim extracts."""
790	    claims = extract_numeric_claims([row])
791	    body = _row_text(row)
792	    if claims:
793	        chosen = None
794	        if bucket_value is not None:
795	            for c in claims:
796	                if round(float(getattr(c, "value", 0.0) or 0.0), 6) == bucket_value:
797	                    chosen = c
798	                    break
799	        if chosen is None:
800	            chosen = claims[0]
801	        snip = getattr(chosen, "context_snippet", "") or ""
802	        if snip:
803	            # Use the focused ~200-char value-window directly. (Expanding to the full
804	            # surrounding sentence was tried and REGRESSED precision on web-fetch corpora
805	            # whose bodies are "Title: ... URL Source: ..." boilerplate dumps — the
806	            # expansion re-introduced boilerplate the snippet had excluded; see the
807	            # I-wire-001 audit. The focused window is the cleaner claim representation.)
808	            return snip
809	    return body
810	
811	
812	def _cluster_text(
813	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]], member_ris: list[int], rank_fn, bucket_value: Any,
814	) -> str:
815	    """The representative CLAIM SENTENCE fed to the NLI cross-encoder for one literal
816	    cluster: the focused ``context_snippet`` of the cluster's best-ranked row (the same
817	    authority/relevance ranking the corroboration step uses). Deterministic."""
818	    rep_ri = max(member_ris, key=rank_fn)
819	    return _claim_sentence(rows[rep_ri], bucket_value)
820	
821	
822	def _cluster_value_bucket(key: tuple, rows: list[dict[str, Any]], member_ris: list[int]) -> Any:
823	    """The numeric VALUE a literal cluster asserts — used to BUCKET clusters before NLI so
824	    only same-VALUE clusters are pairwise-compared. Two sources can corroborate the SAME
825	    claim only if they carry the SAME number, so bucketing by value is both a scale fix
826	    (O(n^2) -> O(sum bucket^2)) and a precision guard (never NLI-compare 30% vs 12%).
827	
828	    Known-subject keys carry the value at index 2. The ``__unknown__`` sentinel key does
829	    not, so the value is recovered from the representative row's extracted claim; a cluster
830	    with no recoverable numeric value buckets under ``None`` (its own no-merge bucket)."""
831	    if isinstance(key, tuple) and key and key[0] != "__unknown__" and len(key) >= 3:
832	        return round(float(key[2]), 6)
833	    for ri in member_ris:
834	        claims = extract_numeric_claims([rows[ri]])
835	        if claims:
836	            return round(float(getattr(claims[0], "value", 0.0) or 0.0), 6)
837	    return None
838	
839	
840	def _apply_consolidation_nli(
841	    groups: dict[tuple, list[int]],
842	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
843	    rank_fn,
844	) -> tuple[dict[tuple, list[int]], int]:
845	    """Merge literal ``_finding_key`` clusters whose representatives BIDIRECTIONALLY
846	    entail (the bake-off winner — same-claim paraphrases the exact subject/predicate/value
847	    floor left separate, board R=0.0). Returns ``(merged_groups, nli_merge_count)`` where
848	    ``nli_merge_count`` = number of literal clusters absorbed into another.
849	
850	    VALUE-BUCKETING (scale + precision): clusters are first bucketed by the numeric value
851	    they assert (``_cluster_value_bucket``); NLI runs only WITHIN a bucket. Same-claim
852	    corroborators must share the number, so this never misses a real merge, bounds the
853	    pairwise cost to per-bucket O(k^2), and can never NLI-pair two different numbers.
854	
855	    UNKNOWN-SUBJECT clusters ARE eligible (the clinical extractor dumps many same-claim
856	    paraphrases into per-row ``__unknown__`` sentinels — exactly the R=0.0 floor the winner
857	    fixes). Merging them is SAFE: corroboration_count / member_hosts are a Signal-D WEIGHT
858	    consumed only as grouping by the downstream consumer (``credibility_pass`` relabel +
859	    edge-remap), never a verify gate — the isolated per-member entailment verify is
860	    UNCHANGED, so no member newly passes verification (faithfulness FROZEN, §-1.3). A merge
861	    can only inflate a weight count, never drop a row, never relax a gate.
862	
863	    Determinism + order-independence: each bucket runs a bounded-parallel pairwise NLI
864	    (cap = ``PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_WORKERS``) then a deterministic union-find post-step
865	    (attach-to-lowest-index), so the merged grouping is identical for any worker count.
866	    The merged member-index lists are sorted, so the downstream loop is unchanged.
867	
868	    Any failure (e.g. the cross-encoder cannot load) RAISES — a flag-ON winner that
869	    silently no-ops would defeat the §-1.4 canary (no silent fallback, LAW II)."""
870	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.consolidation_nli import group_clusters  # noqa: PLC0415
871	
872	    keys = list(groups.keys())
873	    if len(keys) < 2:
874	        return groups, 0
875	
876	    # Bucket every cluster index by the numeric value it asserts. Only buckets with >=2
877	    # clusters carry NLI-merge candidates; the rest pass through unchanged.
878	    bucket_of: dict[int, Any] = {
879	        i: _cluster_value_bucket(keys[i], rows, groups[keys[i]]) for i in range(len(keys))
880	    }
881	    by_value: dict[Any, list[int]] = {}
882	    for i in range(len(keys)):
883	        v = bucket_of[i]
884	        if v is None:
885	            continue  # no recoverable value => its own singleton, never merged
886	        by_value.setdefault(v, []).append(i)
887	
888	    # Union-find over ALL cluster indices; only within-bucket NLI edges union.
889	    parent = list(range(len(keys)))
890	
891	    def _find(x: int) -> int:
892	        while parent[x] != x:
893	            parent[x] = parent[parent[x]]
894	            x = parent[x]
895	        return x
896	
897	    def _union(a: int, b: int) -> None:
898	        ra, rb = _find(a), _find(b)
899	        if ra == rb:
900	            return
901	        lo, hi = (ra, rb) if ra < rb else (rb, ra)
902	        parent[hi] = lo  # attach to lower => deterministic
903	
904	    for value, cluster_idxs in sorted(by_value.items(), key=lambda kv: str(kv[0])):
905	        if len(cluster_idxs) < 2:
906	            continue
907	        texts = [_cluster_text(rows, groups[keys[i]], rank_fn, value) for i in cluster_idxs]
908	        root_by_pos = group_clusters(texts)  # bounded-parallel NLI + union-find post-step
909	        for pos, eli in enumerate(cluster_idxs):
910	            _union(eli, cluster_idxs[root_by_pos[pos]])
911	
912	    # Re-emit clusters: every union-find root keeps the LOWEST-index member's key as the
913	    # merged cluster key; folded clusters disappear (their members move to the root).
914	    merged_members: dict[int, list[int]] = {}
915	    for i in range(len(keys)):
916	        merged_members.setdefault(_find(i), []).extend(groups[keys[i]])
917	
918	    new_groups: dict[tuple, list[int]] = {}
919	    absorbed = 0
920	    for i in range(len(keys)):  # original key order => order-stable result dict
921	        root = _find(i)
922	        if root != i:
923	            continue  # this cluster was folded into its root; emitted there
924	        new_groups[keys[i]] = sorted(set(merged_members[root]))
925	    absorbed = len(keys) - len(new_groups)  # clusters absorbed = before - after
926	    return new_groups, absorbed
927	
928	
929	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
930	# Qualitative-claim basket formation — I-deepfix-001 D1 (#1344)
931	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
932	#
933	# §-1.3 Principle 2 (CONSOLIDATE qualitative claims TOO, never numeric-only):
934	# the numeric ``_finding_key`` path above keys every corroboration basket on an
935	# EXTRACTED NUMERIC value slot, so a QUALITATIVE (non-numeric) claim that several
936	# INDEPENDENT sources assert can never form a multi-source basket — it survives
937	# as a SAFE singleton (never dropped) but earns NO corroboration weight. That is
938	# the D1 diced-dice blind spot (``dice_d1_consolidation_qualitative_basket``):
939	# baskets keyed numeric-only. This pass groups the NO-numeric-finding rows that
940	# assert the SAME qualitative claim into ONE multi-citation basket carrying ALL
941	# members, keyed on a NON-NUMERIC normalized subject/predicate signature, so the
942	# corroboration (count + distinct hosts) is surfaced as a WEIGHT.
943	#
944	# CONSERVATIVE (false-merge is worse than no-merge, §-1.3): two rows cluster ONLY
945	# when their content-word shingle sets clear a HIGH Jaccard threshold AND their
946	# polarity signatures match (an antonym / negation flip blocks the merge even at
947	# Jaccard ~1.0). A genuinely-unique claim stays a singleton (never emitted as a
948	# basket). Plain greedy single-pass clustering — deterministic + order-stable.
949	#
950	# FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL / KEEP-ALL: this only ADDS corroboration baskets +
951	# ``corroboration_count`` WEIGHT. It DROPS NO ROW (every member still flows
952	# through ``deduped_rows`` under keep-all), and touches NO verify gate
953	# (strict_verify / the NLI entailment verifier / 4-role D8 / provenance /
954	# span-grounding are untouched). Even an over-merge can only inflate a weight
955	# count — it can never relax faithfulness and never lose a source.
956	#
957	# The shingle / polarity / Jaccard predicates are REUSED from the proven
958	# fact_dedup prose path (the polarity guard is the Codex #1289 P1 antonym-flip
959	# defense), imported LAZILY at function scope — the same defer-to-dodge-cycles
960	# discipline this module already uses for credibility_pass / consolidation_nli.
961	_QUAL_BASKET_ENV = "PG_FINDING_DEDUP_QUALITATIVE"
962	_QUAL_JACCARD_ENV = "PG_FINDING_DEDUP_QUALITATIVE_JACCARD"
963	_QUAL_JACCARD_DEFAULT = "0.82"
964	# Cap the readable signature-token word count (deterministic, bounded key string).
965	_QUAL_KEY_MAX_WORDS = 16
966	# I-deepfix-001 P4 recall rung-1 (#1344): the qualitative-NLI union SUB-flag. The lexical
967	# greedy pass (_build_qualitative_groups) is the cheap near-verbatim CANDIDATE stage; when
968	# this sub-flag AND the master PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI gate are BOTH ON, a SECOND semantic-recall
969	# pass unions candidate clusters whose representatives BIDIRECTIONALLY entail (the SAME strict
970	# NLI the numeric path uses). OFF (either flag) => byte-identical lexical-only behavior.
971	_QUAL_NLI_ENV = "PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_QUALITATIVE"
972	
973	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
974	# Coverage-fix keystone — I-deepfix-001 Wave 1b (#1344), REAL_PLAN_2026 coverage_fix item 1
975	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
976	# The plan-canonical qualitative same-claim grouping flag: `PG_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI`. When ON, a
977	# THIRD (directional) semantic-recall pass unions the lexical qualitative candidate clusters
978	# whose representatives STRICTLY BIDIRECTIONALLY entail into ONE corroboration basket, using the
979	# 3-state directional primitive `consolidation_nli.entails_directional` (True / False / None):
980	#   * bidirectional entails (BOTH directions True)  => MERGE (keep-all, one basket);
981	#   * one-direction-only (exactly one True)         => an EXTENSION relation, do NOT merge;
982	#   * contradiction (neither direction entails)     => a durable relation, do NOT merge;
983	#   * infra `None` on EITHER direction (empty text / cross-encoder unavailable) => NO merge, a
984	#     FAIL-CLOSED singleton, and the run CONTINUES (never raises, never drops a row).
985	# This is the fail-closed-CONTINUE keystone the `score_pairs`-based `_apply_qualitative_nli_union`
986	# (PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_QUALITATIVE) does not provide (that path RAISES on a non-OOM model
987	# failure). Both flags are additive / merge-only / keep-all, BUT they are NOT independently safe
988	# together: when BOTH are ON the legacy union runs FIRST and would RAISE on a non-OOM model fault
989	# BEFORE the keystone's fail-closed grouping runs, aborting the run at the dedup step (the exact
990	# Wave-3 slate config). So the wiring in `_build_qualitative_groups` GUARDS the legacy union
991	# under the keystone regime ONLY: it degrades a legacy raise to a §-1.3-safe under-merge (logged
992	# loud, never except:pass) and lets the keystone's own None path yield singletons if the model is
993	# truly dead. When the keystone is OFF the legacy union is UNGUARDED => byte-identical legacy
994	# behavior. §-1.3 CONSOLIDATE-keep-all, WEIGHT-ONLY: no row dropped, no verify gate touched. The
995	# extension / contradiction relations are surfaced downstream in Wave 2 (cross_source_synthesis);
996	# this build's contribution is the MERGE decision plus leaving non-bidirectional pairs un-merged.
997	# DEFAULT-OFF => byte-identical. Slate-ON per the plan (Wave-3 activation).
998	_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_ENV = "PG_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI"
999	# LAW VI knobs (no hardcoded values): bounded scoring concurrency, an O(n^2) pair-count cap, and a
1000	# total wall-clock deadline (a CPU-degraded cross-encoder must not run-pin the box across up to
1001	# 2*MAX_PAIRS single-item forwards — mirrors the consolidation W04 wall).
1002	_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WORKERS_ENV = "PG_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WORKERS"
1003	_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WORKERS_DEFAULT = "8"
1004	_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_MAX_PAIRS_ENV = "PG_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_MAX_PAIRS"
1005	_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_MAX_PAIRS_DEFAULT = "20000"
1006	_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WALL_SECONDS_ENV = "PG_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WALL_SECONDS"
1007	_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WALL_SECONDS_DEFAULT = "180"
1008	
1009	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1010	# 3a — WIDENED qualitative CANDIDATE NOMINATION (F3, I-deepfix-001 #1369)
1011	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1012	# ROOT CAUSE (Fable F3-3a, forensic on the drb_72 real run): the qualitative
1013	# candidate NOMINATION is lexical NEAR-VERBATIM only. `_build_qualitative_groups`
1014	# greedy-clusters rows by content-word shingle Jaccard >= 0.82, and the keystone
1015	# `_apply_finding_dedup_nli_grouping` then NLI-confirms only cluster REPRESENTATIVES
1016	# among the survivors. So a cross-document PARAPHRASE whose surface wording differs
1017	# enough that its shingle-Jaccard falls below 0.82 stays its OWN singleton cluster —
1018	# and when the corpus is large the O(n^2) rep-pair count trips the MAX_PAIRS cap and
1019	# the whole directional grouping SKIPS (an under-merge), so the paraphrase never even
1020	# becomes a CANDIDATE for the NLI union. The multi-source basket collapses.
1021	#
1022	# THE FIX (widen the NOMINATION only, NLI stays the sole MERGE decision — §-1.3 +
1023	# the F3 anti-fabrication law): NOMINATE additional cross-cluster candidate PAIRS by
1024	# TOKEN-CONTAINMENT (the smaller cluster's content-token set is largely CONTAINED in
1025	# the other's — an ASYMMETRIC overlap that catches paraphrase/expansion pairs the
1026	# symmetric Jaccard 0.82 near-verbatim gate misses), then CONFIRM each nominated pair
1027	# through the SAME strict bidirectional-entailment NLI gate the keystone uses
1028	# (`consolidation_nli.entails_directional`, BOTH directions True) plus the polarity
1029	# hard-block. A pair MERGES iff the NLI confirms it — token-containment is ONLY a
1030	# recall-oriented candidate BLOCKER, never a merge. This is strictly MORE recall than
1031	# rep-only near-verbatim, and it is BOUNDED (only containment-passing pairs are ever
1032	# NLI-scored), so it also RECALLS in the over-cap regime where the all-pairs keystone

1052	def _qualitative_enabled() -> bool:

1054	    qualitative-basket pass is the §-1.3 CONSOLIDATE-qualitative-too path. Set to

1056	    baskets formed). It is ADDITIONALLY gated on the consolidate-keep-all regime
1057	    (``credibility_redesign_enabled``) by the caller, so a legacy (drop) run never
1058	    sees a qualitative basket."""

1060	        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",

1067	    gate (single source of truth in ``consolidation_nli.consolidation_nli_enabled``) AND

1094	def _finding_dedup_nli_workers() -> int:
1095	    """Bounded scoring concurrency for the directional qualitative grouping (LAW VI). A
1096	    malformed / out-of-range value falls back to the default (fail-safe, never an unbounded
1097	    pool). Clamped to [1, 64] to mirror ``consolidation_nli._workers``."""
1098	    raw = os.environ.get(_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WORKERS_ENV, "").strip() or _FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WORKERS_DEFAULT
1099	    try:
1100	        value = int(raw)
1101	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1102	        logger.warning(
1103	            "[finding_dedup] %s=%r not an int; using default %s",
1104	            _FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WORKERS_ENV, raw, _FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WORKERS_DEFAULT,
1105	        )
1106	        return int(_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WORKERS_DEFAULT)
1107	    return max(1, min(64, value))

1110	def _finding_dedup_nli_max_pairs() -> int:
1111	    """The O(n^2) candidate-pair cap for the directional qualitative grouping (LAW VI). Over
1112	    the cap the pass SKIPS scoring and leaves the clusters UNMERGED (an under-merge, keep-all,
1113	    §-1.3 safe — never drops a corroborator). A malformed value falls back to the default."""
1114	    raw = os.environ.get(_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_MAX_PAIRS_ENV, "").strip() or _FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_MAX_PAIRS_DEFAULT
1115	    try:
1116	        value = int(raw)
1117	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1118	        logger.warning(
1119	            "[finding_dedup] %s=%r not an int; using default %s",
1120	            _FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_MAX_PAIRS_ENV, raw, _FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_MAX_PAIRS_DEFAULT,
1121	        )
1122	        return int(_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_MAX_PAIRS_DEFAULT)
1123	    return max(1, value)

1126	def _finding_dedup_nli_wall_seconds() -> float:
1127	    """The TOTAL wall-clock deadline (seconds) for the directional qualitative scoring loop
1128	    (LAW VI; mirrors ``consolidation_nli._wall_seconds``). A CPU-degraded cross-encoder would
1129	    otherwise run-pin the box across up to ``2*MAX_PAIRS`` single-item forwards. On the deadline
1130	    the loop STOPS scoring further pairs and keeps the edges gathered so far — an UNDER-merge only
1131	    (§-1.3-safe: keeps MORE/equal baskets, drops no corroborator). A malformed / non-finite / ``<=0``
1132	    value disables the wall (unbounded — the escape hatch). Default 180s."""
1133	    raw = os.environ.get(_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WALL_SECONDS_ENV, "").strip() or _FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WALL_SECONDS_DEFAULT
1134	    try:
1135	        value = float(raw)
1136	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1137	        logger.warning(
1138	            "[finding_dedup] %s=%r not a float; using default %s",
1139	            _FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WALL_SECONDS_ENV, raw, _FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WALL_SECONDS_DEFAULT,
1140	        )
1141	        return float(_FINDING_DEDUP_NLI_WALL_SECONDS_DEFAULT)
1142	    if not math.isfinite(value) or value <= 0:
1143	        return 0.0
1144	    return value

1147	def _qual_jaccard_threshold() -> float:
1148	    """Read ``PG_FINDING_DEDUP_QUALITATIVE_JACCARD`` as a float in (0, 1].
1149	    Malformed / out-of-range => default 0.82 (logged once at WARNING, never
1150	    raised — a typo must not crash a paid run). 0.82 is the proven conservative
1151	    prose-merge threshold (only near-identical restatements cluster)."""
1152	    raw = os.environ.get(_QUAL_JACCARD_ENV, "").strip() or _QUAL_JACCARD_DEFAULT
1153	    try:
1154	        value = float(raw)
1155	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1156	        logger.warning(
1157	            "[finding_dedup] %s=%r is not a float; using default %s",
1158	            _QUAL_JACCARD_ENV, raw, _QUAL_JACCARD_DEFAULT,
1159	        )
1160	        return float(_QUAL_JACCARD_DEFAULT)
1161	    if not (0.0 < value <= 1.0):
1162	        logger.warning(
1163	            "[finding_dedup] %s=%s out of (0,1]; using default %s",
1164	            _QUAL_JACCARD_ENV, value, _QUAL_JACCARD_DEFAULT,
1165	        )
1166	        return float(_QUAL_JACCARD_DEFAULT)
1167	    return value

1170	def _qual_key_token(text: str) -> str:
1171	    """A deterministic, NON-NUMERIC, human-auditable signature token for a
1172	    qualitative basket: lowercased -> citation-tokens stripped -> alnum
1173	    word-tokenized -> stopwords dropped -> SORTED+deduped -> capped to
1174	    ``_QUAL_KEY_MAX_WORDS`` -> space-joined. The whole token is a single STRING
1175	    element of the finding_key tuple, so a content word that happens to be a bare
1176	    number (e.g. ``2024``) never makes the key NUMERIC — it stays a string.
1177	    Reuses fact_dedup's citation/stopword/word predicates so the normalization is
1178	    byte-consistent with the prose path."""
1179	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.fact_dedup import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1180	        _CITATION_TOKEN_RE,
1181	        _STOPWORDS,
1182	        _WORD_RE,
1183	    )
1184	
1185	    low = _CITATION_TOKEN_RE.sub(" ", (text or "").lower())
1186	    words = [w for w in _WORD_RE.findall(low) if w not in _STOPWORDS]
1187	    return " ".join(sorted(set(words))[:_QUAL_KEY_MAX_WORDS])
1188

1190	def _apply_qualitative_nli_union(
1191	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
1192	    clusters: list[list[Any]],
1193	    *,
1194	    predict_fn=None,
1195	) -> list[list[Any]]:
1196	    """SECOND semantic-recall pass (I-deepfix-001 P4 recall rung-1, #1344): UNION lexical
1197	    candidate clusters whose REPRESENTATIVE claim texts BIDIRECTIONALLY entail, reusing the
1198	    SAME strict bidirectional-NLI machinery the NUMERIC path uses
1199	    (``consolidation_nli.score_pairs``). The lexical greedy pass above is the cheap
1200	    near-verbatim CANDIDATE stage (shingle-Jaccard 0.82); this is the NLI CONFIRM stage that
1201	    RECALLS the same-claim paraphrases lexical Jaccard leaves as singletons — exactly the
1202	    qualitative-corroboration blind spot most DRB-II rubric facts fall into (a non-numeric
1203	    claim two independent sources assert in NON-overlapping wording, e.g. a Brynjolfsson-family
1204	    and an OECD/WEF-family source both stating 'AI adoption is concentrated among large firms').
1205	
1206	    ``clusters`` is the greedy list of ``[rep_shingles, rep_polarity, [member_ris]]`` triples
1207	    (INCLUDING lexical singletons — a lexical singleton is exactly a claim in unique wording that
1208	    the NLI can still recall onto a paraphrase). Returns the SAME triple shape with the merged
1209	    member lists; the caller then emits only clusters with >= 2 members.
1210	
1211	    REQUIRED HARD OVER-MERGE BLOCKERS (§-1.1 clinical-lethal if a false 'corroborated' renders;
1212	    NONE optional — an NLI union raises verified_support_origin_count which P3 renders as a
1213	    per-item 'corroborated' label, so a wrong union is a misstated-corroboration statement):
1214	      (i)  bidirectional entailment stays STRICT — ``score_pairs`` emits an edge ONLY when
1215	           A entails B AND B entails A (entailment the argmax in BOTH directions, no relaxed
1216	           threshold). This structurally blocks three of the four over-merge canaries:
1217	           HEDGED-vs-FLAT ('reduces' entails 'may reduce' but 'may reduce' does NOT entail
1218	           'reduces' => one-directional => no union — merging them is itself a certainty
1219	           distortion, From-May-to-Is 2606.07951), and SCOPE (manufacturing-vs-services) /
1220	           CAUSAL-DIRECTION (A->B vs B->A) / TEMPORALITY (2020 vs 2026), where NEITHER
1221	           direction entails.
1222	      (ii) the ``_polarity_signature`` antonym/negation guard HARD-BLOCKS any opposite-polarity
1223	           union even if the cross-encoder scored the pair entailing — an 'increased' vs
1224	           'decreased' antonym can never corroborate (defense-in-depth: a model-independent
1225	           deterministic block, not left to the NLI verdict alone).
1226	      (iii) DIRECT-EDGE grouping, NOT transitive union-find (I-deepfix-001 P4 Codex fix, #1344):
1227	           a redundant cluster joins a PRIMARY cluster ONLY when it DIRECTLY bidirectionally-
1228	           entails THAT primary. Transitive union-find over NLI edges over-merges — A::B and
1229	           B::C bidirectional edges would fold A/B/C into ONE basket even when A and C do NOT
1230	           directly entail, inflating a basket head's corroboration_count with a claim that
1231	           verifies only against a sibling span (the false-'corroborated' render chain §-1.1
1232	           calls clinical-lethal). This mirrors the VALIDATED-SAFE direct-to-primary pattern the
1233	           prose path already uses (``fact_dedup.py`` FIX-D, #1335), which replaced the same
1234	           unsafe transitive merge. The numeric sibling path bounds this with value-bucketing;
1235	           this is the qualitative path's equivalent precision guard.
1236	
1237	    (iv) ALL-MEMBER SCORING (I-deepfix-001 C2, #1344 — "score ALL in-section member pairs,
1238	         not just representatives"): the greedy candidate stage groups near-verbatim members
1239	         into clusters, but two clusters that carry the SAME claim can have REPRESENTATIVES
1240	         whose surface wording does not entail while a NON-representative member of one DOES
1241	         entail a member of the other (a large paraphrase cluster only PARTIALLY unions on the
1242	         rep alone). C2 links two clusters when ANY cross-cluster member pair bidirectionally
1243	         entails (with matching per-member polarity), so a large paraphrase cluster FULLY
1244	         unions. The representative-level edges are ALWAYS included as a floor, so this can
1245	         NEVER union LESS than the pre-C2 rep-only behavior (monotone recall). All-member
1246	         scoring is bounded by ``score_pairs``'s ``PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_MAX_PAIRS`` cap: over
1247	         the cap it returns NO edges (safe UNDER-merge) and the rep-edge floor still applies,
1248	         so a huge corpus degrades to rep-only, never regresses, never over-merges.
1249	
1250	    KEEP-ALL / WEIGHT-ONLY (§-1.3): ONLY member-index lists are unioned (corroboration_count /
1251	    independent_hosts rise); NO row is dropped, NO verify gate (strict_verify / the NLI
1252	    entailment verifier / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding) is touched. Deterministic +
1253	    order-independent: ``score_pairs`` sorts its edges and the keep-first grouping attaches every
1254	    redundant to the LOWEST-INDEX primary it DIRECTLY entails, so the merged grouping is identical
1255	    for any worker count. ``predict_fn`` is the deterministic test-injection seam; production
1256	    passes None => the real lazy cross-encoder.
1257	    """
1258	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.consolidation_nli import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1259	        score_pairs,
1260	    )
1261	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.fact_dedup import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1262	        _polarity_signature,
1263	    )

1272	    #   (2) ALL-MEMBER (C2) — every member of every cluster, bounded by MAX_PAIRS.
1273	    cluster_links: set[tuple[int, int]] = set()

1289	    member_texts: list[str] = []

1298	    for a, b in score_pairs(member_texts, predict_fn=predict_fn):
1299	        ca, cb = member_owner[a], member_owner[b]
1300	        if ca == cb:
